title: Garides Saganaki
instructions: Boil the prawns in a pot of water for about 5 minutes, then drain, reserving the liquid, and set them aside to cool.
Warm 2 tablespoons of the olive oil in a saucepan and cook the onion until soft.
Stir in the parsley, wine, tomatoes, garlic and the remaining olive oil, then simmer for about 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens.
While the sauce simmers, let the prawns cool enough to handle, then pinch off the legs and peel away the shells, leaving the head and tail intact.
Once the sauce has thickened, stir in the prawns, bring back to a gentle simmer if needed, and cook for about 5 minutes.
Add the feta, remove from the heat, and let it stand until the cheese starts to melt.
Serve warm with slices of crusty bread.
For an untraditional twist, stir in a few tablespoons of stock or passata, toss with cooked pasta after adding the feta, and serve.

ingredients.note: prawns are boiled first per instructions, then added already-cooked to the sauce
3 tbsp ≈ 41 g; all oil ends up in the sauce that's served, so counted as remaining in dish
weight inferred from 'a chopped onion' ≈ average medium onion (~110 g); not weighed in original
'pinch' inferred as ~1 g; imprecise quantity in original

title: Chicken Enchilada Casserole
instructions: Cut each chicken breast into about three pieces so it cooks faster, then place it in a small pot.

Pour the enchilada sauce over the chicken and cook covered on low-medium heat until the chicken is cooked through, about 20 minutes; no water is needed, the sauce does the cooking, and stir occasionally so it doesn't stick.
Remove the chicken from the pot and shred it with two forks.
Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
Spread about ¼ cup of the leftover enchilada sauce over the bottom of a baking dish.
Lay two corn tortillas over the sauce, then top with a third of the shredded chicken, a third of the remaining sauce, and a third of the cheese.
Repeat the layering two more times with tortillas, chicken, sauce, and cheese, finishing with the last of the ingredients on top.
Bake uncovered for 20 to 30 minutes, until bubbly and the cheese has melted and started to brown.
Serve warm.

ingredients.note: 14 oz jar converted to grams (1 oz = 28.35 g)
3 cups shredded cheese estimated at ~113 g/cup
6 tortillas estimated at ~30 g each
2 breasts estimated at ~170 g each raw weight

title: Bakewell tart
instructions: Tip the flour into a bowl and rub in the chilled butter with your fingertips until it looks like fine breadcrumbs.
Mix in the cold water a little at a time until a soft dough forms.
Roll the dough out on a floured surface and line a 20cm/8in flan tin with it.
Chill the lined tin in the fridge for 30 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 200C/400F/Gas 6 (180C fan).
Line the pastry case with foil, fill with baking beans and bake blind for about 15 minutes.
Remove the beans and foil, then bake for a further 5 minutes to dry out the base.
Spread the raspberry jam generously over the base of the flan.
Melt the butter in a pan, take it off the heat and stir in the caster sugar.
Stir in the ground almonds, beaten egg and almond extract.
Pour the almond filling into the flan tin and scatter the flaked almonds over the top.
Bake for about 35 minutes, covering loosely with foil if the almonds start browning too fast.

ingredients.note: '2-3 tbsp' — took midpoint (2.5 tbsp ≈ 37.5g)
1 tbsp jam estimated at 20g
1 egg estimated at standard 50g
1/2 tsp estimated at 2.5g

title: Apple Frangipan Tart
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200C/180C Fan/Gas 6.
Bash the digestive biscuits in a sealed freezer bag with a rolling pin until they're fine crumbs.
Melt the butter in a small pan, then stir in the biscuit crumbs until fully coated.
Tip the buttery crumbs into the tart tin and press evenly over the base and sides with the back of a spoon.
Chill the crumb base in the fridge while you make the filling.
Cream the salted butter and caster sugar together until light and fluffy, using a food processor for 2-3 minutes if you have one.
Mix in the beaten eggs, then add the ground almonds and almond extract, blending until well combined.
Peel the apples and slice them thinly at the last minute so they don't brown.
Arrange the apple slices over the chilled biscuit base.
Spread the frangipane filling evenly over the apples, level the surface, and sprinkle with flaked almonds.
Bake for 20-25 minutes until golden-brown and set.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 15 minutes, then release the tin sides, standing the tin on a can of beans and pushing down gently on the edges to help.
Transfer the tart, still on its tin base, to a serving plate and serve warm with cream, crème fraiche, or ice cream.

ingredients.note: melted and mixed into the crumb base, remains in the served dish
grams inferred from '2 free-range eggs' at ~50g each; no metric weight given
grams inferred from '1 tsp' (~5g); no metric weight given

title: Kapsalon
instructions: Slice the doner meat into strips.
Heat oil in a pan and fry the meat strips for about 6 minutes until cooked through.
Deep-fry the fries until golden brown, then spread them evenly across the base of a baking dish.
Layer the fried meat evenly over the fries.
Scatter the cheese over the meat, whole slices or grated, then bake for a few minutes until the cheese melts.
Chop the lettuce, tomato and cucumber into small pieces and toss together for the salad, adding olives, jalapeños or red onion if you like.
Spread the salad over the finished dish and serve with garlic sauce and hot sauce.

ingredients.note: Listed only as 'Topping' with no quantity; 30 g inferred as a typical drizzle amount.
Listed only as 'Topping' with no quantity; 20 g inferred as a typical drizzle amount.
'1 Bulb' interpreted as one whole lettuce head; weight (~400 g) inferred from typical head size, not stated.
Weight inferred from typical size of one tomato; not stated in original.
'3rd Cucumber' interpreted as one-third of a cucumber (~100 g of an average 300 g cucumber); inferred.

title: Spaghetti Bolognese
instructions: Fry the onion in the olive oil over a fairly high heat for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and minced beef, frying until both are browned.
Stir in the mushrooms and dried oregano, cooking for a couple more minutes.
Add the canned tomatoes, beef stock, tomato purée and Worcestershire sauce, then season.
Bring to the boil, reduce the heat, cover and simmer gently, stirring occasionally, for about 30 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook the spaghetti in a large pot of boiling salted water according to the packet instructions.
Drain the spaghetti well, rinse briefly with hot water, then return it to the pan with a splash of olive oil if you like.
Stir the meat sauce through the spaghetti and serve hot.
Hand round grated Parmesan for sprinkling on top.

ingredients.note: 2 onions estimated at ~150g each
onion and oil fried together into the sauce base; oil remains in the served dish
1 tsp dried oregano estimated at ~2g
300ml converted to grams assuming water-like density
amount not specified for topping; inferred a typical 30g serving

title: Spicy Arrabiata Penne
instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil.
Season the water generously with kosher salt.
Drop in the penne and cook per the package timing, about 9 minutes.
Meanwhile, warm the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until it shimmers.
Toss in the garlic and stir until fragrant, 1 to 2 minutes.
Pour in the chopped tomatoes, sprinkle in the chilli flakes and Italian seasoning, then season with salt and pepper.
Bring the sauce to a boil and let it simmer for 5 minutes.
Pull the skillet off the heat and stir in the chopped basil.
Drain the pasta and toss it straight into the sauce.
Serve warm, finished with Parmigiano-Reggiano flakes and extra basil.

ingredients.note: oil is cooked into the sauce and served, not drained off
standard tin assumed to be 400g
6 basil leaves estimated at ~0.5g each
"sprinkling" is a vague garnish amount, estimated at 5g

title: Teriyaki Chicken Casserole
instructions: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) and lightly grease a 9x13-inch baking dish.
In a small saucepan, combine the soy sauce, water, brown sugar, ginger and garlic. Cover and bring to a boil over medium heat, then uncover and simmer for one minute.
In a separate small bowl, whisk the cornstarch with 2 tablespoons of water until smooth.
Stir the cornstarch slurry into the boiling sauce and cook until it thickens, then remove from the heat.
Lay the chicken breasts in the prepared baking dish and pour about a cup of the sauce over them.
Bake for 35 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through.
Take the dish out and shred the chicken right in the pan using two forks.
Meanwhile, steam or cook the stir-fry vegetables according to their package directions.
Add the cooked vegetables and the cooked rice to the dish with the shredded chicken, then pour in most of the remaining sauce, keeping a little back for drizzling.
Toss everything together gently in the dish, then return it to the oven and bake for another 15 minutes.
Let it stand for 5 minutes before serving, drizzling each portion with the reserved sauce.

ingredients.note: 3/4 cup converted using standard soy sauce density (~1.15 g/ml)
1/2 cup water, standard 240g/cup conversion
1/4 cup packed brown sugar, standard 220g/cup conversion
1/2 tsp ground ginger, standard ~2g/tsp conversion
1/2 tsp minced garlic, standard ~5g/tsp conversion
4 tbsp cornstarch, standard ~8g/tbsp conversion
2 chicken breasts estimated at ~170g each, added raw and baked
1 (12 oz.) bag = 340g; steamed/cooked before adding per instructions
3 cups cooked brown rice, ~185g/cup; added as already-cooked rice per 'cooked vegetables and rice' phrasing

title: Honey Teriyaki Salmon
instructions: Whisk together the soy sauce, sake, and honey (if using) to make the teriyaki glaze.
Toss the salmon in the glaze so it's well coated.
Heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium-low heat.
Pan-fry the salmon on both sides until cooked through and the glaze has thickened into a sticky coating.
Scatter over the sesame seeds and serve right away.

ingredients.note: oil is used to sear the salmon but remains as part of the glazed dish, not drained off
used as a raw garnish, not cooked

title: Pad See Ew
instructions: Whisk together the dark soy sauce, oyster sauce, soy sauce, vinegar, sugar and water in a small bowl to make the sauce.
Mince the garlic straight into a hot wok with the peanut oil; add the chicken and Chinese broccoli stems, and stir-fry until the chicken turns light golden.
Push everything to one side of the wok, crack in the egg and scramble it, letting a little char develop for extra flavour.
Add the noodles, Chinese broccoli leaves and the sauce; toss until the noodles are stained dark and the leaves have wilted, then serve immediately.

ingredients.note: 6oz/180g stated directly; rice stick noodles typically need soaking before stir-fry but recipe doesn't mention it explicitly
2 tbsp converted to grams (~16g/tbsp)
2 tbsp converted to grams (~20g/tbsp, denser sauce)
2 tsp converted to grams (~5g/tsp)

4 cups estimated at ~75g/cup for leafy greens

title: Vegan Lasagna
instructions: Preheat your oven to 180°C.
Boil the carrots, onion and zucchini together for 5-7 minutes until soft.
Stir in the lentils, add a stock cube if you like, and let everything simmer gently for about 20 minutes until the lentils are tender.
In a separate pan, blanch the spinach for a couple of minutes, then drain and set aside.
Cook the lasagne sheets in boiling water until tender, then drain and set aside.
Melt the vegan butter in a saucepan, whisk in the flour, then gradually pour in the soya milk, mustard and vinegar, stirring until you have a smooth sauce.
Layer the cooked lentil-vegetable mixture, spinach, sauce and lasagne sheets in a baking dish as you like.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 25 minutes until golden and bubbling.

ingredients.note: 1 cup dry lentils estimated at 200g
1 medium carrot estimated at 60g
1 medium onion estimated at 110g
1 small zucchini estimated at 100g
'sprinkling' is a vague amount; estimated 2g for garnish
blanched separately before assembly, per instructions
10 sheets estimated at 15g each; pre-boiled and drained before assembly
4 tbsp flour estimated at 8g each
300ml estimated as 300g
1.5 tsp estimated at 5g/tsp
1 tsp estimated at 5g

title: Chocolate Gateau
instructions: Preheat your oven to 180°C/350°F/Gas Mark 4, then grease and line the base of an 8in springform tin with baking parchment.
Break the chocolate into a heatproof bowl and set over a pan of gently simmering water, stirring until melted (or melt in the microwave in 2-3 min bursts, stirring between).
Cream the butter and sugar together in a mixing bowl with a wooden spoon until light and fluffy.
Beat in the eggs gradually, adding a little of the flour if the mixture looks like it's curdling.
Fold in the remaining flour along with the cooled melted chocolate and the milk, mixing until smooth.
Spread the batter into the prepared tin and bake for 50-55 minutes, until firm in the centre and a skewer comes out clean.
Cool in the tin for 10 minutes, then turn out onto a rack and leave to cool completely.

ingredients.note: 2 tablespoons converted to ~30g
5 eggs converted using ~50g per egg average

title: Mediterranean Pasta Salad
instructions: Bring a big pot of salted water to a rolling boil.
Tip in the farfalle, give it a stir, and cook for about 10 minutes or until just tender per the packet.
While the pasta cooks, wash and quarter the baby plum tomatoes, slice the green olives, and rinse the basil.
Tip the tomatoes into a salad bowl, tear the basil leaves over them, drizzle with a tablespoon of the olive oil, and toss to coat.
Drain the pasta into a colander and run cold water over it to cool it quickly.
Add the cooled pasta to the bowl with the tomatoes and basil and toss together.
Fold in the sliced olives, drained mozzarella balls, and chunks of tuna, mix well, then let the salad sit for at least half an hour so the flavours mingle.
Season with a generous grind of black pepper and salt to taste, drizzle over the remaining olive oil, and serve.

ingredients.note: '1 bunch' has no standard weight; inferred ~30 g for a bunch of fresh basil.
3 tablespoons converted at ~13 g/tbsp; used as dressing, remains in the served salad.
'to taste' with no quantity given; estimated a small pinch (~2 g).
'to taste' with no quantity given; estimated a small amount (~2 g) of black pepper.

title: Cream Cheese Tart
instructions: Make the crust: mix 250g flour, 125g butter, 1 egg, and a pinch of salt into a smooth dough.
Press the dough into a tart tin and chill it in the fridge.
For the filling, stir 300g cream cheese with 100ml milk until smooth.
Whisk in 3 eggs and 100g grated parmesan, then season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
Remove the chilled crust from the fridge and prick the base all over with a fork.
Pour the filling into the crust and bake at 175°C for about 25 minutes, covering with foil halfway through baking.
While the tart bakes, thinly slice about 350g mini tomatoes.
In a small pan, warm 3 tbsp olive oil, 3 tbsp white vinegar, and 1 tbsp honey with salt and pepper, stirring to combine.
Pour the warm dressing over the tomato slices and toss to coat.
Spoon the tomato slices over the baked tart, leaving excess liquid behind.
Finish with fresh basil leaves before serving.

ingredients.note: 1 egg estimated at 50g standard size
'pinch' inferred as ~1g
100ml converted 1:1 to grams
3 eggs estimated at 50g each
instructions call these 'mini tomatoes'; closest available key is plum tomatoes
3 tbsp converted at 15g/tbsp
1 tbsp converted at ~21g/tbsp (honey density)
no amount given for basil garnish; inferred small quantity

title: Potato Gratin with Chicken
instructions: Slice the potatoes thinly and simmer them for 5-8 minutes until just firm, not soft.
Finely slice the onions and place them in an oven dish with the olive oil and chicken stock.
Cook the onions until soft.
Drain the potatoes and tip them onto the softened onions.
Season everything and spoon over the cream or crème fraîche until covered but not swimming in it.
Grate the Parmesan over the top.
Finish under the grill until golden and bubbling.
Serve alongside cooked chicken, bacon, peas and spinach.

ingredients.note: 3 onions, no weight given; estimated 150g each
2 tbsp converted at 15g/tbsp; oil stays in the baking dish and is served, not a frying medium
'1 pot' quantity not specified; estimated standard 200g pot
topping amount not specified; estimated 30g grated for 4 servings

4 rashers, no weight given; estimated 30g each, assumed cooked and served alongside

title: Irish stew
instructions: Heat the oven to 180C/350F/gas 4.
Drain and rinse the soaked wheat, cover with plenty of water, bring to a boil and simmer about 1 hour until tender. Drain and set aside.

Season the lamb with 1 tsp salt and black pepper. Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large deep saute pan over medium-high heat, and sear the lamb in batches for 4 minutes until browned all over, adding oil as needed. Transfer to a bowl.
Lower to medium heat, add 1 tbsp oil, and fry the shallots 4 minutes until caramelised; add to the lamb. Brown the remaining vegetables the same way, adding oil as needed.
Add the wine, sugar, herbs, 1 tsp salt and a good grind of pepper to the pan and boil hard for 3 minutes.
Return the lamb, vegetables and wheat to the pot, add the stock, cover and boil for 5 minutes.
Transfer to the oven and cook, covered, for 1 hour 30 minutes.
Check the liquid; if there is a lot, uncover and boil a few minutes to reduce.

ingredients.original: 300g soaked overnight in water whole wheat
2kg cut into 3cm cubes lamb loin chops

ingredients.note: 120ml olive oil converted at ~0.9 g/ml; used in batches to sear lamb and brown vegetables and mostly left in the pan

title: Lamb tomato and sweet spices
instructions: Soak the pickled vine leaves in boiling water for 10 minutes, then pat them dry on a tea towel.
Mix all the filling ingredients except the fresh tomatoes together in a bowl.
Coarsely grate the tomatoes into the bowl, discarding the skins, then season with salt and pepper and stir well.
Leave the filling to rest for up to a day, then squeeze out and drain any excess juice before using.
Heat the olive oil in a pan and fry the ginger and garlic for a minute or two, taking care not to burn them.
Add the tomatoes, lemon juice and sugar, season, and simmer gently for 20 minutes to make the sauce.
Line the base of a heavy pan with any torn vine leaves, then cover completely with sliced fennel.
Lay each prepared vine leaf veiny-side up, spoon on about two teaspoons of filling, fold the sides over and roll tightly into a cigar shape.
Pack the rolls seam-down into the pan in tight layers or circles.
Pour the sauce over the rolls, topping up with water if needed to just cover them.
Weigh the rolls down with a plate, cover with a lid, bring to the boil, then reduce to a bare simmer and cook for 70 minutes until most of the liquid has evaporated.
Let everything cool a little, then bring the pan to the table and serve the rolls and fennel warm with rice, spooning over the braising juices and scattering with coriander.

ingredients.note: weight inferred from '4cm piece' description
weight inferred, ~3g per clove
weight inferred, ~3g per leaf for 50 leaves
weight inferred for '1 large fennel bulb'
weight inferred for '1 medium onion'

ingredients.original: 1 small peeled and coarsely grated potatoes
ingredients.note: weight inferred for '1 small potato'

title: Rigatoni with fennel sausage sauce
instructions: Heat a tablespoon of olive oil in a large lidded sauté pan and fry the sausage slices over medium-high heat for about 10 minutes, turning now and then, until deeply browned all over.
Lift the sausages onto a plate, then add the onion and fennel to the same pan and fry gently for 15 minutes until soft and caramelised, splashing in a little more oil if the pan looks dry.
Stir in the smoky paprika, sliced garlic and half the fennel seeds and cook for 2 more minutes.
Pour in the red wine, bring to a boil and let it bubble for 30 seconds to reduce by half.
Add the chopped tomatoes, sugar, 100ml water, the browned sausages and half a teaspoon of salt, cover and simmer for 30 minutes, lifting the lid after 10 minutes so the sauce thickens.
Take the pan off the heat, stir through the olives and remaining fennel seeds, then set the ragù aside until serving.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil, cook the rigatoni for 12-14 minutes or according to the packet until al dente, and reheat the ragù while it cooks.
Drain the pasta, return it to the pot, toss with a tablespoon of olive oil, then divide among bowls.

Blitz the pecorino, anchovy, crushed garlic and olive oil with a tablespoon of water in a small food processor to a rough paste, then add the basil and pulse just until combined, keeping the pesto a little rough.
Spoon the ragù over the pasta, top with a spoonful of pesto, scatter over any reserved fennel fronds, and serve at once.

ingredients.note: 2½ tbsp converted to grams (≈15ml/tbsp); oil stays in the sauce, not drained off

ingredients.original: 6 cut into 1.5cm-thick slices Italian fennel sausages

title: Smoky Lentil Chili with Squash
instructions: Slice the squash into thin crescents, drizzle with oil, and season with sea salt (toss in a few sage leaves if you like).
Roast the squash at 205°C (400°F) for 20-30 minutes, flipping halfway, until soft and golden, then let it cool and cube it.
Rinse the lentils, cover with water, bring to a boil, then simmer uncovered for 20-30 minutes until tender, then drain and set aside.
Heat the olive oil in a medium pot over low heat and sauté the onion and leek for about 5 minutes until softening.
Stir in the garlic, cumin, and coriander and cook for a few more minutes.
Add the paprika, cinnamon, chili powder, cocoa, Worcestershire sauce, salt, and oregano.

Pour in the diced tomatoes, water (or stock), and carrots, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes until the vegetables are tender and the chili has thickened, stirring occasionally and topping up liquid as needed.
Stir in the cooked lentils and roasted squash cubes and simmer for 10 more minutes to heat through.
Serve topped with sliced jalapeño, lime wedges, cilantro, green onions, and cashew sour cream.
For the cashew sour cream, soak the cashews in boiled water for at least four hours, or in cold water overnight.
Drain the cashews, blend with about 1/2 cup fresh water until creamy, adjusting the water as needed.
Season the cashew cream with a pinch of sea salt and the apple cider vinegar (or lemon juice).

ingredients.note: assumed medium onion ~150g
assumed medium leek ~100g
assumed average carrot ~100g each
boiled separately until tender, then added already cooked to the chili

No yield stated in recipe; servings inferred as 5 from total dish mass (~2.7kg food, ~550g/serving). Squash weight assumed ~500g edible flesh for one small squash, roasted whole then cubed and added already cooked.

title: Dal fry
instructions: Rinse the toor dal and soak it in about 3 cups of water for at least an hour, until it doubles in volume.
Drain off the soaking water.
Put the dal in a pot with 2½ cups fresh water, salt and turmeric, and cook on medium-high heat for about 30 minutes until soft and the mixture is like a thick soup.
In a frying pan, heat the ghee and add the cumin and mustard seeds.
Once the seeds crackle, add the bay leaves, green chilli, ginger and chilli powder, and stir for a few seconds.
Add the tomatoes, salt and sugar, and cook, stirring, until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy.
Stir in the cilantro and garam masala and cook for about a minute more.
Pour this tempering over the cooked dal, mix well, and simmer for another minute.
Serve hot with naan.

only the 2½ cups cooking water counted; soak water is drained and discarded

title: Rocky Road Fudge
instructions: Line an 8-inch square pan with wax paper or foil and give it a light coat of non-stick spray.
Scatter half a cup of the miniature marshmallows over the bottom of the pan.
Melt the chocolate chips and peanut butter together in a microwave-safe bowl, zapping in 20-second bursts and stirring between each until smooth.
Stir in the vanilla extract until fully combined.
Set aside two tablespoons of the chopped peanuts for topping later.
Fold the remaining marshmallows and the rest of the chopped peanuts into the melted chocolate mixture.
Spread the chocolate mixture evenly into the prepared pan, then scatter the reserved peanuts and any extra marshmallows on top.
Chill in the fridge for about 4 hours, or until firm.
Lift the fudge out of the pan and peel away the wax paper.
Slice into bite-sized pieces and serve.

ingredients.note: 2 cups converted using ~50g/cup for mini marshmallows
3 cups converted using ~170g/cup for chocolate chips
½ cup converted using ~258g/cup for peanut butter
1 tsp converted using ~4g/tsp for liquid extract
1¼ cups chopped peanuts converted using ~150g/cup; instructions also mention almonds as an alternative nut, not summed

title: Hot Chocolate Fudge
instructions: Line an 8-inch square baking pan with wax paper or foil and give it a light coat of non-stick spray.
In a microwave-safe bowl, combine the dark chocolate chips, heavy cream and half of the sweetened condensed milk.
Microwave the mixture in 20-second bursts, stirring between each, until the chocolate is fully melted.
Stir in the vanilla extract until the mixture is smooth.
Pour the dark chocolate mixture into the prepared pan and spread it into an even layer.
In a separate bowl, combine the white chocolate chips with the remaining sweetened condensed milk.
Microwave in 20-second bursts, stirring between each, until melted and smooth.
Spread the white chocolate mixture evenly over the dark chocolate layer.
Scatter the miniature marshmallows over the top and press them gently into the surface.
Refrigerate for about 4 hours, or until fully set.
Lift the fudge out of the pan using the wax paper and peel the paper away carefully.
Cut into bite-sized pieces to serve.

ingredients.note: 2 cups converted to grams using standard chocolate chip density (~168 g/cup); inferred conversion.
2 tbsp converted at ~15 g/tbsp; inferred conversion.
1 standard 14 oz can = 397 g; split between two chocolate mixtures per instructions but total amount used is one can.
1 tsp converted at ~4.2 g/tsp; inferred conversion.
1-1/3 cups converted using ~170 g/cup for white chocolate chips; inferred conversion.
1.5 cups converted at ~50 g/cup for miniature marshmallows (light, aerated); inferred conversion.

title: Christmas Pudding Flapjack
instructions: Preheat your oven to 180°C (fan 160°C, gas mark 4) and grease and line a 25cm x 20cm tin.
Melt the butter, sugar, syrup and orange zest together in a large saucepan over medium heat, stirring gently until smooth — keep the heat gentle so you don't boil off any volume.
Tip in the oats and stir until every flake is coated.
Fold through the leftover Christmas pudding, then spread the mixture into the prepared tin and flatten the top with a spoon.
Bake for 40 minutes, until the edges start to turn golden brown.
While still warm in the tin, score the slab into 12 squares.
Let it cool completely in the tin before cutting along the scored lines.
Store in an airtight tin for up to 5 days, or freeze for up to 1 month.

ingredients.note: inferred grams for zest of 1 orange (~6g); not stated in original text
missing ingredient: 'Christmas pudding' has no matching key in the allowed list; leftover pudding is already-cooked when added

title: Eton Mess
instructions: Blitz half the strawberries in a blender to a smooth purée.
Roughly chop the rest of the strawberries, keeping four whole ones aside for decoration.
Whip the double cream to stiff peaks, then gently fold in the strawberry purée and the crushed meringue nests.
Fold through the chopped strawberries and the ginger cordial, if you're using it.
Divide the mixture evenly between four chilled wine glasses.
Top each glass with a reserved whole strawberry and a sprig of mint, then serve straight away.

ingredients.note: 400ml converted at ~1g/ml for double cream
inferred ~10g per nest for 3 x 7.5cm nests; no weight given in original
1 tbsp converted assuming ~20g for a syrupy cordial; optional per 'if using'
garnish sprigs, no weight given; small inferred amount

title: Bread and Butter Pudding
instructions: Grease a 1 litre pie dish generously with butter.
Trim the crusts from the bread, butter one side of each slice, then cut into triangles.
Lay a layer of bread in the dish, buttered side up, and scatter over some sultanas and a dusting of cinnamon.
Keep layering bread, sultanas and cinnamon until everything is used, finishing with a layer of bread on top.
Warm the milk and cream together gently in a pan until scalding but not boiling.
Whisk the eggs with three quarters of the sugar until pale.
Pour in the warm milk and cream, stir well, then strain the mixture into a jug to make a smooth custard.
Pour the custard over the bread, sprinkle with nutmeg and the remaining sugar, and let it stand for 30 minutes to soak in.
Heat the oven to 180C/355F/Gas 4.
Bake for 30-40 minutes until the custard is set and the top is golden-brown.

ingredients.note: 8 thin slices, weight inferred at ~35g per slice
2 tsp converted to grams (~2.5g/tsp)
50ml converted at ~1.02g/ml
2 free-range eggs, assumed ~50g each
quantity 'to taste' not specified, inferred a small pinch (~1g)

title: Sticky Toffee Pudding Ultimate
instructions: Stone and finely chop the dates, then tip them into a bowl and pour over the boiling water.
Leave to soak for about 30 minutes until cool, then mash lightly with a fork and stir in the vanilla extract.
Butter and flour seven mini pudding tins (about 200ml each) and sit them on a baking sheet; heat the oven to 180C (fan 160C, gas 4).
Mix the flour and bicarbonate of soda together in a bowl.
Beat the eggs in a separate bowl.
Beat the butter and demerara sugar together in a large bowl until slightly creamy.
Add the beaten eggs a little at a time, beating well between additions.
Beat in the black treacle.
Fold in a third of the flour mixture, then half the milk, repeating until all the flour and milk are incorporated without overbeating.
Stir the soaked dates into the batter.
Spoon the batter evenly into the tins.
Bake for 20-25 minutes until risen and firm.
Meanwhile, make the sauce: put the muscovado sugar, butter and half the double cream in a saucepan.
Bring to the boil over medium heat, stirring until the sugar dissolves completely.
Stir in the black treacle, raise the heat slightly and let it bubble for 2-3 minutes until rich toffee-coloured, stirring so it doesn't catch.
Take off the heat and beat in the remaining cream.
Remove the puddings from the oven, leave briefly in the tins, then loosen and turn out.
Serve now with the sauce drizzled over, or coat fully in sauce and rest a day or two for extra stickiness.
To do this, pour half the sauce into an ovenproof dish, sit the puddings on top, pour over the rest, and cover loosely with foil.
When ready to serve, reheat at 180C (fan 160C, gas 4) for 15-20 minutes, covered, until the sauce is bubbling.
Serve on their own, or with cream, custard, or a scoop of ice cream.

ingredients.note: '1 tsp' converted to grams (~4g); inferred conversion
'1 tsp' converted to grams (~5g); inferred conversion
'2 eggs' assumed 50g each (standard large egg); inferred conversion
'2 tbsp black treacle' converted to grams (~20g/tbsp); mapped to closest key 'treacle'; inferred conversion
'1 scoop' assumed ~60g portion; inferred conversion; serving suggestion
'1 tbsp black treacle' converted to grams (~20g); mapped to closest key 'treacle'; inferred conversion

title: Vegan Chocolate Cake
instructions: Whisk together the self-raising flour, coco sugar, raw cacao and baking powder in a bowl.
In a separate bowl, combine the flax eggs, almond milk, vanilla and boiling water.
Pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients and stir until fully blended into a smooth batter.
Pour the batter into a prepared cake tin and bake at 180°C for 45 minutes.
Once cooled, decorate with melted vegan chocolate.

ingredients.note: 1 1/4 cup converted to grams using ~125g/cup for flour; inferred.
1/2 cup converted to grams using ~200g/cup; inferred.

title: Chicken Handi
instructions: Heat the oil in a large pot or wok big enough to hold all the chicken.
Add the sliced onion and fry until deep golden brown, then remove it to a plate and set aside.
In the same pot, sauté the chopped garlic for a minute.
Add the chopped tomatoes and cook until soft, about 5 minutes.
Return the fried onion to the pot and stir well.
Stir in the ginger paste and sauté thoroughly.
Add the cumin seeds, half the coriander seeds, and the chopped green chillies, giving them a quick stir.
Add the turmeric powder and chilli powder, sautéing the spices for a couple of minutes.
Add the chicken, season with salt to taste, cover, and cook on medium-low heat until almost cooked through, about 15 minutes—keep the heat gentle to build flavor, don't rush it on high heat.
Once the oil separates from the spices, drop the heat to the lowest setting and stir in the beaten yogurt so it doesn't split.
Sprinkle in the remaining coriander seeds and half the dried fenugreek leaves, mixing well.
Stir in the cream and mix to bring everything together.
Finish with the remaining kasuri methi and garam masala, then serve the chicken handi hot with naan or rotis.

inferred grams: 2 tomatoes at ~120g each (no weight stated)

title: Chicken Alfredo Primavera
instructions: Melt 1 tablespoon of butter with 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Season the chicken breasts on both sides with salt and a pinch of pepper.
Cook the chicken in the skillet for 5-7 minutes per side until cooked through, then transfer to a cutting board to rest.
Meanwhile, bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the bowtie pasta, stirring occasionally, until al dente, about 12 minutes.
Reserve 1/2 cup of the pasta water, then drain the rest.
Lower the heat to medium and add the remaining butter and olive oil to the same skillet.
Add the squash, broccoli, mushrooms, red pepper, onion and red pepper flakes, stirring to coat, and cook until tender, about 5 minutes, holding off on salt until they've browned.
Stir in the garlic with a generous pinch of salt and pepper and cook for 1 minute.
Deglaze the pan with the white wine and simmer until reduced by half, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the milk, heavy cream and reserved pasta water, bring to a gentle boil, then simmer for 2-3 minutes to reduce.
Turn off the heat and stir in the Parmesan cheese and the cooked pasta.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Garnish with extra Parmesan and chopped parsley before serving.

title: Spicy North African Potato Salad
instructions: Put the potatoes in a pot of cold water and bring to a boil.
Boil for about 20 minutes, until a knife slides through them easily.
Meanwhile, whisk together the harissa spice, olive oil, salt, pepper and lemon juice in a small bowl.
Drain the potatoes and roughly chop them in half once cool enough to handle.
Stir the harissa mix and spring onions into the warm potatoes.
Lay the rocket out in a large salad bowl.
Top with the potato mixture and toss gently.
Scatter over the feta, mint and pine nuts.
Adjust salt and pepper to taste before serving.

title: Fish pie
instructions: Boil the potatoes in salted water until tender, about 15 minutes, then drain and dry them off over low heat for 30 seconds.
Mash the potatoes with a tablespoon of the olive oil and season to taste.
Meanwhile, bring the milk to the boil in a sauté pan with the fish, then take off the heat, cover and let it stand for 3 minutes.
Lift out the fish, pat it dry, flake it into an ovenproof dish and discard the skin and bones, keeping the poaching milk.
Heat the rest of the oil in a pan, stir in the flour and cook briefly, then gradually whisk in 200-250ml of the reserved milk, discarding the rest.
Grate in a little nutmeg, season, and let the sauce bubble until thickened, then stir in the cream.
Preheat the oven to 190°C (fan 170°C, gas 5).
Grate the Jerusalem artichokes and add them to the fish along with the leek, prawns and herbs.
Stir the lemon zest and juice into the sauce, pour it over the fish mixture and mix gently.
Spoon the mash on top, fork up peaks so they crisp in the oven, scatter over the Gruyère and bake for 35-40 minutes until golden and bubbling.
Serve with wilted greens.

title: Beef Wellington
instructions: Whizz the mushrooms in a food processor with a little seasoning until you get a rough paste.
Tip the paste into a hot pan and cook for about 10 minutes, tossing often, until all the moisture has cooked away, then spread it out on a plate to cool.
Heat a splash of olive oil in a frying pan, season the beef fillet and sear it for just 30 seconds on each side to colour it, not cook it through.
Let the beef cool, then brush it all over with the English mustard.
Lay a sheet of cling film on the counter and arrange the Parma ham slices on it in slightly overlapping rows.
Spread the cooled mushroom paste over the ham with a palette knife, then set the seared beef fillet in the middle.
Using the cling film, roll the ham and mushrooms tightly around the beef into a neat barrel shape, twist the ends to seal, and chill for 15-20 minutes so it firms up.
Roll the puff pastry out on a floured surface into a large rectangle about as thick as a £1 coin.
Unwrap the beef and place it in the centre of the pastry, brush the surrounding pastry with egg yolk, then fold and wrap the pastry snugly around the beef, trimming off any excess.
Turn the parcel seam-side down onto a baking sheet, brush all over with egg yolk, and chill for about 15 minutes to let the pastry rest.
Heat the oven to 200C/400F/gas 6.
Score the pastry lightly at 1cm intervals, glaze again with beaten egg yolk, and bake for 20 minutes.
Lower the oven to 180C/350F/gas 4 and bake for a further 15 minutes.
Rest the wellington for 10-15 minutes before slicing and serving with your chosen sides — the beef should still be pink in the centre.

title: Poutine
instructions: Heat your oil in a deep fryer or heavy skillet to 185°C (365°F).
Warm the beef gravy in a saucepan or the microwave until hot.
Slide the thin-cut potatoes into the hot oil and fry until light golden, about 5 minutes.
Lift the fries out onto a paper-towel-lined plate to drain.
Pile the fries onto a serving platter and scatter the cheese curds over the top.
Ladle the hot gravy over the fries and cheese, and serve right away.

ingredients.note: Original just says 'Dash Vegetable Oil'; deep-frying needs a submersion quantity of oil, inferred at ~750g since only absorbed oil counts toward the dish and this is a frying medium.
'1 Can' — assumed a standard ~300g can of beef gravy; size not specified in recipe.
5 thin-cut potatoes, estimated ~120g each for fry-cut size; not weighed in original.

title: Lamb Biryani
instructions: Grind the cashews, poppy seeds and cumin seeds with a little water into a smooth paste; set aside.
Heat oil and deep-fry the sliced onions until light brown, then drain on paper towels — they'll crisp further as they cool.
Fry the cashew nuts in the same oil until golden brown; set aside.
Wash the basmati rice and soak it in water for 20 minutes.
In a wide pan, heat oil over medium heat, then add the sliced onions, the ground paste, green chillies, ginger garlic paste and garlic; fry for a minute.
Add the tomatoes and cook until soft but not mushy.
Stir in the red chilli powder, biryani masala, mint and coriander leaves, and sauté briefly.
Pull the pan off the heat, stir in the yogurt to stop it curdling, then return it to the stove.
Add the washed lamb, salt and half a cup of water; cover and cook on medium-low for about an hour, or give it 6 whistles in a pressure cooker, until tender and most of the water has evaporated.
Meanwhile, bring a large pot of salted water with cumin seeds to a boil, add the soaked rice and cook about 7 minutes until 80% done, then drain.
Spread the cooked lamb evenly in the pan, then layer the drained rice on top.
Garnish with fried onions, ghee, mint, coriander leaves and saffron dissolved in warm milk.
Cover tightly and bake at 350°F for 15 minutes, or simmer covered on the stove — medium heat for 12 minutes then lowest heat for 5 minutes — until the rice is cooked but not mushy.
Mix gently and serve hot.

title: Tandoori chicken
instructions: Combine the lemon juice, paprika, and chopped red onions in a large shallow dish.
Slash each chicken thigh three times, then turn them in the lemon mixture and let sit for 10 minutes.
Mix the yogurt, ginger, garlic, garam masala, cumin, chilli powder, and turmeric together to make the marinade.
Pour the marinade over the chicken and mix well to coat every piece.
Cover and chill for at least 1 hour, or up to a day ahead.
Heat the grill.
Lift the chicken pieces onto a rack set over a baking tray.
Brush lightly with oil.
Grill for 8 minutes on each side, until lightly charred and cooked through.

title: Baingan Bharta
instructions: Rinse the aubergine well and pat it completely dry.
Rub oil all over the skin and set it up for roasting over an open flame, on the grill, or in the oven — flame gives the best smoky flavour.
Turn it every 2-3 minutes so it cooks evenly; you can tuck a few garlic cloves in with it while it roasts.
Roast until fully tender — a knife should slide in with no resistance.
Plunge the roasted aubergine into a bowl of water to cool.

Optionally infuse extra smokiness: heat a small piece of charcoal until red-hot, make small cuts in the aubergine, place the charcoal on the plate beside it, drizzle a few drops of oil onto the charcoal so it smokes, then cover tightly with a large bowl for 1-2 minutes to trap the smoke.
Peel the skin off the roasted aubergine.
Chop or mash the flesh finely.
Heat oil in a kadai or pan and add the chopped onion and garlic.
Sauté until the onion turns translucent, without letting it brown.
Add the chopped green chilli and sauté for a minute.
Stir in the chopped tomatoes.
Cook the tomatoes down until the oil starts to separate from the mixture.
Add the red chilli powder and stir it through.
Fold in the chopped roasted aubergine.
Mix the aubergine thoroughly into the onion-tomato masala.
Season with salt and cook for a further 4-5 minutes.
Finish by stirring through the coriander leaves, or use them as a garnish, then serve with phulkas, rotis, chapatis, bread, or plain or jeera rice.

title: Lamb Rogan josh
instructions: Whizz the onions in a food processor until very finely chopped.
Heat the oil in a large heavy-based pan and fry the onions with the lid on, stirring now and then, until they're golden and soft.
Stir in the garlic and ginger and fry for 5 minutes more.
Tip in the curry paste, all the spices, the bay leaves and the tomato purée; stir well over the heat for about 30 seconds.
Add the lamb and 300ml water, stir to mix, then turn down the heat and stir in the yogurt.
Cover the pan and simmer gently for 40-60 minutes until the lamb is tender and the sauce is thick.
Scatter with coriander and serve with plain basmati or pilau rice.

title: Recheado Masala Fish
instructions: Soak the spices, ginger, garlic, tamarind pulp and kashmiri chillies (skip the oil) in vinegar.
Stir in the sugar and salt.
Add the turmeric powder.
Mix everything well and let it marinate for 35-40 minutes.
Grind the mixture into a smooth, thick paste, adding a splash more vinegar only if needed - keep it thick so it clings to the fish.
Rinse the mackerel, slit each one down the centre and score a few extra cuts on top.
Stuff the paste into the centre and cuts, then coat the whole fish with the remaining paste; marinate for 30 minutes.
Heat oil in a shallow pan until hot, then shallow-fry the stuffed mackerels.
Fry until golden brown on both sides.
Serve the recheado mackerels hot with salad, lime wedges, rice and curry.

title: Osso Buco alla Milanese
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300°F (150°C).
Season the veal shanks all over with salt and pepper, then dredge each one in flour, shaking off the excess; discard whatever flour is left over.
Heat the olive oil with 1 tablespoon of the butter in a wide Dutch oven over medium-high heat until the butter melts and the oil shimmers.
Lay the shanks in flat-side down and brown well on both sides, about 5 minutes per side, working in batches if the pot is crowded, then move them to a platter.
Pour off and discard the fat, wipe the pot clean without disturbing the caramelized bits, then melt the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat.
Add the onion, carrot, celery, and fennel, season with salt and pepper, and cook until softened but not browned, about 6 minutes.
Stir in the garlic, orange zest, marjoram, and bay leaf, and let them stew for a minute or two.
Pour in the wine, bring to a boil, and reduce by about half, roughly 5 minutes.
Add the stock and tomatoes with their juice, and boil again until the liquid reduces to about a cup, roughly 10 minutes.
Nestle the shanks into the pot bone-up, pour over any accumulated juices, press a sheet of parchment down close to the meat, then cover tightly with the lid.

Slide the pot into the lower part of the oven and braise at a gentle simmer for about 2 hours, checking the heat after 15 minutes and basting the shanks with pan juices after 40 minutes, until the meat is fork-tender and pulling from the bone.
While the shanks braise, stir together the minced garlic, parsley, and lemon zest for the gremolata, and keep it covered somewhere cool.
Once the veal is tender, uncover, sprinkle over half the gremolata, and return to the oven uncovered for 15 minutes to caramelize.
Carefully lift the shanks out of the pot, arrange them on a platter, and cover loosely with foil to keep warm.
Skim any surface fat from the braising liquid, boil to concentrate the sauce if it tastes thin, then adjust the seasoning and stir in a bit more gremolata for extra brightness if needed.
Portion the shanks — one per person if modest, or split larger ones along their natural seams — and make sure the marrow goes to whoever wants it most.
Plate the veal alongside risotto if serving, scatter over the remaining gremolata, and spoon the hot sauce over just before bringing it to the table.

ingredients.note: 4 shanks, no weight stated; estimated at ~450g each based on typical osso buco veal shank size.

title: Ribollita
instructions: Warm 2 tablespoons of the oil in a large pot over medium heat, then add the onion, carrot, celery and garlic with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Cook, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables soften, 5 to 10 minutes.
Heat the oven to 500°F.
Drain the beans, rinsing them if canned, then add them to the pot with the tomatoes and their juices, the water, rosemary and thyme.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat so it simmers steadily; cover and cook, stirring once or twice to break up the tomatoes, until the flavors meld, 15 to 20 minutes.
Fish out and discard the rosemary and thyme stems if you like, then stir in the kale.
Taste and adjust the seasoning.
Lay the bread slices over the top of the stew so they overlap as little as possible.
Scatter the red onion slices on top, drizzle with the remaining 3 tablespoons of oil, and sprinkle with Parmesan.
Put the pot in the oven and bake until the bread, onions and cheese are browned and crisp, 10 to 15 minutes (or finish under the broiler if your pot fits).
Divide the soup and bread among 4 bowls and serve.

title: Beef Brisket Pot Roast
instructions: Trim any large loose fat chunks from one side of the brisket, keeping the thin fat cap.
Score the fat cap in a cross-hatch pattern, cutting through the fat only, not the meat.
Salt the brisket generously and let it rest at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Pat the brisket dry and sear it fatty-side down in a thick-bottomed, oven-proof pot over medium-high heat for 5-8 minutes until nicely browned.
Flip and brown the other side for a few more minutes, then remove the brisket and set it aside.
Add the chopped onions to the fat left in the pot (add a splash of olive oil if there isn't enough), season with salt, and sauté over high heat for 5-8 minutes until lightly browned.
Stir in the garlic and cook for another 1-2 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 300°F and tie the bay leaves, rosemary and thyme into a bundle with kitchen twine.
Nestle the brisket back into the pot among the onions, add the beef stock and herb bundle, and bring to a boil on the stovetop.
Cover and transfer the pot to the oven, cooking for 3 hours, flipping the brisket every hour so it cooks evenly.
Add the carrots, cover, and cook for 1 more hour until the carrots are tender and the brisket is falling-apart tender.
Lift the brisket onto a cutting board and tent it with foil; discard the herb bundle.
If making a sauce, set aside the carrots and half the onions, then blend the remaining pot contents smooth, stirring in the mustard if desired, and keep warm in a small pot.
Slice the brisket across the grain into 1/4 to 1/2-inch slices.
Serve with the onions, carrots, gravy, and your choice of mashed potatoes, egg noodles or polenta.

title: Kentucky Fried Chicken
instructions: Preheat your fryer to 350°F (175°C).
Mix all the spice-mix ingredients thoroughly in a bowl.
Combine the spice mix with the flour, brown sugar and salt.
Dip each chicken piece in egg white to lightly coat it, then dredge it in the flour mixture, turning and pressing so the coating really sticks.
Repeat until all the chicken pieces are coated.
Let the coated chicken rest for 5 minutes so the crust can dry a little.
Fry the chicken in batches — breasts and wings need 12-14 minutes, legs and thighs a bit longer — until a thermometer in the thickest part reads 165°F (74°C).
Drain the fried chicken on paper towels and serve hot.

title: Thai Green Curry
instructions: Boil the potatoes for 5 minutes, then add the green beans and simmer for another 3 minutes until both are just tender.
Drain and set the potatoes and beans aside.
Heat the oil in a wok until very hot, then fry the garlic for a few seconds until golden - don't let it darken.
Stir in the curry paste and cook briefly to release the spices' aroma.
Pour in the coconut milk and bring it to a gentle bubble.
Add the fish sauce and sugar, then the chicken pieces.
Reduce the heat, cover, and simmer for about 8 minutes until the chicken is cooked through.
Return the potatoes and beans to the pan and let them heat through in the coconut milk.
Stir in the shredded lime leaves for a citrus lift.
Add the basil leaves and cook only briefly so they stay bright.
Garnish with extra lime and serve immediately with boiled rice.

title: French Lentils With Garlic and Thyme
instructions: Warm the oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Tip in the chopped onion, garlic and carrot and celery, and sauté until softened, about 5-10 minutes.
Pour in 6 cups of water, then add the lentils, thyme, bay leaves and salt.
Bring to a boil, then drop to a fast simmer.
Simmer until the lentils are tender and most of the water is absorbed, 20-25 minutes, draining off any excess liquid if needed.
Serve right away, or let cool and reheat later.
For a richer flavour, swap some of the water for chicken stock, reducing the water by the same amount.

title: Roasted Eggplant With Tahini, Pine Nuts, and Lentils
instructions: Heat the oven to 450°F (230°C) with a rack in the center.
Warm 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the carrots, celery, and onion, and cook, stirring, until softened but not browned, about 4 minutes.
Stir in the garlic and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add the lentils, bay leaves, water, and a pinch of salt, then bring to a simmer.
Cover with the lid slightly ajar and simmer until the lentils are tender, about 30 minutes, topping up with water if they dry out.
Uncover, stir in the vinegar, and reduce until the lentils are moist but not soupy.
Season with salt and pepper, then cover and keep warm.
Halve each eggplant and score the flesh in a cross-hatch pattern with a paring knife.
Arrange the eggplant halves cut-side up on a foil-lined baking sheet.
Brush each half with oil, letting it soak in before adding more, then season with salt and pepper.
Top each half with a rosemary sprig and roast until completely tender and well charred, 25 to 35 minutes.
Remove from the oven and discard the rosemary.
Toast the pine nuts in 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat, tossing frequently, until golden and aromatic, about 4 minutes.
Stir half the parsley and rosemary into the warm lentils and spread onto a serving platter.
Arrange the eggplant halves on top, spoon tahini sauce over each, and sprinkle with the toasted pine nuts.
Finish with the remaining parsley and rosemary, drizzle with more olive oil, and serve.

title: Stovetop Eggplant With Harissa, Chickpeas, and Cumin Yogurt
instructions: Heat the oil in a 12-inch skillet over high heat until it shimmers.
Add the eggplants, lower the heat to medium, and season with salt and pepper as you rotate them to brown on all sides.
Keep turning the eggplants, standing them on end if needed, until a fork slides through with no resistance, about 20 minutes, lowering heat and splashing in water if they start to scorch.
Stir together the harissa, chickpeas, and tomatoes, then tip them into the pan with the eggplants.
Cook until the tomatoes blister and break down, about 5 minutes, seasoning with salt and pepper and loosening with a little water to a saucy consistency.
Meanwhile, stir the yogurt and cumin together in a serving bowl and season with salt and pepper.
Scatter the parsley over the eggplant mixture, drizzle with more extra virgin olive oil, and serve with the cumin yogurt alongside.

title: Chicken Fajita Mac and Cheese
instructions: Sauté the onion, peppers and garlic in olive oil until softened and translucent.
Push the vegetables aside, add the diced chicken breast to the pan, and season with the fajita seasoning and salt.
Let the chicken pick up a little colour.
Pour in the cream and stock, then stir in the macaroni.
Simmer gently on low heat for 20 minutes.
Stir in the cheddar cheese until melted and combined.
Finish with a scatter of roasted peppers and chopped parsley.

title: Cajun spiced fish tacos
instructions: Mix the cajun spice and cayenne pepper with a pinch of seasoning on a plate, then coat the fish fillets all over.
Heat a little oil in a frying pan and add the fish, cooking over medium heat until golden.
Lower the heat and keep frying until the fish is cooked through, about 10 minutes, working in batches if the pan's too crowded.
Meanwhile, whisk together the dressing ingredients (sour cream, lemon, garlic) with a little seasoning.
Warm the tortillas in the microwave for 5-10 seconds to soften them.
Pile the tortillas with avocado, lettuce and spring onion, spoon over the salsa, top with flaked fish and drizzle with the dressing.

title: Katsu Chicken curry
instructions: Warm the oil in a medium non-stick saucepan and cook the onion and garlic until softened.
Stir in the carrots and let them cook gently over low heat for 10 to 12 minutes.
Sprinkle in the flour and curry powder, stirring for a minute to cook off the rawness.
Gradually whisk in the stock until smooth, then add the honey, soy sauce and bay leaf.
Bring the sauce slowly to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 20 minutes until it thickens but still pours easily.
Stir in the garam masala, then strain the sauce through a sieve back into the pan and keep it warm on low heat.
Season the chicken breasts on both sides with salt and pepper.
Set up three bowls with flour, beaten egg and breadcrumbs.
Dredge each chicken breast in flour, dip in egg, then coat thoroughly in breadcrumbs on both sides.
Heat the frying oil in a large pan over medium-high heat and fry the chicken until golden, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Drain the cooked chicken on kitchen paper to remove excess oil.
Slice the chicken, pour the curry sauce over it, and serve with white rice.

title: Laksa King Prawn Noodles
instructions: Warm the olive oil in a medium saucepan and toss in the sliced red chilli, letting it cook for a minute.
Stir in the Thai red curry paste and cook for another minute to release its aroma.
Dissolve the vegetable stock cube in a jug with 700ml boiling water, then pour into the pan and stir to combine.
Add the coconut milk and bring the mixture to a boil.
Season with fish sauce and a little salt.
Drop in the rice noodles and simmer for 3-4 minutes until they start to soften.
Squeeze in the lime juice, add the king prawns, and cook for 2-3 minutes until they're warmed through.
Scatter over half of the coriander.
Serve in bowls topped with the remaining coriander and lime wedges for squeezing over.

title: Toad In The Hole
instructions: Heat your oven to 200°C (fan 180°C, gas 6).
Fry the sausages in a non-stick pan until nicely browned all over.
Pour the vegetable oil into a 30cm x 25cm x 6cm deep roasting tray and slide it into the oven for 5 minutes to get smoking hot.
In a bowl, tip in the plain flour, crack in the eggs, and stir in the grated horseradish.
Gradually beat in the milk until you have a smooth batter, then season.
Nestle the browned sausages into the hot roasting tray and pour the batter straight over them.
Scatter the cherry tomatoes on top and bake for 30 minutes until the batter is puffed up and golden.

title: Salmon Prawn Risotto
instructions: Melt the butter in a thick-based pan over low heat.
Add the finely chopped onion and cook gently, without letting it colour, until soft.
Stir in the rice, coating every grain in the butter.
Pour in the white wine and cook, stirring, until it's fully absorbed.
Add the hot vegetable stock a ladle at a time, stirring and waiting for each addition to be absorbed before adding more, until the rice is tender.
Season with the lemon juice and zest and a good grind of black pepper — the salmon and prawns should bring enough saltiness, so hold off on extra salt.
Stir gently to heat everything through.
Grill the salmon until just cooked.
Blanch the asparagus tips briefly in boiling water.
Scatter the risotto with Parmesan shavings and the seasonal vegetables.
Place the grilled salmon on top along with the prawns and blanched asparagus, then serve.

title: Beef Sunday Roast
instructions: Bring a pan of water to the boil and cook the broccoli and carrots together until tender.
Roast the beef and potatoes in the oven for about 45 minutes, checking the potatoes regularly so they don't overcook.
Heat the oven to 230°C (fan 210°C / gas 8) and drizzle a little sunflower oil evenly into the Yorkshire pudding tins, then pop them in the oven to heat through.
Beat the plain flour and eggs together in a bowl until smooth, then gradually whisk in the milk until the batter is completely lump-free; season with salt and pepper.
Pour the batter into a jug, pull the hot tins from the oven, and carefully pour the batter into each hole.
Return the tins to the oven and leave undisturbed for 20-25 minutes until the puddings have puffed up and browned; serve immediately.
Plate everything up and finish with gravy as desired.

title: Braised Beef Chilli
instructions: Set your oven to 120C (225F, gas mark 1) to warm up.
Pull the beef out of the fridge early so it loses its chill before cooking.
Blitz the onions and garlic in a food processor until finely chopped.
Warm the olive oil in a large casserole and sear the beef on all sides until deeply golden, then set it aside.
Add a little more oil to the same pot and brown the chorizo, then remove it too.
Tip in the chopped onion and garlic along with the spices, herbs and ancho chillies, and cook gently in the chorizo oil until softened.
Season with salt and pepper, then stir in the balsamic vinegar, plum tomatoes, ketchup and brown sugar.
Return the beef and chorizo to the pot, pour in the water (or red wine), bring to a simmer, cover, and slide into the low oven.
After 2 hours, check the meat and stir in the borlotti beans.
Cook for a further hour, then just before serving, shred the beef apart with two forks.

title: Massaman Beef curry
instructions: Heat your oven to 200C (180C fan) and roast the peanuts on a tray for about 5 minutes until golden.
Let the peanuts cool, then chop them roughly.
Drop the oven down to 180C (160C fan).
Warm 2 tbsp of the coconut cream in a large lidded casserole.
Stir in the curry paste and fry for a minute.
Add the beef strips and fry until they're coated and sealed all over.
Pour in the rest of the coconut cream along with half a can of water.
Add the potatoes, onion wedges, lime leaves, cinnamon stick, tamarind paste, sugar, fish sauce and most of the chopped peanuts.
Bring everything to a simmer.
Cover and transfer to the oven, cooking for 2 hours until the beef is tender.
Scatter over the sliced chilli and the reserved peanuts.
Serve straight from the dish with jasmine rice.

title: Vietnamese Grilled Pork (bun-thit-nuong)
instructions: Slice the raw pork thin, about 1/8 inch thick — freezing it briefly first makes this easier.
Mince the garlic and shallots, then stir them into a bowl with sugar, fish sauce, thick soy sauce, pepper, and oil until the sugar dissolves.
Marinate the pork in this mixture for at least an hour, or overnight for better flavor.
Bake the pork at 375°F for 10-15 minutes until about 80% cooked, then finish under the broiler until golden brown, flipping the pieces halfway through.
Cook the rice vermicelli and assemble bowls with the noodles, cucumber, mint, peanuts, and egg rolls.
Top with the grilled pork, then either mix the whole bowl together or eat in individual bites, spooning fish sauce over as you go.

title: Grilled Mac and Cheese Sandwich
instructions: Boil a big pot of well-salted water and cook the macaroni until al dente, about 8 to 10 minutes.
Whisk together the flour, mustard powder, garlic powder, salt, black pepper, and cayenne pepper in a small bowl while the pasta cooks.
Drain the pasta, then melt the butter in the same dry pot over low heat.
Whisk in the flour mixture and cook until it turns lightly browned and smells nutty, about a minute, watching it doesn't scorch.
Slowly whisk in the milk and cream, then cook, whisking, until the sauce thickens slightly, about 2 minutes.
Take off the heat and stir in the cheese until melted, then fold in the drained pasta.
Spread the mac and cheese onto a parchment-lined baking sheet, press another buttered sheet on top, and chill until firm, about an hour.
Heat a skillet over medium-low heat for the grilled cheese.
Mix the second batch of butter with garlic powder to make garlic butter.
Cut the chilled mac and cheese slab into 8 equal pieces.
Butter one side of each bread slice with the garlic butter, then build sandwiches: bread, cheddar, a piece of mac and cheese, Colby Jack, and the second bread slice, buttered side out.
Fry the sandwiches in the skillet, covered, about 4 minutes per side until golden and the cheese inside is melted.
Repeat with the remaining sandwiches, then slice in half if you like and serve.

title: Crock Pot Chicken Baked Tacos
instructions: Place the raw chicken breasts in the crock pot.
Pour the whole bottle of vinaigrette dressing over the chicken.
Scatter the cumin, smoked paprika and garlic over the top and stir them in a little.
Cover and cook on high for 4 hours.
Lift the chicken breasts out of the crock pot and let them cool.
Shred the chicken and put it in a glass bowl.
Spoon most of the cooking liquid over the shredded chicken.
Blend the avocado with the green salsa, push it through a strainer until smooth, then load it into a squeeze bottle (snip the tip wider if needed).
Whisk the sour cream with the milk until pourable, then load it into a squeeze bottle.
In a 9x13 glass baking dish, layer refried beans, the cooked shredded chicken and shredded cheese into all 12+ taco shells.
Bake at 450°F for 10-15 minutes, until the cheese is melted and bubbling.
Straight from the oven, top the tacos with halved grape tomatoes, sliced jalapeno and cilantro.
Finish with a drizzle of the guacamole sauce and the sour cream sauce.
Serve and enjoy.

title: Chicken Karaage
instructions: Whisk together the ginger, garlic, soy sauce, sake and sugar in a bowl.
Add the chicken and stir to coat it evenly in the marinade.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
Pour 1 inch of vegetable oil into a heavy-bottomed pot and heat it to 360°F (182°C).
Line a wire rack with two sheets of paper towel and have your tongs ready.
Tip the potato starch into a bowl.
Toss a handful of marinated chicken in the potato starch until evenly coated.
Fry the karaage in batches until golden brown on the outside and cooked through, then drain on the paper towel-lined rack.
For extra-crispy karaage, let the chicken cool then fry it a second time until darker in color.
Serve hot with lemon wedges.

title: Coq au vin
instructions: Warm about a tablespoon of the olive oil in a large heavy pan or flameproof casserole.
Tip in the bacon and fry until it turns crisp, then lift it out onto kitchen paper.
Add the shallots to the same pan and fry, shaking often, for 5-8 minutes until well browned all over, then set them aside with the bacon.
Pat the chicken pieces dry, pour in the rest of the oil, and brown half the chicken pieces for 5-8 minutes, turning regularly.
Remove that batch and brown the remaining chicken the same way, then set all the chicken aside.
Scatter in the garlic and fry briefly, then turn the heat up and pour in the brandy, scraping the base of the pan to deglaze until the alcohol mostly evaporates.
Return the chicken legs and thighs to the pan with their juices, pour in a little of the wine while stirring the base, then add the rest of the wine, the stock and the tomato purée.
Drop in the thyme, rosemary and bay leaves, season with pepper and a pinch of salt, and return the bacon and shallots to the pan.
Cover, lower the heat to a gentle simmer, add the chicken breasts and cook for 50 minutes to an hour.
Just before serving, heat a little oil in a separate frying pan and fry the mushrooms over high heat until golden, then keep them warm.
Lift the chicken, shallots and bacon onto a warmed serving dish and discard the herb sprigs and bay leaves.
Mash the flour, olive oil and butter together in a small bowl to make a smooth paste for thickening.
Bring the wine sauce to a gentle boil, then whisk in small pieces of the flour-butter paste until it thickens, and simmer for 1-2 minutes.
Scatter the mushrooms over the chicken, pour the sauce over the top, and finish with a scattering of chopped parsley.

title: Salted Caramel Cheescake
instructions: Blitz the digestive biscuits and pretzels together in a food processor, then stir the crumbs into the melted butter.
Press the mixture firmly into the base of a 20cm deep springform tin and chill while you make the filling.
Whisk the cream cheese, vanilla extract and icing sugar together until smooth.
Add the caramel and whisk again until fully combined and lump-free.
Pour in the double cream and sea salt flakes, then whisk on medium speed until thick and mousse-like, being careful not to overwhip.
Spread the filling over the chilled biscuit base and refrigerate overnight to set.
Carefully unmould the cheesecake from the tin.
Decorate with a drizzle of caramel sauce, toffee popcorn and extra pretzels before serving.

title: Beef stroganoff
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a non-stick frying pan.
Add the sliced onion and cook over medium heat until completely softened, about 15 minutes, splashing in a little water if it starts to stick.
Crush in the garlic and cook for a further 2-3 minutes.
Add the butter and let it foam slightly.
Add the mushrooms and cook until completely softened, about 5 minutes.
Season everything well, then tip the mixture onto a plate.
Toss the flour with a big pinch of salt and pepper in a bowl, then coat the steak pieces in the seasoned flour.
Add the steak pieces to the pan, splashing in a little oil if the pan looks dry, and fry for 3-4 minutes until well coloured.
Return the onions and mushrooms to the pan.
Whisk together the crème fraîche, mustard and beef stock, then pour into the pan.
Cook over medium heat for about 5 minutes.
Scatter with parsley and serve with pappardelle or rice.

title: Fettucine alfredo
instructions: Warm the clotted cream, butter and cornflour together in a medium saucepan over low-ish heat, stirring until it comes to a gentle simmer, then turn off the heat and keep it warm.
While that's warming, mix the grated cheese and nutmeg in a small bowl with a good grinding of black pepper — hold off on salt for now.
Cook the fettuccine in a separate pan of well-salted boiling water (2 tsp salt) according to the pack timing, usually 3-4 minutes.
Scoop out a mugful of the pasta cooking water before draining the pasta, leaving it a little wet.
Tip the drained pasta into the cream mixture and gently fold in the cheese over low heat with a rubber spatula.
Splash in about 3 tbsp of the reserved cooking water and keep stirring — the sauce will look loose at first, then turn glossy as the water is absorbed.
Taste and adjust the seasoning, then spoon into warmed bowls.
Scatter over chopped chives or parsley and serve immediately.

title: Seafood fideuà
instructions: Boil the kettle and get your seafood ready: tip the mussels into a colander and rinse under cold water, discarding any with broken or cracked shells.
Tap each mussel shell on the sink - if it stays open, bin it; pull off any beards or stray barnacles and rinse well, then keep the mussels covered with a cold damp cloth until you need them.
Peel the body section of each prawn, leaving the heads and tails on, then score down the back and pull out the dark vein; chill until ready to cook.
Put the saffron in a small cup, cover with 50ml just-boiled water and leave to steep for 10 minutes.
If using vermicelli, crush it in a bowl into roughly 1cm lengths with your hands.
Heat the oil in a large frying pan or paella pan and cook the onion for 5 minutes until soft.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute more, then tip in the vermicelli and toast for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until golden brown.
Stir in the paprika.
Keeping the heat moderate, stir in the monkfish, squid and the saffron with its soaking water, seasoning well.
Spread everything out in an even layer, pour over the hot stock, scatter the tomatoes on top, bring to a simmer, then cover tightly and cook for 6 minutes.
Uncover and stir to bring the dry top layer of pasta into the liquid.

Push the mussels down into the pasta so they stand upright with hinges buried, arrange the prawns on top, cover again and cook for another 6 minutes until the mussels open, the prawns turn pink and the pasta is cooked through.
Let it simmer uncovered for 2-3 minutes to cook off most of the remaining liquid, leaving just a little so the pasta doesn't stick.
Rest for 2-3 minutes, then squeeze over the lemon juice, arrange the wedges on top and scatter with parsley before serving.

title: Pilchard puttanesca
instructions: Cook the spaghetti according to the pack instructions until al dente.
Meanwhile, heat the olive oil in a non-stick frying pan and gently cook the onion, garlic and chilli for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Stir in the tomato purée and cook for 1 minute.
Add the pilchards with their sauce, breaking up the fish with a wooden spoon as it cooks.
Add the black olives and continue cooking for a few more minutes.
Drain the pasta, reserving 2-3 tablespoons of the cooking water, and add both to the pan.
Toss everything together well until coated.
Divide between plates and serve scattered with shaved Parmesan.

title: Venetian Duck Ragu
instructions: Warm the olive oil in a large pan over medium-high heat.
Brown the duck legs on all sides, about 10 minutes, then lift them out onto a plate.
Tip the onions into the pan and cook for 5 minutes until softened.
Stir in the garlic and cook for 1 minute more.
Mix in the cinnamon and flour and cook for another minute, stirring constantly.
Return the duck legs to the pan and pour in the wine, tomatoes, stock, herbs, sugar and seasoning.
Bring everything to a simmer, then cover, lower the heat and cook gently for 2 hours, stirring occasionally.
Lift the duck legs out onto a plate, discard the fat, and shred the meat off the bones with two forks, discarding the bones.
Return the shredded duck to the sauce with the milk and simmer uncovered for 10-15 minutes while the pasta cooks.
Cook the paccheri according to the pack instructions, then drain, keeping a cup of the pasta water.
Add the pasta to the ragu and stir to coat, cooking for 1 minute more and loosening with a splash of pasta water if it looks dry.
Serve topped with grated Parmesan if you like.

title: Chilli prawn linguine
instructions: Whisk the fromage frais, lime zest and caster sugar together in a small bowl, then season with salt and pepper; set the dressing aside.
Cook the linguine in a large pot of boiling water according to the packet timing, tossing in the sugar snap peas for the final minute of cooking.
Meanwhile, warm the olive oil in a wok or large frying pan and sizzle the garlic and chilli gently for about 30 seconds, taking care not to let the garlic brown.
Turn the heat up, add the king prawns and stir-fry for about 3 minutes until they turn pink.
Stir in the cherry tomatoes and cook for a further 3 minutes until they just begin to soften.
Drain the pasta and sugar snap peas well, then toss them into the prawn and tomato mixture.
Tear in the basil leaves, stir everything together, and season with salt and pepper.
Serve with the lettuce leaves drizzled with the lime dressing, alongside warm crusty bread.

title: Clam chowder
instructions: Rinse the clams well in several changes of cold water and drain.
Tip them into a large pan with 500ml water, cover, bring to a boil, and simmer for 2 minutes until the shells pop open.
Drain everything through a colander set over a bowl to catch the stock, then let cool.
Pick the clam meat from the shells, saving a few empty shells for serving if you like.
Strain the stock into a jug, leaving any grit behind — you should end up with about 800ml.
Melt the butter in the same pan and fry the bacon for 3-4 minutes until it starts to brown.
Add the onion, thyme, and bay leaf, and cook gently for 10 minutes until the onion turns soft and golden.
Sprinkle in the flour, stir to a sandy paste, and cook for 2 more minutes.
Gradually stir in the clam stock, then the milk and cream.
Add the potatoes, bring to a simmer, and cook gently for 10 minutes until tender.
Crush a few potato chunks against the pan to thicken the chowder while keeping plenty of texture.
Stir in the clam meat (and reserved shells if using) and simmer for a minute to reheat.
Season generously with black pepper and a little salt if needed.
Stir through the parsley just before ladling into bowls or hollowed-out crusty rolls.

title: Creamy Tomato Soup
instructions: Warm the oil in a big pot with the onions, celery, carrots, potatoes and bay leaves.
Fry gently for 10-15 minutes until the onions soften.
Boil a kettle of water.
Stir in the tomato purée, sugar, vinegar, chopped tomatoes and passata, then crumble in the stock cubes.
Pour in 1 litre of boiling water and bring to a simmer.
Cover and simmer for 15 minutes until the potatoes are tender.
Remove the bay leaves.
Blend the soup until very smooth, using a stick blender or working in batches in a blender.
Season to taste, adding a pinch more sugar if needed.
Cool and chill for up to 2 days, or freeze for up to 3 months, if not serving right away.
To serve, reheat the soup and stir in the milk, keeping it just below a boil.
Serve in small bowls, alongside cheesy sausage rolls.

title: Broccoli & Stilton soup
instructions: Warm the rapeseed oil in a large saucepan and tip in the onion.
Cook over medium heat until softened, splashing in a little water if it starts to catch.
Add the celery, leek, potato and a knob of butter, stirring until the butter melts.
Cover with a lid and let the vegetables sweat for 5 minutes, then uncover.
Pour in the hot vegetable stock and drop in any chunky broccoli stalk pieces.
Simmer for 10-15 minutes until all the vegetables are soft.
Stir in the rest of the broccoli and cook for a further 5 minutes.
Carefully transfer the soup to a blender and blitz until smooth.
Stir in the stilton, leaving a few lumps, then season with black pepper and serve.

title: Lamb Tagine
instructions: Warm the olive oil in a heavy-based pan and toss in the onion and carrot, cooking for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add the diced lamb and brown it all over.
Stir in the garlic and all the spices, cooking for a couple of minutes until fragrant.
Add the honey and apricots, crumble in the stock cube, and pour over about 500ml boiling water or enough to just cover the meat.
Stir well, bring to the boil, then turn down to a simmer, cover with a lid, and cook for 1 hour.
Remove the lid and cook for a further 30 minutes.
Stir in the squash and cook for another 20-30 minutes until it's soft and the lamb is tender.
Serve alongside rice or couscous, sprinkled with parsley and pine nuts if you like.

title: Lasagne
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan.
Snip or chop the bacon into small pieces and add it to the pan, cooking for a few minutes until it starts to turn golden.
Add the onion, celery and carrot, and cook over medium heat for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Tip in the minced beef and cook, stirring and breaking it up, for about 6 minutes until browned all over.
Stir in the tomato purée and cook for 1 minute, mixing it in well with the beef and vegetables.
Tip in the chopped tomatoes, then fill each can half full with water to rinse out any remaining tomatoes and add that to the pan.
Add the honey, season to taste, and simmer for 20 minutes.
Heat the oven to 200C/180C fan/gas 6.
Ladle a little of the ragu into the bottom of a roasting tin or casserole dish and spread it over the base.
Lay 2 sheets of lasagne on top, overlapping to fit, then repeat with more sauce and another layer of pasta, building up to 4 layers of sauce and pasta and finishing with a layer of pasta.
Mix the crème fraîche with 2 tablespoons of water in a bowl until smooth and pourable.
Pour this over the top of the pasta, then scatter over the mozzarella and sprinkle with Parmesan.
Bake for 25–30 minutes until golden and bubbling.
Serve scattered with basil leaves, if you like.
