title: Classic Christmas pudding
instructions: Coarsely chop the almonds, then peel, core and chop the Bramley apples.
Finely chop the candied peel by hand - don't cheat with the processor here.
Grate three quarters of the whole nutmeg into a large bowl.
Add the almonds, apples, candied peel, raisins, flour, breadcrumbs, muscovado sugar, eggs and 2 tbsp brandy to the bowl and mix well.
Grate the butter into the bowl a quarter at a time, stirring well after each addition until the mixture subsides slightly - about 3-4 minutes total.
Butter two 1.2 litre pudding bowls generously and line the bottoms with a circle of baking parchment.
Pack the pudding mixture firmly into the bowls.
Cover each with a double layer of pleated baking parchment, tie down with string, and trim the excess paper.
Wrap each bowl in a double layer of foil, sealing well to make it watertight, and tie with string, leaving a handle for lifting.
Boil or oven-steam the puddings for 8 hours, topping up the water as needed.
Cool the puddings overnight, then unwrap, discard the old wrapping, and re-wrap in fresh parchment, foil and string for storage.
Store the puddings in a cool, dry place until Christmas.
For the brandy butter, cream butter with orange zest and icing sugar, then beat in brandy and chopped stem ginger, and chill to set.
On Christmas Day, boil or oven-steam the pudding for 1 hour, then unwrap and turn out.
To flame, warm 3-4 tbsp brandy, pour over the pudding, and light it at the table.

ingredients.note: assumed ~200g per large Bramley apple
'1 whole Nutmeg' estimated at ~5g typical whole nutmeg weight
3 large eggs estimated at 60g each
2 tbsp brandy converted at ~15g/tbsp
recipe just says 'Butter'; assumed unsalted for baking

title: Christmas Pudding Trifle
instructions: Peel the oranges with a sharp knife, removing all the pith.
Slice the oranges thinly and arrange them over a dinner plate.
Sprinkle the demerara sugar over the oranges.
Drizzle with Grand Marnier and set aside.
Crumble the Christmas pudding into large pieces and scatter it over the base of a trifle bowl.
Layer the oranges on top of the pudding and pour over any accumulated juices.
Beat the mascarpone until smooth.
Stir the custard into the mascarpone.
Spoon the custard mixture over the oranges.
Lightly whip the cream and spoon it over the custard layer.
Sprinkle with flaked almonds and grated chocolate.
Chill until ready to serve, or make up to a day ahead.

1 tbsp ≈ 12g
2 tbsp ≈ 15g each, estimated

title: Christmas cake
instructions: Preheat the oven to 160°C (fan 140°C, gas 3) and line the base and sides of a 20 cm round, 7.5 cm deep cake tin.
Beat the butter and sugar with an electric hand mixer for 1-2 minutes until pale and creamy, scraping down the bowl halfway through.
Stir in a spoonful of the flour, then alternate the beaten egg and remaining flour a quarter at a time, beating well with a wooden spoon after each addition.
Stir in the ground almonds.
Mix in the sherry - the batter will look curdled, that's fine.
Fold in the candied peel, glace cherries, raisins, currants, pecans, lemon zest, mixed spice, rose water and vanilla extract, beating together to combine.
Stir in the baking powder.
Spoon the mixture into the prepared tin, smooth the top and make a slight dip in the centre.
Bake for 30 minutes, then lower the oven to 150°C (fan 130°C, gas 2) and bake a further 2-2¼ hours, until a skewer inserted in the middle comes out clean.
Leave the cake to cool completely in the tin, then turn out and peel off the lining paper.
Once fully cold, wrap tightly in cling film and foil to store until ready to decorate; it will keep for several months.

ingredients.note: 4 eggs estimated at 50g each (200g total); no gram weight given in original.
100ml converted 1:1 to grams.

1½ tbsp converted at ~7g/tbsp (~11g), inferred conversion.

title: Mince Pies
instructions: Rub the cold diced butter into the plain flour until it resembles breadcrumbs, then mix in the caster sugar and a pinch of salt.
Bring the mixture together into a firm ball without adding any liquid, then knead briefly - it should feel like shortbread dough. Use straight away or chill for later.
Heat the oven to 200C/gas 6/fan 180C. Press small walnut-sized balls of pastry into 18 holes across two 12-hole patty tins.
Spoon the mincemeat into each pastry case.
Pat out slightly smaller balls of pastry into round lids big enough to cover the pies.
Top each pie with a lid, pressing the edges together gently to seal - no need for milk or egg to help them stick. At this point the pies can be frozen for up to a month.
Beat the egg and brush over the tops of the pies. Bake for 20 minutes until golden.
Leave the pies to cool in the tin for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack.
Dust lightly with icing sugar before serving.

ingredients.note: '1 small egg' inferred as ~45g
no quantity given for dusting topping; estimated 10g

title: Soy-Glazed Meatloaves with Wasabi Mashed Potatoes & Roasted Carrots
instructions: Crank the oven to 425°F and get your station ready — wash and dry all produce.

Dice the potatoes into half-inch pieces, cut the carrots on a diagonal into half-inch pieces, thinly slice the scallions (keeping the whites and greens separate, chopping the whites fine), and finely chop the garlic.
In a bowl, soak the bread in a splash of water until it turns pasty, then mash it up with your hands.
Mix in the ground beef, sriracha, scallion whites, half the garlic, salt, and pepper, then shape into two tall loaves.
Set the loaves on one side of a baking sheet, toss the carrots with oil, salt, and pepper on the other side, and roast everything for 20 minutes.
Meanwhile, boil the potatoes in salted water until very tender, about 12-15 minutes, then reserve a little of the cooking water and drain.
While the potatoes cook, stir together the soy sauce, garlic powder, ketchup, and sugar to make the glaze.

Once the meatloaves and carrots have had their 20 minutes, pull them out, brush half the glaze over the meatloaves, and return everything to the oven for 4-5 minutes until the carrots are tender and the glaze is tacky.
Meanwhile, melt the butter in the empty potato pot, add the remaining garlic, and cook until fragrant.
Add the potatoes and wasabi to the pot and mash, loosening with splashes of the reserved cooking water until smooth, then season with salt and pepper.
Plate the meatloaves with the mashed potatoes and roasted carrots, scatter over the scallion greens, and serve the extra glaze on the side for dipping.

title: Honey Balsamic Chicken with Crispy Broccoli & Potatoes
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425°F and get all the produce washed and dried.
Cut the potatoes into half-inch wedges and toss them on one side of a baking sheet with a drizzle of oil, salt, and pepper.
Roast the potatoes on the top rack for 5 minutes before adding the broccoli.
While that starts, cut the broccoli into bite-size florets and finely chop the garlic.
Combine a tablespoon of olive oil with half the garlic in a small bowl and microwave until the garlic sizzles, about 30 seconds.
Pull the sheet, add the broccoli to the empty side, and toss it with the garlic oil, salt, and pepper.
Return the sheet to the oven and roast until the potatoes and broccoli are browned and crispy, another 15-20 minutes.
Meanwhile, pat the chicken dry and season it all over with salt and pepper.
Heat a drizzle of vegetable oil in a large pan over medium-high heat, add the chicken, and cook until browned and cooked through, 5-6 minutes per side, lowering the heat if it browns too fast.
Set the chicken aside to rest and wipe out the pan.
Heat the same pan over medium-high heat, add a drizzle of oil and the remaining garlic, and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Stir in the balsamic vinegar, honey, chicken stock concentrate, and a quarter cup of water, then simmer until thick and glossy, 2-3 minutes.
Remove from heat and stir in the butter, seasoning with salt and pepper.
Return the chicken to the pan and turn to coat it in the glaze.
Divide the chicken, broccoli, and potatoes between plates, spooning any remaining glaze over the chicken.

ingredients.note: 5 potatoes, weight per potato not stated; estimated ~130g each
1 head broccoli, weight not stated; estimated typical head size
2 chicken breasts, weight not stated; estimated ~170g each typical meal-kit portion
amount not specified in text; inferred ~2 tbsp based on sauce proportions

title: Skillet Apple Pork Chops with Roasted Sweet Potatoes & Zucchini
instructions: Crank your oven to 450°F with racks in the top and middle slots.
Wash and dice the sweet potatoes into 1/2-inch cubes, then toss them on a baking sheet with a drizzle of oil, salt, and pepper.
Roast the sweet potatoes on the top rack for 12 minutes.
While those start, halve, core, and thinly slice the apple into half-moons.
Peel and finely chop the garlic, and quarter the lemon.
Trim the zucchini, halve it lengthwise, and slice into 1/2-inch half-moons; toss on a second sheet with a drizzle of oil, salt, and pepper, then set aside.
Pat the pork dry and season all over with salt and pepper.
Heat a drizzle of oil in a large pan over medium-high heat and cook the pork until browned and cooked through, about 4-5 minutes per side, then turn off the heat and move it to a plate.
Once the sweet potatoes have had their 12 minutes, add the zucchini sheet to the middle rack and roast both together for 12-15 more minutes until browned and tender.
Melt 1 tablespoon of butter in the same pan you used for the pork over medium-high heat.
Add the apple, season with salt and pepper, and cook while scraping up the browned bits from the pan until the apple softens slightly, 2-3 minutes.
Stir in the garlic and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Pour in the water, stock concentrate, and sugar, then simmer, stirring, until the sauce thickens and the apple turns very tender, 3-5 minutes; season with salt and pepper.
Off the heat, stir the remaining butter and a squeeze of lemon juice into the apple sauce.
Plate the pork, zucchini, and sweet potatoes, spoon the glazed apple sauce over the pork, and finish the zucchini with a squeeze of lemon juice.

title: BBQ Pork Sloppy Joes
instructions: Preheat the oven to 450°F and wash and dry the produce.
Cut the sweet potatoes into ½-inch wedges, toss with a drizzle of oil, salt, and pepper on a baking sheet, and roast 20-25 minutes until browned and tender.
Halve and peel the onion; thinly slice about ¼ cup for pickling and finely chop the rest.
Peel and finely chop the garlic.
Halve the lime and squeeze its juice into a small bowl.
Halve the buns; melt the butter and brush it onto the cut sides.
Add the sliced onion, sugar, and a pinch of salt to the lime juice, stir, and set aside to quick-pickle.
Heat a drizzle of oil in a large pan over medium-high heat and cook the chopped onion with salt and pepper until softened, 4-5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add the pork, season with salt and pepper, and cook, breaking it up, until browned and cooked through, 4-6 minutes.
Combine the BBQ sauce, pickling liquid, ketchup, remaining sugar, and water, then stir into the cooked pork and simmer until thickened, 2-3 minutes; taste and adjust seasoning.
Toast the buns until golden, 3-5 minutes.
Fill the toasted buns with the BBQ pork, top with pickled onion and hot sauce, and serve with the sweet potato wedges on the side.

Weight inferred: 1 medium red onion ≈150g, split between pickled slices and chopped filling.
Weight inferred: 2 cloves ≈3g each = 6g.

title: French Onion Chicken with Roasted Carrots & Mashed Potatoes
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425°F, then wash and prep the produce: cut carrots on a diagonal into 1/4-inch pieces, dice the potatoes into 1/2-inch pieces, and thinly slice the peeled onion.
Toss the carrots on a baking sheet with a drizzle of oil, salt, and pepper, then roast until browned and tender, 15-20 minutes.
Meanwhile, boil the potatoes in salted water (covering by 2 inches) until tender, 12-15 minutes, then drain and return them to the pot, covered, to keep warm.
While the potatoes cook, sauté the sliced onion in a drizzle of oil over medium-high heat until softened and lightly browned, 8-10 minutes.
Sprinkle the onion with sugar, stir in the stock concentrate and a splash of water, season with salt and pepper, and cook until jammy, 2-3 minutes more; transfer to a bowl and wipe out the pan.
Pat the chicken dry, season with salt and pepper, then sear in the same pan with a drizzle of oil until browned and cooked through, 5-6 minutes per side.
In the last 1-2 minutes, top the chicken with the caramelized onion and mozzarella, cover the pan, and let the cheese melt.
Mash the drained potatoes with sour cream, butter, salt, pepper, and a splash of water or milk until smooth.
Divide the chicken, roasted carrots, and mashed potatoes between plates.

title: Beef Banh Mi Bowls with Sriracha Mayo, Carrot & Pickled Cucumber
instructions: Rinse the rice in a fine-mesh sieve until the water runs clear.
Simmer it in 1 cup water with a pinch of salt: boil, cover, reduce heat to low, and cook 15 minutes.
Turn off the heat and keep the rice covered at least 10 minutes before serving.
Peel and finely chop the garlic; zest and quarter the lime.
Halve the cucumber lengthwise and thinly slice into half-moons.
Peel and medium-dice the onion; peel and grate the carrot.
Toss the cucumber with lime juice, a pinch of sugar, and a pinch of salt to quick-pickle it.
Stir together mayonnaise, a pinch of garlic, a squeeze of lime juice, and sriracha to taste; season with salt and pepper.
Heat a drizzle of oil in a large pan over medium-high heat and cook the onion, stirring, until softened, 4-5 minutes.
Add the beef, remaining garlic, and sugar; cook, breaking up the meat, until browned and cooked through, 4-5 minutes.
Stir in the soy sauce, then taste and season with salt and pepper.
Fluff the rice, stir in the lime zest and butter, and divide between bowls.
Top with the beef, grated carrot, and pickled cucumber, finish with a squeeze of lime juice, and drizzle with the sriracha mayo.

title: Corned Beef and Cabbage
instructions: Put the corned beef in a large pot or Dutch oven and cover it with water.
Add the spice packet that came with the corned beef.
Cover the pot, bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer.
Simmer for about 50 minutes per pound, until the meat is tender.
Add the whole potatoes and the peeled, cut carrots, and cook until the vegetables are almost tender.
Add the cabbage and cook for 15 more minutes.
Remove the meat and let it rest for 15 minutes.
Place the vegetables in a bowl and cover them.
Ladle in as much of the reserved cooking broth as you like.
Slice the meat across the grain and serve.

ingredients.note: Inferred: 10 small potatoes estimated at ~100g each; no weight given in text.
Inferred: 5 carrots estimated at ~60g each; no weight given in text.

title: Crispy Sausages and Greens
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Strip the stems from a bunch of kale and tear the leaves into rough 1-inch pieces.
Coarsely chop the cabbage.
Toss the kale and cabbage together in a large baking dish.
Scatter in the thinly sliced garlic.
If using, sauté the sliced onion and shiitake mushrooms in a splash of olive oil until softened, then stir them into the greens.
Drizzle everything with olive oil and pour in the chicken stock.
Cover the dish tightly with foil and bake until the greens wilt, about 15 minutes.
Uncover, season with salt and pepper, and keep baking until the cabbage turns tender, another 20-25 minutes.
Meanwhile, warm a little olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Prick the sausages with a fork and cook, turning, until browned all over and cooked through, 10-12 minutes.
Slice the cooked sausages and toss them through the finished greens with a splash of vinegar.

ingredients.note: No amount given for the kale bunch; used a typical bunch weight (~340 g).

8 cloves estimated at ~5 g each.
No quantity given for the optional sliced onion; assumed one medium onion (~110 g), sautéed before adding so counted as cooked.
No quantity given for the optional shiitake mushrooms; assumed ~100 g typical addition, sautéed before adding so counted as cooked.

title: Vegetable Shepherds Pie
instructions: Soak the dried porcini in hot water and set aside.
Simmer the lentils with a garlic clove, salt, and water until just tender, then drain and discard the garlic.
Heat the oil in a large pot and cook the onions until soft.
Stir in the chopped garlic, then add the tomato puree and cook until it caramelizes.
Add the bay leaves and wine, scraping up the browned bits from the pot.
Pour in the porcini and its soaking liquid, leaving the grit behind, and simmer until reduced by half.
Stir in the vegetable stock and simmer until reduced by half again.
Strain the sauce, discard the solids, and bring the liquid back to a boil.
Whisk in the cornstarch slurry and simmer until the sauce thickens.
Stir in the soy sauce and season the sauce with salt and pepper; set aside.
Preheat the oven to 450°F and roast the mixed vegetables and pearl onions tossed with oil, garlic cloves, and rosemary until tender.
Mash the roasted garlic and stir it into the sauce; discard the rosemary sprigs.
Bake the potatoes until tender, then peel and press them through a ricer into a bowl.
Stir the butter and milk into the riced potatoes and season with salt.
Spread the cooked lentils in an even layer in a baking dish.
Toss the roasted vegetables with the fresh mushrooms and chopped herbs, then layer over the lentils.
Pour the sauce evenly over the vegetables.
Spoon the mashed potato topping evenly over everything.
Bake until browned and bubbling, then let stand before serving.

ingredients.note: Two potato lines (regular + small) both 3 lbs; the topping method only describes riced potatoes once, so both are assumed combined for the mash — split unclear.
Cooked separately and layered into the dish already tender per instructions.
No quantity given; assumed 2 leaves, used to flavor the sauce and later strained out/discarded.
Quantity inferred by summing '3 Tbsp' (sautéing onions) + 'remaining 2 Tbsp' (tossing roast vegetables) from the instructions; both uses stay in the served dish, so not a frying medium.
Instructions call for 'miso' at this step but the ingredient list specifies soy sauce; used soy sauce as listed, flagging the mismatch.
Used to flavor the roasting vegetables, then discarded per instructions.
No quantity given in ingredient list; inferred ~2 Tbsp chopped per the 'chopped herbs' step.

title: Strawberry Rhubarb Pie
instructions: Pulse the flour, salt and sugar in a food processor until combined.
Add the butter and pulse until the mixture looks like coarse meal, about 15 seconds.
Drizzle in the water through the feed tube in a slow stream until the dough just holds together when pinched, adding a touch more water if needed, but never process past 30 seconds.
Turn the dough out, gather into a ball, divide in half, flatten each half into a disk, wrap and chill for about an hour.
Roll one disk into a 12-inch circle on a floured surface, turning it a quarter turn as you go so it doesn't stick.
Fold the round in half, lift it into a 9-inch pie pan, brush off excess flour and trim the overhang to about half an inch, then chill covered while you make the filling.
Roll the second disk into a 13-inch circle and cut it into roughly 3/4-inch strips with a pastry wheel.
Lay the strips on a parchment-lined sheet, cover and chill for about 10 minutes.
Toss the cut strawberries and rhubarb together in a large bowl.
Stir the cornstarch, sugar and cinnamon together in a small bowl.
Sprinkle about 2 tablespoons of the sugar mixture over the bottom of the chilled pie shell.
Toss the remaining sugar mixture through the strawberries and rhubarb, then pour the fruit into the pie shell.
Sprinkle the fruit with the lemon juice and dot with the unsalted butter.
Weave the pastry strips into a lattice over the filling, working from the center outward and folding alternating strips back to cross them, trimming to a 1-inch overhang.
Tuck the strip ends under the bottom crust, seal and flute the edges.
Brush the lattice with milk and scatter over a little sugar.
Chill the assembled pie while the oven heats to 400°F (205°C) with a foil-lined baking sheet on the lower rack to catch drips.
Set the pie on the hot baking sheet and bake for about 35 minutes, shielding the edges with a foil ring if they brown too fast.
Keep baking for another 10 minutes or so, until the crust is deep golden and the juices bubble.
Cool the pie on a wire rack for several hours before slicing.
Serve at room temperature with whipped cream or vanilla ice cream.

ingredients.note: 1 tsp salt approximated as 6 g
2 tbsp sugar approximated as 25 g
1 cup butter approximated as 227 g
1/2 cup water approximated as 120 g
3 tbsp cornstarch approximated as 24 g
1/4 tsp cinnamon approximated as 0.6 g
1 tsp lemon juice approximated as 5 g
2 tbsp unsalted butter approximated as 28 g
2 tbsp milk approximated as 30 g
amount not specified in original ('Sprinkling Sugar'); inferred small garnish amount

title: Moussaka
instructions: Heat your grill to high.
Brown the beef in a deep ovenproof frying pan over high heat for about 5 minutes.
While the beef browns, prick the aubergine all over with a fork and microwave on high for 3-5 minutes until soft.
Whisk together the yogurt, egg and parmesan, then season lightly.
Stir the tomatoes, tomato puree and potatoes into the browned beef, season, and heat through.
Smooth the beef mixture flat in the pan with the back of a spoon.
Slice the cooked aubergine and arrange it over the beef.
Pour the yogurt mixture over the aubergine and smooth it out evenly.
Grill until the topping has set and turned golden.

ingredients.note: grams inferred: '1 large Aubergine' with no weight given, estimated ~400g for a large aubergine
grams inferred: '1 beaten Egg' converted to standard egg weight ~50g
grams inferred: '3 tbs Parmesan' converted at ~7g per tbsp grated
assumed canned/chopped tomatoes given standard 400g tin quantity
grams inferred: '4 tbs Tomato Puree' converted at ~15g per tbsp

title: Honey Yogurt Cheesecake
instructions: Heat your oven to 160°C (140°C fan, gas 3).
Crush the digestive biscuits and most of the almonds together in a bag with a rolling pin.
Mix the crumbs with the butter and press into the base of a deep, oval 23cm dish.
Bake the base for 10 minutes until crisp.
Stir or mash the yogurt and mascarpone together, then whisk in the eggs one at a time.
Fold in the lemon and orange zest, then stir in most of the honey, keeping about 3 tbsp aside.
Spread the filling over the biscuit base.
Cover loosely with foil and bake for 1 hour.
Uncover and bake a further 15 minutes until lightly golden with just a slight wobble in the middle.
Leave to cool completely, then chill — it keeps in the fridge for up to 2 days.
To serve, scatter over the reserved almonds, drizzle with the remaining honey, and hand around fresh fruit.

ingredients.note: most used crushed into the crust, remainder reserved for topping; total kept as one line, not split
250ml converted to grams using yogurt density (~1.03g/ml)
assumed ~50g per egg, no weight given
amount inferred; zest of 1 lemon approximated at ~6g
amount inferred; zest of 1 orange approximated at ~8g
250ml converted to grams using honey density (~1.36g/ml); most goes into filling, ~3 tbsp reserved for drizzling

title: Stuffed Lamb Tomatoes
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C (160°C fan, gas 4).
Slice the tops off the tomatoes and set the lids aside.
Scoop out most of the pulp with a teaspoon, keeping the skins intact.
Finely chop the pulp and save any juices.
Sprinkle the inside of each tomato with a pinch of sugar to cut the acidity, then arrange them on a baking tray.
Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large frying pan and gently cook the onion and garlic for about 10 minutes until soft but not coloured.
Add the lamb, cinnamon and tomato purée, turn up the heat and fry until the meat is browned.
Stir in the chopped tomato pulp and juice, the rice and the stock, then season generously.
Bring to the boil and simmer for 15 minutes, or until the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed.
Let the filling cool slightly, then stir through the dill, parsley and mint.
Pack the tomatoes with the filling right up to the brim and top with their lids.
Drizzle with the remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil, sprinkle 3 tablespoons of water into the tray, and bake for 35 minutes.
Serve hot or cold with salad and crusty bread.

ingredients.note: no explicit weight given; assumed ~150g per large tomato x4
pinch, approximated as 1g
4 tbsp total (2 tbsp for sautéing aromatics/meat that stays in the dish, 2 tbsp drizzled before baking); both remain in served food, so not a frying medium

title: Lamb and Lemon Souvlaki
instructions: Pound the garlic with sea salt in a mortar until it turns into a paste.
Whisk the olive oil, lemon juice, lemon zest and dill together with the garlic paste.
Toss the lamb through the marinade until well coated.
Cover and let it marinate in the fridge for at least 2 hours, or overnight for best flavor.
If using bamboo skewers, soak them in cold water before threading.
Let refrigerated lamb sit out for 30 minutes before cooking.
Thread the marinated lamb onto the skewers.
Heat a grill, griddle pan or barbecue until very hot.
Cook the skewers 2-3 minutes per side, basting with the leftover marinade as they cook.
Warm the pita or flatbreads briefly.
Stuff the warmed bread with the cooked souvlaki.
Serve with Greek salad and tzatziki if you like.

ingredients.note: 2 cloves estimated at 3g each
2 tsp estimated at 6g each
4 tbsp estimated at 13.5g each; part of marinade, stays in dish
juice of 1 lemon estimated at ~45g; zest quantity not separately measured, included as flavoring only
1 tbsp fresh dill estimated at ~3g
no yield stated in recipe; servings inferred as 4 based on 750g lamb leg (~190g cooked meat per serving), a plausible souvlaki portion
amount not specified ('To serve'); inferred 4 pieces at ~60g each to match 4 servings

title: Lamb Tzatziki Burgers
instructions: Tip the bulgur into a pan, cover with water and simmer for 10 minutes.
Drain the bulgur really well in a sieve, pressing out any excess water.
For the tzatziki, squeeze the grated cucumber to remove excess juice and discard it, then stir the cucumber into the yogurt with the chopped mint and a pinch of salt.
Work the drained bulgur into the lamb mince along with the cumin, coriander, paprika, garlic and some seasoning.
Shape the mixture into 4 burgers.
Brush the burgers lightly with olive oil and fry or barbecue for about 5 minutes on each side until cooked all the way through.
Serve the burgers in the buns, toasted if you like, topped with the tzatziki, tomatoes, onion and a few mint leaves.

ingredients.note: boiled and drained before being worked into the raw lamb mixture

1 tsp converted to grams; assumed ground coriander

2 tbsp chopped mint converted to grams

title: Chicken Quinoa Greek Salad
instructions: Cook the quinoa according to the packet instructions, then rinse it under cold water and drain thoroughly.
Mash the butter, chilli and garlic together into a spicy paste.
Toss the chicken breasts in a little of the olive oil with some seasoning.
Lay the chicken in a hot griddle pan and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side until cooked through.
Transfer the chicken to a plate, dot with the spicy butter and let it melt over the top.
Tip the tomatoes, olives, onion, feta and mint into a large bowl.
Fold the cooked quinoa through the salad mixture.
Stir in the remaining olive oil, lemon juice and zest, and season well.
Serve the chicken fillets on top of the salad, drizzled with any buttery juices left on the plate.

ingredients.note: estimated 10g for 1 chopped chilli; no weight given in original

title: Gigantes Plaki
instructions: Soak the butter beans overnight in plenty of water.
Drain and rinse the beans, then place them in a pan, cover with fresh water, bring to the boil, reduce the heat, and simmer for about 50 minutes until slightly tender but not soft.
Drain the beans and set aside.
Preheat the oven to 180°C (160°C fan / gas 4).
Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan, add the chopped onion and garlic, and cook over medium heat for 10 minutes until softened but not browned.
Stir in the tomato puree and cook for a further minute.
Add the tomatoes, sugar, oregano and cinnamon, then simmer for 2-3 minutes.
Season generously, then stir in the cooked beans.
Tip everything into a large ovenproof dish and bake, uncovered, for about 1 hour without stirring, until the beans are tender and the sauce has thickened.
Let the dish cool, then scatter with chopped parsley and drizzle with a little extra olive oil before serving.

ingredients.note: beans are boiled ~50 min before being added to the sauce, so counted as cooked; 400g is the stated dry weight.
oil is sautéed with onion/garlic and remains in the baked dish; converted 3 tbsp at ~13.5g/tbsp.

converted 1 tbsp at ~12g.

title: Big Mac
instructions: Whisk mayonnaise, white wine vinegar, mustard, onion salt, garlic powder, paprika and a pinch of pepper together for the Big Mac sauce; season with salt and chill until ready to use.
Season the minced beef with salt and pepper, then shape into four balls and flatten each between sheets of baking paper into 15cm patties.
Heat the oil in a large frypan over high heat and sear the patties in two batches, 1-2 minutes per side, until lightly charred and cooked through; keep warm.
Slice each sesame seed bun into three layers and toast lightly.

Build each burger: spread sauce on the base bun, add chopped onion, shredded lettuce, a cheese slice, a beef patty and pickle slices, repeat on the middle bun layer with more sauce, onion, lettuce, pickles and patty, then top with the lid.
Let the burger rest for half an hour before eating, then go for a jog.

weight per bun inferred at ~70g since none given
quantity not specified; inferred small garnish amount for two burgers

title: Pizza Express Margherita
instructions: Preheat your oven to 230°C.
Stir the sugar into warm water and crumble in the fresh yeast.
Let the yeast mixture stand in a warm spot for 10–15 minutes until frothy on top.
Sift the flour and salt into a large bowl and make a well in the centre.
Pour the frothy yeast mixture and the olive oil into the well.
Flour your hands and mix everything together slowly until it binds into a dough.
Dust your work surface generously with flour.
Turn the dough out and knead for 10 minutes until smooth, silky and soft.
Place the dough in a lightly oiled non-stick baking tray, shaping it as you like.
Spread the passata over the dough right to the edges.
Scatter the mozzarella evenly over the passata.
Season with oregano and black pepper, then drizzle with a little olive oil.
Bake for 10–12 minutes until the cheese just starts to colour.
Finish with a fresh basil leaf on top and serve straight away.

ingredients.note: converted from '1 tsp' using standard teaspoon weight (~4g)
converted from '1 1/2 tsp' using standard teaspoon weight (~6g/tsp)
amount not specified for 'drizzle'; estimated 10g, oil is drizzled onto the served pizza so counted whole
quantity not given ('Peeled and Sliced Oregano' appears to be a listing error); estimated small seasoning amount

title: Krispy Kreme Donut
instructions: Dissolve the yeast in the warm water in a large bowl.
Add the milk, sugar, salt, eggs, shortening and 2 cups of the flour, then beat on low for 30 seconds, scraping the bowl constantly.
Beat on medium speed for 2 minutes, scraping the bowl occasionally.
Stir in the remaining flour until the dough is smooth.
Cover and let the dough rise until doubled, about 50-60 minutes, until a poked indentation stays.
Turn the dough onto a floured surface and roll it around lightly to coat with flour.
Gently roll the dough to about 1/2 inch thick with a floured rolling pin.
Cut out rounds with a floured doughnut cutter.
Cover and let the cut doughnuts rise until doubled, about 30-40 minutes.
Heat the canola oil in a deep fryer to 350°F.
Slide the doughnuts into the hot oil with a wide spatula.
Turn the doughnuts as they rise to the surface and fry until golden brown, about a minute per side.
Lift the doughnuts carefully from the oil without piercing them, then drain.
Dip the warm doughnuts into the glaze set on a rack.
For the glaze, melt the butter, then remove from the heat.
Stir in the powdered sugar and vanilla until smooth.
Stir in the boiling water a tablespoon at a time until the glaze reaches the consistency you want.

ingredients.note: Instructions only mention 'milk' being added after the yeast step; taking this line's stated 'Water' literally though it may actually be milk.

Original line read '5 drops Flour', clearly a unit error; inferred 5 cups (~600g) based on the standard yield for this dough and used to estimate a 12-donut servings count since no yield is stated in the text.
No amount given ('Sprinkling'); inferred a typical deep-fryer oil volume. Counted as frying medium since it is drained off, not eaten with the doughnuts.
Line reads 'Sugar' but the glaze instructions specify powdered sugar; mapped accordingly.
Original amount '1 1/2 cups Vanilla' is implausible for a glaze; corrected to a plausible 1 1/2 teaspoons (~6g) based on standard glaze proportions.

title: Chick-Fil-A Sandwich
instructions: Wrap the chicken breast in plastic wrap and pound it gently with the flat side of a tenderizer until it's an even 1/2 inch thick.
Slice the flattened breast into two roughly equal pieces.
Let the chicken marinate in the pickle juice for 30 minutes to an hour — stir in a teaspoon of Tabasco here if you want it spicy.
Beat the egg together with the milk in one bowl.
In a second bowl, mix the flour, sugar, and all the spices.
Dip each chicken piece into the egg wash, then dredge it in the seasoned flour on both sides.
Heat the oil in a skillet to about 1/2 inch deep, bringing it to 345-350°F.
Fry each cutlet for 2 minutes per side, until golden and cooked all the way through.
Blot the cutlets on paper towel and stack onto a toasted bun with pickle slices to serve.

ingredients.note: '1 chicken breast' — assumed standard ~170 g breast, weight inferred.
1/4 cup converted to grams.
1/4 cup converted to grams.
1/2 cup converted to grams.
1 tbsp converted to grams.

title: Paszteciki (Polish Pasties)
instructions: Sift the flour and salt into a large bowl.
Push the egg yolk through a fine sieve into the flour, then mix in the whole egg.
Beat in the butter a tablespoon at a time until combined.
Knead the dough on a floured surface until smooth and elastic, then wrap in wax paper and chill for at least 30 minutes.
Melt 2 tablespoons of the butter in a heavy skillet and sauté the onion and swede until the onion turns soft and translucent, about 5 minutes.
Grind the onion, swede and beef together twice, or chop them very finely by hand.
Melt the remaining butter in the skillet and add the ground meat mixture.
Cook over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the liquid evaporates and the mixture holds its shape.
Cool the filling, then stir in the remaining egg and season with salt and pepper.
Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
Roll the chilled dough into a 13x8 inch rectangle about 1/8 inch thick.
Spoon the filling lengthwise down the center, leaving an inch of space at each end.
Brush the long edges with cold water, then fold one side over the filling and the other side on top.
Brush the short ends with water and fold them in to seal the parcel.
Place the pastry seam-side down on a baking sheet and brush the top with the beaten egg.
Bake until deep golden brown, about 30 minutes.
Slice the pastry diagonally into 1.5 inch pieces and serve as an appetizer or alongside soup.

ingredients.note: used in the dough itself, not a frying medium
used to sauté onion/swede then cook the meat filling; stays in the served filling so not counted as frying medium
sautéed then ground into the cooked filling
sautéed then ground into the cooked filling

title: Bigos (Hunters Stew)
instructions: Preheat your oven to 175°C (350°F).
Warm a large pot over medium heat and fry the bacon with the kielbasa until the bacon renders its fat and the sausage turns lightly golden.
Lift the meat out with a slotted spoon and set it into a large casserole or Dutch oven.
Toss the pork cubes lightly in flour, then brown them in the bacon drippings over medium-high heat until golden.
Transfer the pork to the casserole with a slotted spoon.
In the same pot, add the garlic, onion, carrots, mushrooms, cabbage and sauerkraut.
Cook over medium heat, stirring, for about 10 minutes until the carrots soften, keeping the vegetables from browning.
Pour in the red wine to deglaze, scraping up the browned bits and flour stuck to the bottom of the pan.
Stir in the bay leaf, basil, marjoram, paprika, salt, pepper, caraway seeds and cayenne pepper, and cook for a minute more.
Mix in the dried mushrooms, hot sauce, Worcestershire sauce, beef stock, tomato paste and tomatoes, then bring to a boil.
Pour the vegetable mixture and all its liquid over the meat in the casserole and cover with a lid.
Bake in the preheated oven for 2½ to 3 hours, until the meat is very tender.

title: Pierogi (Polish Dumplings)
instructions: Melt the butter in a skillet over medium heat.
Stir in the chopped onion for the sauerkraut filling and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the drained sauerkraut and cook for another 5 minutes.
Season the sauerkraut filling with salt and pepper, then set aside on a plate to cool.
Melt more butter in a skillet over medium heat.
Stir in the chopped onion for the potato filling and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Fold the cooked onion into the mashed potatoes and season with salt and white pepper.
Beat the eggs and sour cream together until smooth.
Sift the flour, salt, and baking powder together, then stir into the egg mixture until a dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface until firm and smooth.
Divide the dough in half and roll one half out to about 1/8 inch thick.
Cut rounds from the dough using a 3-inch biscuit cutter.
Spoon a little mashed potato filling into the center of each round.
Moisten the edges with water, fold over, and crimp with a fork to seal.
Repeat with the remaining dough, filling those with the sauerkraut mixture.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil.
Drop the pierogi in and cook for 3 to 5 minutes until they float to the top.
Lift the pierogi out with a slotted spoon and serve.

ingredients.note: assumed already-cooked/mashed potatoes per instructions ('stir into the mashed potatoes')
amount given as '1 pot' with no standard size stated; assumed a typical 200g tub

title: Rosol (Polish Chicken Soup)
instructions: Place the chopped chicken legs in a large Dutch oven or stockpot.
Cover the chicken completely with water.
Bring to a boil, then lower the heat and simmer for 2 to 2 1/2 hours, skimming off any foam to keep the broth clear.
If your pot has room, add the vegetables and spices for the last hour of simmering.
If the pot's too small, cook the chicken alone for the full time, then simmer the vegetables and spices separately in another pot.
Strain everything out of the finished broth.
Pick the chicken meat off the bones, pulling it into large chunks.
Slice the cooked carrots.
Return the chicken and sliced carrots to the strained broth.
Cook the noodles separately according to package instructions, if using.
Ladle noodles into bowls and top with the hot soup.

ingredients.note: weight inferred: 4 legs at ~150g each, no gram amount given
weight inferred: 2 large onions at ~150g each
weight inferred: 5 carrots at ~60g each
weight inferred: 1 leek at ~150g

title: Golabki (cabbage roll)
instructions: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil.
Add the whole cabbage head, cover the pot, and cook for about 3 minutes until the leaves soften.
Remove the cabbage and let it cool for about 10 minutes until the leaves are easy to handle.
Peel off 18 whole leaves, trimming out the thick center ribs, and set them aside.
Chop the remaining cabbage and spread it over the bottom of a casserole dish.
Melt the butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Cook the onion in the butter until tender, 5 to 10 minutes, then let it cool.
Mix the cooled onion with the beef, pork, rice, garlic, salt, and pepper in a large bowl.
Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
Spoon about 1/2 cup of the meat mixture onto each cabbage leaf and roll it up, tucking in the sides to enclose the filling.
Repeat with the remaining leaves and filling.
Arrange the cabbage rolls on top of the chopped cabbage in the casserole dish, seasoning with salt and pepper.
Whisk together the tomato soup, tomato juice, and ketchup, then pour over the rolls.
Cover the dish with foil.
Bake for about 1 hour, until the cabbage is tender and the filling is cooked through.

ingredients.note: 1 whole cabbage head, no weight given; estimated ~900g typical medium head
2 tbsp ≈14g each; butter and cooked onion remain in the filling, not drained off

title: Polskie Nalesniki (Polish Pancakes)
instructions: Whisk the flour, eggs, milk, water, and salt together in a large bowl until you have a smooth, lump-free batter.
Stir in the butter (or set it aside to grease the pan between pancakes instead).
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat, pour in a portion of batter, and swirl to spread it thin.
Once the edges pull away from the pan and the top looks set, flip and cook briefly on the other side.
Transfer to a plate and repeat with the remaining batter.
Serve warm with your favourite filling.

ingredients.note: pinch estimated as 1g
instructions allow using this butter either mixed into batter or to grease the pan for frying; treated as batter enrichment since it remains in/on the served pancake, not drained off

title: Sledz w Oleju (Polish Herrings)
instructions: Soak the herring in cold water for at least an hour, changing the water and repeating if the fish tastes very salty.
Drain the herring well, then slice it into bite-size pieces.
Pack the pieces into a jar big enough to hold them, then cover with the oil, allspice, peppercorns, and bay leaf, and seal the jar.
Refrigerate for 2 to 3 days before eating; it keeps in the fridge up to 2 weeks.
Serve topped with chopped onion or onion slices, lemon, and parsley or dill.

ingredients.note: no weight given for 8 herring; assumed ~40 g per fillet
1 sliced onion assumed ~110 g
1/2 cup olive oil converted to grams; oil is served with the fish, not a frying medium
1 tsp allspice converted to grams
1 tsp pepper assumed whole peppercorns
1 bay leaf, standard small weight assumed

title: Rogaliki (Polish Croissant Cookies)
instructions: Whip the egg yolks, cream cheese and baking powder together with a hand mixer until combined.
Gradually work in the flour until the mixture turns crumbly like wood chips, then switch to kneading by hand.
Shape the dough into a roll, wrap in foil and freeze for 15 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 375°F while the dough chills.
Divide the dough in two, roll each half out and cut into 3-inch triangles.
Spoon a little jam onto the centre of each triangle and roll into a croissant shape.
Arrange the croissants on a greased baking sheet and bake for 10-12 minutes until golden.
Repeat with the remaining dough.
Let the cookies cool, then dust with powdered sugar before serving.

ingredients.note: 1 cup butter ≈ 227 g (standard conversion)
3 egg yolks ≈ 18 g each, estimated
8 oz cream cheese = 227 g
1 tsp baking powder ≈ 4 g
3 cups flour ≈ 120 g per cup, estimated
'1 jar' jam quantity not specified; estimated a standard 340 g jar as filling

title: Ful Medames
instructions: Cook the drained beans in fresh unsalted water in a large lidded saucepan until tender, topping up water as needed to keep them covered.
Only salt once the beans have softened, and keep them at a gentle simmer for 2 to 2 1/2 hours total.
Once the beans are soft, let the liquid reduce down to a thicker consistency.
Scoop out a ladle or two of beans, mash them with a bit of the cooking liquid, then stir the mash back into the pot to thicken the sauce.
Serve the beans hot in soup bowls, sprinkled with chopped parsley, alongside Arab bread.
Set out the dressing components separately - olive oil, quartered lemons, salt, pepper, crushed garlic, chili flakes, and ground cumin - so everyone can dress their own bowl.
Let diners gently crush the beans with a fork so they soak up the dressing.
Optionally garnish with peeled hard-boiled eggs cut into the bowl, a chopped cucumber-and-tomato salad, and thinly sliced onions or scallions.
Alternatively serve with tahina cream sauce or a side salad, plus pickles and onions soaked in vinegar for 30 minutes.
For a variation, smother the beans in a garlicky tomato sauce, or serve Syrian/Lebanese-style with yogurt, feta, olives, and small cucumbers.
To thicken the sauce the traditional way, add a handful of red lentils to the cooking water at the start.

ingredients.note: 2 cups dried broad beans estimated at ~170g/cup; simmered raw until tender per instructions, so not marked cooked.
1/3 cup chopped parsley estimated at ~40g/cup.
"Dash" is not a measurable quantity; estimated a small ~5g pour for the dressing, served as-is (not a frying medium).
3 lemons estimated at ~100g each.
4 cloves estimated at ~5g each.
"Sprinkling" is not a measurable quantity; estimated a small ~2g garnish amount.

title: Tamiya
instructions: Cover the broad beans with water and let them soak overnight.
Drain the beans; if you can't find skinless ones, rub them between your hands to loosen the skins, then pick out and discard the skins, and pat everything dry with a towel.
Grind the beans in a food mill or meat grinder — or pulse them in a food processor just until they form a paste, not a smooth puree, or the patties will fall apart when fried.
Mix in the scallions, garlic, cilantro, cumin, baking powder, cayenne, salt, pepper, and coriander if using, then refrigerate the mixture for at least 30 minutes.
Shape the chilled mixture into 1-inch balls, flatten them slightly, and dust with flour.
Heat at least 1½ inches of oil in a pan over medium heat until it reaches 365°F.
Fry the patties in batches, turning once, until golden brown all over, about 5 minutes, then lift out with a wire skimmer or slotted spoon.
Serve as part of a meze spread, or tucked into pita with tomato-cucumber salad and tahina sauce.

ingredients.note: 3 cups dried broad beans estimated at ~200g/cup
6 scallions estimated at ~15g each
4 cloves estimated at ~4g each
1/4 cup chopped parsley estimated at ~15g; instructions mention cilantro, ingredient list says parsley
amount not quantified ('sprinkling'); estimated small coating amount
amount not quantified ('as required'); estimated for 1.5in deep-fry depth, most is drained off and not eaten

title: Koshari
instructions: Boil the brown lentils in 4 cups of water until just tender, about 15-17 minutes, keeping them slightly firm so they finish cooking with the rice.
Drain the lentils and season lightly with salt.
Drain the soaked rice and combine it with the par-cooked lentils in the same pot with a little oil, salt, pepper, and coriander over medium-high heat, stirring for 3 minutes.
Add warm water to cover the rice and lentils by about 1½ inches, bring to a boil, then cover and simmer until the liquid is absorbed and both are tender, about 20 minutes.
Let the rice and lentils rest covered and undisturbed for 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook the macaroni in boiling salted water with a splash of oil until al dente, then drain.
Warm the canned chickpeas briefly in the microwave just before serving.
For the crispy onion topping, salt the onion rings, toss them in flour, and shake off any excess.
Fry the onions in hot oil over medium-high heat, stirring often, until deeply caramelized and crisp but not burnt, about 15-20 minutes.

ingredients.note: 1 1/2 cups converted at ~200g/cup dry lentils; no yield stated in recipe, servings (6) inferred from total food mass of the dish
1 1/2 cups converted at ~200g/cup dry rice
1/2 tsp ground coriander estimated at ~1g
2 cups dry macaroni estimated at ~100g/cup
standard can (~400g); canned chickpeas are already cooked, only warmed before serving
1 large onion estimated at ~150g
sprinkling of salt estimated at ~2g
1/2 cup oil (~120g) used to shallow-fry the onions until crisp; acts as a frying medium and largely drains off rather than being served with the dish

title: Shawarma
instructions: Whisk together the marinade spices, lemon juice and olive oil in a large bowl or ziplock bag.
Add the chicken thighs and massage the marinade into every piece until well coated.
Cover and marinate in the fridge overnight, up to 24 hours.
Stir together the yoghurt, garlic and cumin for the sauce, then cover and chill until needed (keeps up to 3 days).
Heat a grill, BBQ, or heavy pan over medium-high heat; the marinade's oil should be enough to stop sticking.
Grill the chicken for 4-5 minutes on the first side until nicely charred, then flip and cook 3-4 minutes more on the second side.
Lift the chicken off the heat, loosely tent with foil, and let it rest for 5 minutes.
Slice the chicken and arrange on a platter with the flatbreads, salad and yoghurt sauce.
To build a wrap, smear flatbread with yoghurt sauce, add lettuce, tomato and sliced chicken, then roll up and serve.

ingredients.note: Marinade oil stays on the chicken and is eaten, not used as a frying medium.
Quantity given only as 'splash'; grams estimated.
No quantity given for 'sliced lettuce' topping; estimated typical amount.
No quantity given for 'sliced tomato' topping; estimated typical amount.
No stated yield in the recipe text; servings inferred as 6, one flatbread per person, based on the 6 pita breads listed (60g each assumed).

title: Mulukhiyah
instructions: Sauté the chopped onions in the olive oil until soft.
Add the beef cubes and sear them for 3-4 minutes on each side.
Pour in about 1 liter of water, just enough to cover the meat.
Simmer over medium heat until the meat is tender (or pressure-cook it for about 5 minutes).
Stir in the frozen mulukhiyah and let it thaw completely, then bring the pot to a boil.
In a separate pan, warm the remaining olive oil with the garlic cloves over medium-low heat until fragrant, taking care not to let the garlic brown.
Pour the garlic oil into the mulukhiyah, lower the heat, and simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Season with salt to taste.
Serve with a generous squeeze of lemon juice, alongside rice or pita bread.

ingredients.note: No yield stated in recipe; servings inferred as 4 based on total food mass (~2.4kg) divided into typical stew portions (~590g/serving).
Weight estimated for '1 chopped onion' (no gram amount given).
Estimated gram weight for '1 tsp salt'.
Estimated gram weight for '1 garlic clove'.
Cup-to-gram conversion approximate (1/2 cup ≈ 110g); oil is stirred into the finished stew and eaten, not drained, so treated as non-frying-medium.

title: Feteer Meshaltet
instructions: Whisk the flour and salt together, then pour in about a cup of the water and start kneading.
Add the remaining water gradually until the dough turns smooth and elastic enough to stretch without tearing.
Let the dough rest for 10 minutes, then divide it into 6-8 equal balls depending on how big you want each feteer.
Warm the butter (or ghee or oil) and pour it into a deep bowl.
Submerge the dough balls in the warm butter and let them rest for 15-20 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 550°F.
Stretch the first ball out on a clean counter until it's paper-thin, thin enough to see the counter through it.
Fold the dough over itself into a square, brushing with the butter mixture between each fold.
Set it aside and stretch the next ball the same way.
Place the first folded square on the freshly stretched dough seam-side down, then fold the outer dough over it, brushing with more butter as you go.
Repeat with the third and fourth balls the same way, building up the layers.
Lay the finished stack seam-side down in a 10-inch baking dish and brush the top with more butter.
Repeat the whole process with the remaining four balls to build a second feteer.
Press the folded dough gently with your hands to spread it across the dish.
Bake for 10 minutes, then switch on the broiler once it starts puffing to brown the top.
Once done, brush a little more butter on top and cover so it stays soft.

ingredients.note: 4 cups converted using ~125g/cup standard weight
1 1/2 cups converted using ~236g/cup
1/4 tsp converted using ~6g/tsp
1 cup converted using ~227g/cup; butter is folded into and brushed on the dough, remains in the served pastry
1/4 cup converted using ~216g/cup; used interchangeably with butter for brushing, stays in the dish

title: Egyptian Fatteh
instructions: Rip the pita bread into bite-size pieces.
Fry the bread pieces in about a quarter stick of butter until golden and crisp, then tip into a glass baking dish and set aside.
In the same pan, melt a little more butter with crushed garlic, salt and cumin and stir until fragrant.
Toss the fried bread back into this garlicky butter to coat, then return it to the baking dish.
Brown the beef in butter in a pot.
Add the quartered onion, salt, pepper, the chicken bouillon cube and enough water to cover the meat.
Bring to a boil, then cover and simmer for about 2 hours until the beef is tender.
Once the meat has cooled, lift out the chunks and set aside, reserving the broth separately.
In another pot, melt butter and toast a handful of fideo noodles until golden but not dark.
Stir in the rice until some of the grains turn opaque white.
Add water and salt to taste, bring to a boil, then cover and simmer until tender, checking doneness around 35 minutes.
Ladle some of the reserved meat broth over the bread in the baking dish to soak it through.
Spoon the cooked rice on top of the bread, then add more broth until it just reaches the top of the rice.
For the red sauce, warm a little oil or butter with crushed tomato, tomato paste, salt, pepper, crushed garlic and cumin.
Stir in the vinegar and cook until fragrant and slightly thickened, thinning with a splash of water if it's too thick.
Spread the sauce over the rice.
Fry the reserved meat in a little butter or oil with a touch of tomato paste, crushed garlic, salt, pepper and cumin until golden.
Spoon the fried meat over the top and serve.

ingredients.note: No servings stated in the recipe text; inferred 4 servings from total dish mass (beef + rice + bread + sauce ≈1.7kg) giving ~425g per serving, a plausible main-dish portion.
No weight given for '1 Onion'; estimated 150g as a typical medium onion.

Estimated 5g per clove for '4 Garlic Clove'.
Estimated 16g per tablespoon of tomato puree.

Instructions specify 'shareya (fideo noodles)'; estimated 30g for 1/4 cup.

Estimated standard pita bread weight of ~60g; not specified in recipe.

title: Tonkatsu pork
instructions: Trim away the large edge of fat from each pork loin, then bash the meat between two sheets of baking parchment with a rolling pin or meat tenderiser until about 1cm thick.
Use your hands to reshape each loin back to its original size and thickness so it stays succulent.
Set up three wide bowls: one with flour, one with beaten eggs, one with panko breadcrumbs.
Season the pork, then coat each piece first in flour, then egg, then breadcrumbs.
Pour oil into a large frying or sauté pan to a depth of about 2cm and heat to 180C, testing with a pinch of panko if you have no thermometer.
Fry two pork chops at a time for 1 minute 30 seconds per side, then lift onto a wire rack to rest for 5 minutes while you cook the rest.
Whisk together the ketchup, Worcestershire sauce, oyster sauce and caster sugar for the tonkatsu sauce, loosening with a splash of water if it's too thick.
Slice the rested pork and serve drizzled with the sauce.

ingredients.note: '4 Pork Chops' with no stated weight; assumed ~150g per chop, 4 chops for 4 servings.
2 beaten eggs assumed ~50g each.
Amount not specified ('enough oil to come 2cm up the side of the pan'); estimated 500g for shallow frying, used as frying medium and mostly discarded.

title: Japanese gohan rice
instructions: Measure the sushi rice into a bowl and cover with cold water.
Massage the grains gently with your fingers until the water turns cloudy, then drain.
Repeat rinsing and draining five more times until the water runs clear.
Tip the rinsed rice into a saucepan with 400ml water (or 200ml dashi plus 200ml water).
Bring to the boil, then reduce to a low simmer.
Cover with a tight-fitting lid with a steam hole and cook for 15 minutes.
Remove from the heat and let it sit, covered, for another 15 minutes.
Stir through the mirin, then fluff the rice with a fork.
Serve with your choice of pickle juice and spring onions as garnish.

ingredients.note: 1 tbsp ≈ 15g, converted from volume
garnish, amount not specified in original; inferred small quantity
garnish, amount not specified in original; inferred small quantity

title: Japanese Katsudon
instructions: Warm the oil in a pan and fry the sliced onion until golden brown.
Nestle the cooked tonkatsu pork cutlet in the middle of the pan.
Whisk together the dashi (or stock), soy sauce, mirin and sugar, then pour three-quarters of it around the pork.
Let it sizzle for a couple of minutes so the sauce thickens slightly and the pork reheats through.
Pour the beaten eggs around the pork and cook for 2-3 minutes until just set but still a little runny.
Divide the rice between two bowls, top each with half the egg and pork mixture, scatter over the chopped chives, and serve at once, drizzling with extra soy sauce if you like.

ingredients.note: oil sizzles the onion then stays in the simmered sauce that's served, so counted as eaten, not a frying medium
'1 large onion' estimated at 150g
this is the pre-made tonkatsu (breaded pork cutlet) referenced from another recipe; added already cooked, weight estimated at ~150g for one cutlet
ingredient list says vegetable stock but instructions call for dashi; treated as 150ml≈150g
2 beaten eggs estimated at 50g each
rice is divided already prepared/cooked into bowls; 200g assumed as cooked weight
no quantity given for chopped chives; small garnish amount inferred

title: Ham hock colcannon
instructions: Peel the potatoes and cut them into even, medium chunks.
Put the chunks into a large pan of cold salted water, bring to the boil, and simmer for 10-15 minutes until a knife slides in easily.
While the potatoes cook, melt the butter in a large sauté pan over medium heat.
Add the garlic, cabbage, spring onions and a pinch of seasoning, stirring occasionally until the cabbage wilts but keeps a little bite, then set aside.
Drain the potatoes and let them steam-dry for a couple of minutes.
Mash the potatoes in the same pan with the cream, mustard and seasoning.
Stir the wilted cabbage mixture and the cooked ham hock into the mash, then keep everything warm over low heat.
Reheat the pan used for the cabbage without washing it, add a splash of oil, crack in the eggs and fry to your liking.
Divide the colcannon between bowls and top each with a fried egg.

ingredients.note: Butter stays in the dish (used to soften cabbage that's mixed into the mash), not a frying medium.
Estimated 5g per clove for 3 chopped cloves; not stated explicitly.
No weight given for '1 chopped Cabbage'; estimated at an average 800g head.

100ml converted to grams at ~1g/ml.

Estimated 50g per egg for 4 eggs; no weight stated. Fried fresh in the pan, not pre-cooked.

title: Boxty Breakfast
instructions: Preheat your oven to its lowest setting so it's ready to keep the boxty warm.
Peel all four potatoes.
Grate two of the potatoes and set them aside.
Cut the remaining two potatoes into large chunks and boil for 10-15 minutes until tender.
While the potatoes boil, squeeze as much liquid as you can from the grated potato using a clean tea towel.
Mash the boiled potatoes, then mix in the grated potato, spring onions and flour.
Whisk the egg white in a large bowl until it holds soft peaks.
Fold in the milk, then stir in the bicarbonate of soda.
Fold the egg-white mixture into the potato mixture.
Heat a large non-stick frying pan over medium heat and add a tablespoon of butter with a little oil.
Drop 3-4 spoonfuls of the potato mixture into the pan and cook for 3-5 minutes on each side until golden and crusty.
Keep the cooked boxty warm on a plate in the oven while you cook the remaining batches, adding more butter and oil to the pan as needed - you should get about 16 crumpet-sized boxty in total.
Heat the grill to medium and fry the cherry tomatoes in a heavy-based pan with a knob of butter and a little oil for about 5 minutes until softened.
Grill the bacon and pile it onto a plate, keeping it warm.
Stack the boxty, bacon and egg together, and serve with the tomatoes on the side.

ingredients.note: no yield stated for potato size; assumed 200g per large potato
'1 bunch' inferred as ~60g

title: Coddled pork with cider
instructions: Melt a knob of butter in a casserole dish until it's sizzling hot.
Fry the pork chops for 2-3 minutes on each side until nicely browned, then lift them out and set aside.
Tip the bacon, carrot, potatoes and swede into the same pan and fry gently until they pick up a little colour.
Stir in the cabbage, then nestle the pork chops back on top.
Add the bay leaf and pour over the cider and chicken stock.
Cover the pan and let everything simmer gently for about 20 minutes, until the pork is cooked through and the vegetables are tender.
Bring the dish straight to the table and serve spooned out from the pan.

ingredients.note: 'Knob' of butter inferred as ~15g; fat stays in the dish and is served with it, so not treated as a frying medium.
2 pork chops estimated at ~200g each.

title: Mustard champ
instructions: Boil the potatoes for about 15 minutes until tender, then drain and mash them.
Warm the milk with half the butter in the corner of the pan, then beat it into the mash along with the wholegrain mustard.
Fry the spring onions gently in the remaining butter for 2 minutes until just softened but still bright green, then fold them into the mash and serve.

butter is split between the milk mixture and frying the spring onions but stays in the finished dish, so not a frying medium

title: Piri-piri chicken and slaw
instructions: Blitz the chilli, garlic, ginger, oregano, coriander, paprika, vinegar and oil together into a marinade.
Rub the marinade all over the chicken and let it sit at room temperature for 1 hour.
Heat the oven to 190C (fan 170C/gas 5) and roast the chicken for 1 hour 20 minutes.
Rest the chicken under loose foil for 20 minutes.
While it rests, toss together the sliced red onion, carrots, beetroot, cabbage, mayonnaise, yogurt, extra vinegar and cumin seeds, and season to taste.
Serve the chicken with the slaw, fries and your favourite condiments.

title: Spring onion and prawn empanadas
instructions: Rub the butter into the flour until it resembles breadcrumbs.
Mix in the egg white and half the egg yolk (reserve the rest), the vinegar, a pinch of salt, and just enough cold water to bring the dough together.
Knead the dough on a floured surface until smooth, then wrap it and let it rest for 30 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 180°C (fan 160°C / gas 4).
Trim the green ends off the spring onions and finely slice the rest.
Warm a little oil in a pan and gently fry the spring onions until soft but not browned.
Stir in the chilli and garlic, then add the prawns and cook until they turn opaque, seasoning well.
Scoop the prawns out and let the pan juices bubble until thickened, then return the prawns to the pan.
Divide the rested dough into eight balls and roll each into a thin circle on a floured surface.
Spoon some filling onto one half of each circle, sprinkle over the feta, then fold the dough over to enclose it.
Trim the edges and crimp tightly to seal each empanada, placing them on an oiled baking sheet on their side or resting on the crimped edge.
Repeat with the remaining dough and filling.
Beat the reserved egg yolk with a splash of water and brush it over the tops of the empanadas.
Bake for 30 minutes, until golden and slightly crisp around the edges.

ingredients.note: '1 bunch' inferred as ~100g
'Dash' inferred as ~5g; oil remains in the served filling, not a frying medium
'1' chilli inferred as ~10g
'1 clove' inferred as ~5g

title: Grilled Portuguese sardines
instructions: Whisk together the olive oil, garlic, paprika, rosemary and chilli in a bowl with a pinch of seasoning.
Pour the marinade into a baking dish, add the sardines, and toss well to coat.
Cover and chill in the fridge for a few hours to let the flavours soak in.
Heat a BBQ or griddle pan until really hot.
Grill the sardines for 4-5 minutes on each side, until caramelised and charred.
Transfer to a serving plate, drizzle with a little oil, dust with extra paprika, and squeeze over the lemon wedges before serving.

title: Portuguese prego with green piri-piri
instructions: Rub the garlic over the steaks, then seal them in a bag with the olive oil, sherry vinegar and parsley stalks.
Smoosh everything together and bash the steaks a few times with a rolling pin.
Leave the steaks to marinate for 1-2 hours.
Blend all the sauce ingredients with a tablespoon of water until as smooth as possible (makes extra, keeps a week in an airtight jar).
Heat a griddle or frying pan until very hot.
Brush the garlic and parsley stalks off the steaks and season well.
Sear the steaks for 2 minutes on each side, then rest on a plate.
Toast the ciabatta halves cut-side down on the same plate to soak up the meat juices.
Slice the steaks and stuff into the rolls with the green sauce and rocket.

title: Fish fofos
instructions: Simmer the haddock in a lidded pan of water for 10 minutes over low heat, then drain and flake it.
Combine the flaked fish, potato, green chilli, coriander, cumin, black pepper, garlic and ginger in a large bowl, season, add the rice flour and mix well.
Break in one egg and stir the mixture together, then divide it into fifteen portions and shape each into a small log.
Whisk the remaining eggs in one bowl and tip the breadcrumbs into another.
Dip each fofo first in the beaten egg, then coat it in breadcrumbs, and chill for 20 minutes.
Heat about 1cm of oil in a large frying pan over medium heat and fry the fofos in batches, turning gently, for 2 minutes per side until evenly golden.
Drain the fried fofos on kitchen paper and repeat with the rest.
For the onion salad, mix the onion, coriander and lemon juice with a pinch of salt, then serve alongside the fofos and mango chutney.

title: Portuguese barbecued pork (Febras assadas)
instructions: Cut each pork tenderloin into five even pieces, leaving the tail ends a touch longer, then bash each piece inside a plastic bag with a rolling pin until it's a thin escalope the size of a side-plate.
Combine the white wine, paprika, salt, pepper and the juice of half a lemon in a bowl, add the pork and leave to marinate for 20-30 minutes while you get the barbecue coals glowing with no flames.
Cut the potatoes into thick chips, soak in cold water for five minutes, change the water and soak for five minutes more, then drain and pat completely dry.

Blanch the chips in oil at 130°C for 8-10 minutes in batches, drain and let them cool on a tray, then fry again at 180°C for a few minutes until well coloured and crisp, draining and seasoning with sea salt.
Take the pork from the marinade, rub with oil and grill on the barbecue in batches for about a minute per side - it will keep cooking off the heat, so don't overdo it.
Pile the chips on plates, top with the grilled pork, pour over any resting juices so the chips soak up the flavour, and finish with a spoonful of mayonnaise and a lemon wedge.

title: Portuguese fish stew (Caldeirada de peixe)
instructions: Warm a drizzle of oil in a large, deep-sided frying pan and sauté the chopped onions and diced red pepper over medium heat until soft but not coloured.
Finely chop the coriander stalks, saving the leaves for garnish, and stir them into the pan with the chilli and chopped garlic; fry for a few more minutes.
Pour in the white wine, add the saffron and bay leaf, and let it simmer until reduced by half.
Add the potatoes, tomatoes and 300ml water, bring to a gentle boil, and break up the tomatoes with a spoon.
Simmer for 20-25 minutes until the potatoes are just tender and the tomatoes have broken down.
Season well, then nestle the cod into the sauce and arrange the squid, prawns, clams and mussels on top.
Cover and cook for 6-8 minutes until the mussels and clams have opened, the prawns are cooked through and the fish flakes easily.
Toast the baguette slices, rub lightly with a halved garlic clove and drizzle with olive oil.
Scatter the stew with the reserved coriander leaves and serve with the garlic toasts for dunking.

ingredients.note: assumed ~100g per onion for two chopped onions
assumed ~150g for one diced red pepper
assumed ~15g for a small bunch; stalks used in cooking, leaves reserved for garnish

title: Portuguese custard tarts
instructions: Mix the flour and icing sugar and dust your work surface with it.
Roll the pastry into a 45 x 30cm rectangle, then roll it up lengthways into a long sausage.
Cut the pastry log into 24 wheels, about 1-2cm thick.
Roll each wheel lightly to fit into two 12-hole fairy cake tins.
Press the pastry circles into the tins to form thin cases, then chill until needed.
Heat the oven to 220C/fan 200C/gas 7.
Bring the sugar, 200ml water, lemon zest and cinnamon stick to the boil, then reduce to a syrup; cool and discard the cinnamon and lemon zest.
Whisk the eggs, egg yolks and cornflour together in a large pan until smooth.
Heat the milk and vanilla pod seeds in a separate pan until just below boiling.
Gradually pour the hot milk into the egg mixture, then cook on low heat, whisking continuously.
Whisk the cooled sugar syrup into the custard until slightly thickened.
Pour the custard through a sieve into the pastry cases.
Bake for 15 minutes until the pastry is golden and the custard has darkened.
Cool completely in the tins, then dust with icing sugar and ground cinnamon before serving.

ingredients.note: 2 tbsp converted to grams (~8g/tbsp)
2 tbsp converted to grams (~7.5g/tbsp)

title: Moroccan Carrot Soup
instructions: Preheat your oven to 180°C.
Toss the chopped carrots, sliced onion, garlic, cumin, coriander, salt and olive oil together in a bowl until well coated, then spread them out on a baking tray.
Roast in the hot oven for 10-12 minutes, until the carrots soften, then pull the tray out and let everything cool.
Grind the roasted carrot mixture with a little water into a smooth paste, then strain it into a bowl.
Warm the strained carrot mixture in a non-stick pan, stir in two cups of water and bring it to a boil.
Mix in the garam masala and season with salt, stirring well.
Take the pan off the heat, stir in the lemon juice, and mix well.
Serve the soup hot.

1 medium onion estimated at 150 g

title: Mee goreng mamak
instructions: Heat oil in a pan over medium heat, then fry the peanuts, dried chillies, dried shrimp and dhal together until fragrant; remove from the pan and set aside.
Blend the fried aromatics with tamarind paste and water until smooth, then simmer this paste in oil over low heat, stirring until the oil separates and the mixture darkens.
Peel and cube the potatoes, then boil until knife-tender; drain and set aside, discarding the water.

Slice the onion and fried tofu, mince the garlic, and cut the cabbage and Chinese broccoli; prepare and slice the prawn fritters, soften the noodles if using dried, and mix the dark soy sauce with a little water.
For each portion, heat oil in a wok and stir-fry a quarter of the garlic, onion and paste until fragrant, adding prawns if you like.
Add a quarter of the tofu, boiled potatoes, cabbage, Chinese broccoli and prawn fritters, and toss for another 30 seconds.
Add the noodles with 3 tablespoons of the dark soy sauce mixture, tossing for a minute, then push the noodles aside and scramble in an egg.

Finish with a squeeze of lime and a scatter of sliced green chillies, then repeat from the wok step for each additional portion — this recipe makes 4 individual servings, as it explicitly fries 'one portion' at a time using a quarter of the paste and vegetables each round.

title: Apam balik
instructions: Whisk the milk, oil, and eggs together until well combined.
Sift the flour, baking powder, and salt into the wet mixture, then stir until you have a smooth, even batter.
Heat a pan and spread a thin layer of the batter across the surface.
Cover the pan and cook for 30-60 seconds, until small air bubbles appear on top.
Scatter butter, cream corn, crushed peanuts, and sugar over the pancake.
Fold the pancake in half once the underside is browned.
Cut into wedges and serve warm.

title: Ayam Percik
instructions: Blend the shallots, ginger, garlic, cayenne peppers, turmeric, cumin, coriander and fennel into a smooth spice paste.
Fry the paste in a skillet over medium heat for about 10 minutes until fragrant, adding a splash of water or oil if it starts to dry out and keeping the heat gentle so it doesn't burn.

Stir in the tamarind paste, coconut milk, water and sugar, bring to a boil, then reduce to medium-low and simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally until slightly thickened; taste and adjust seasoning.
Let the marinade cool, then pour it over the chicken thighs and marinate in the fridge overnight up to two days.
Preheat the oven to 425°F (220°C).

Lift the chicken out of the marinade, spread the marinade over a greased or foil-lined baking sheet, lay the chicken on top so it fully covers the sauce, then spoon the remaining marinade over the chicken.
Roast for 35-45 minutes, until the thickest part of the chicken reads at least 175°F (80°C) internally.
Rest the chicken for 5 minutes, brush with a little oil, then serve with the sauce over steamed or coconut rice.

title: Nasi lemak
instructions: Simmer the coconut milk, water, ginger paste, fresh ginger, bay leaf, salt and rice together in a covered pot until the rice is tender, about 20-30 minutes.
Hard-boil the eggs: bring to a boil, cover and let stand off the heat 10-12 minutes, then cool, peel and halve them; slice the cucumber.
Fry the peanuts in hot vegetable oil until lightly golden, then drain on paper towels.
Fry half the anchovy fillets in the same oil until crisp, drain them, and discard the frying oil.
Wipe out the pan, heat fresh oil, and sauté the onion, garlic and shallots until fragrant.
Stir in the chilli paste and cook until thickened, splashing in a little water if it dries out too much.
Add the remaining anchovies and cook for a further 5 minutes.
Stir in salt, sugar and tamarind juice, then simmer until the sauce thickens.
Spoon the onion-garlic sambal over the warm rice and top with the fried peanuts, crispy anchovies, cucumber slices and egg halves.

title: Roti john
instructions: Crack the eggs into a bowl and mix in the minced beef, chopped onion, and chilli until well combined.
Heat a pan or griddle with a little vegetable oil.
Pour the beef and egg mixture onto the hot pan and immediately press an open-faced half of the baguette on top.
Press down firmly on the bread with a spatula and let it grill for about 2 minutes so the egg mixture cooks into the bread.
Flip the whole thing over so the bread crisps up on the other side.
Take it off the pan and slice the bread into smaller portions.
Finish with mayonnaise and/or sambal before serving, if you like.

title: Beef Rendang
instructions: Chop up all the spice paste ingredients and blitz them in a food processor until smooth.
Heat the vegetable oil in a stew pot and stir-fry the spice paste with the cinnamon stick, cloves, star anise, and cardamom until fragrant.
Add the beef and pounded lemongrass, stirring for a minute to coat.
Pour in the coconut cream, tamarind paste, and water, then simmer on medium heat, stirring often, until the meat is nearly cooked through.
Stir in the lime leaves, toasted coconut (kerisik), and sugar, blending everything well with the meat.
Turn the heat to low, cover, and let it simmer for 1 to 1½ hours until the beef is fork-tender and the gravy has reduced to a thick paste.
Taste and adjust with more salt and sugar as needed.
Serve hot with steamed rice, saving some for the next day — it tastes even better overnight.

title: Seri muka kuih
instructions: Soak the glutinous rice in water for at least 1½ hours, then drain well.
Grease a 9-inch round or square cake pan with cooking spray or line it with plastic wrap.
Mix the drained rice with coconut milk, water and salt, then pour into the pan and top with pandan knots.
Steam for 30 minutes.
Fluff the rice, remove the pandan knots, then use a greased spatula to flatten the rice, pressing out any air pockets, especially at the sides.
Steam for a further 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, combine the pandan juice, coconut milk, flour, cornflour and sugar in a bowl and mix well.
Whisk in the eggs, then strain the mixture into a metal bowl or pot.
Set the bowl over simmering water (double boiler or bain-marie) and stir continuously until the custard thickens, about 15 minutes.
Pour the pandan custard over the steamed rice, tap gently to release air bubbles, and steam for another 30 minutes.
Remove the kuih from the steamer and let it cool completely before cutting into rectangles or diamonds.

ingredients.note: No yield stated in text; servings inferred from total mass (~1.2kg) and typical 9-inch pan cut into small kuih pieces (~16 pieces, 70-90g each).

title: Cevapi Sausages
instructions: Tip the ground beef and pork into a large bowl.
Finely chop the onion and grate the garlic straight into the bowl.
Stir in the chopped parsley, paprika, baking powder, breadcrumbs, water, crumbled stock cube, salt and pepper.
Mix everything thoroughly with a hand mixer fitted with dough hooks until well combined.
Cover the bowl and chill in the fridge for 1-2 hours to let the flavours settle.
Pinch off portions of about 50-55 g and roll each into a thumb-thick sausage, 7-10 cm long.
Grill the cevapi on a hot grill pan or barbecue for 12-14 minutes, turning several times, until browned and cooked through.
Cut one open to check it's cooked before pulling the rest off the heat.
Serve hot, or reheat later in a 180°C oven for about 10 minutes until piping hot.

no weight given for cloves; assumed ~3 g per clove

title: Croatian lamb peka
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200°C fan (220°C conventional, 425°F, gas mark 7).
If your lamb isn't already diced, cut the shoulder into large chunks.
Whisk together the olive oil, garlic sauce, tomato puree, black pepper and a pinch of salt to make the marinade.
Layer the potatoes and chopped vegetables into a large lidded casserole or cast-iron pot.
Sit the lamb chunks on top of the vegetables, then pour the marinade and white wine over everything.
Scatter the thyme (plus rosemary and sage) over the top, keeping the herbs loosely piled so the stalks are easy to fish out later.
Put the lid on the pot (or cover tightly with foil if you don't have one) and cook for 1 hour 30 minutes.
Take off the lid, remove any thick herb stems, and stir in a little more olive oil.
Return to the oven, uncovered, for a further 20-30 minutes until everything is tender.
Serve with fresh homemade bread for dipping into the juices.
