title: Pan-fried hake, white bean & chorizo broth
instructions: Drain the dried white beans and tip them into a large pan with 2 litres of water.
Add the whole garlic cloves, bay leaves and thyme, then simmer for about 30 minutes until the beans are tender.
Remove from the heat and set the beans aside.
Meanwhile, heat 2 tablespoons of the olive oil in a frying pan.
Fry the bread with the remaining garlic clove until golden and crisp, then scoop it out and drain on kitchen paper.
Add the chorizo to the same pan and fry until crisp, then tip it out and keep warm with the bread.
Add another 2 tablespoons of oil and the onion to the pan, and cook for 5 minutes until softened.
Stir in the paprika.
Drain the beans and add them to the onions along with the chicken stock and 2 teaspoons of salt.
Cook for 5-10 minutes, then stir through the parsley and keep warm.
Season the hake and heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of oil in the frying pan.
Place the hake skin-side down in the pan and cook for 3-5 minutes over medium-high heat to crisp the skin.
Flip the fish and cook for a further 3-5 minutes until cooked through.
Spoon the white bean mix into bowls, top with the hake, and finish with the fried bread, chorizo and a little extra thyme.

ingredients.note: 4 cloves, estimated ~5g each
2 leaves, estimated ~0.5g each
1 tsp fresh thyme, estimated weight
6 tbsp total across steps; oil is absorbed into fried bread, chorizo and fish that are served, so counted as eaten not drained

title: Churros
instructions: Boil the kettle and measure 300ml of boiling water into a jug, then stir in the melted butter and vanilla extract.

Sift the flour and baking powder into a large mixing bowl with a good pinch of salt, make a well in the centre, pour in the wet mixture and beat quickly with a wooden spoon until smooth and lump-free.
Let the dough rest for 10-15 minutes while you make the chocolate sauce.
Combine all the sauce ingredients in a pan and melt gently together, stirring now and then until glossy and smooth, then keep it warm on low heat.
Fill a large deep saucepan one-third full with oil and heat it until a cube of bread browns in 45 seconds to a minute, taking proper care with hot oil.
Line a tray with kitchen paper and mix the caster sugar with the cinnamon for coating.
Fit a piping bag with a star nozzle, fill it with the rested dough, and pipe strips straight into the hot oil, snipping each one off with scissors.
Fry the churros until golden and crisp, watching carefully for air bubbles and working in batches.
Lift the cooked churros out with a slotted spoon, drain on the paper-lined tray, and toss them in the cinnamon sugar while still warm.
Serve the churros hot alongside the warm chocolate sauce for dipping.

1 tsp estimated as ~4g

title: Clam, chorizo & white bean stew
instructions: Fry the chorizo in a large lidded frying pan over medium heat until it crisps and releases its oil.
Add the onion and cook for 5 minutes until softening.
Stir in the garlic and finely chopped parsley and fry for 1 minute more.
Pour in the stock and tinned tomatoes, bring to the boil, then reduce the heat.
Add the butter beans and sherry vinegar and simmer for 10 minutes until the liquid reduces slightly.
Scatter the clams over the top, cover with the lid and steam for 2-4 minutes, shaking the pan occasionally until the clams open.
Taste before seasoning since the clams can be quite salty, then scatter over the remaining chopped parsley.
Serve with plenty of crusty bread.

title: Spanish chicken pie
instructions: Heat your oven to 200°C (fan 180°C, gas 6).
Boil the potatoes for 15-20 minutes until they're tender, then drain and return them to the pan.
Mash the potatoes with a little seasoning and 2 teaspoons of the paprika.
While the potatoes cook, heat the olive oil in a large pan and fry the onions and garlic for a few minutes until softened.
Stir in the remaining paprika and cook for 1 minute, then add the tinned tomatoes and bring to a simmer.
Tip the tomato mixture into a large ovenproof dish and stir in the chicken, roasted peppers, olives and a little seasoning.
Spoon the mashed potato over the top.
Bake for 15 minutes until the mash is golden and the sauce underneath is bubbling.

used to fry onion/garlic then simmered into the sauce which is served, so counted as eaten not a frying medium

title: Arroz al horno (baked rice)
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200C/180C fan/gas 6.
Heat half the olive oil in a wide, deep pan or shallow casserole (about 30cm across) and sear the pork belly slices in batches over high heat until coloured, then set them aside.
Add the remaining oil, lower the heat to medium, and fry the black pudding and bacon lardons for a few minutes until browned, then lift out with a slotted spoon.
Fry the onion and red peppers for about 10 minutes until soft and pale gold.
Stir in the chopped tomato and cook until it breaks down.
Add the garlic, smoked paprika and chilli flakes, and cook for 2 more minutes.
Return the pork, black pudding and bacon to the pan.
Add the dried white beans, chicken stock and thyme, and bring everything to the boil.
Scatter the paella rice around the pork, pushing it under the stock.
Bring back to the boil, season well, then transfer uncovered to the oven.
Bake for 20 minutes without stirring, then check the rice.
If the rice isn't tender and the stock isn't fully absorbed, bake for another 5 minutes and check again.
Taste and adjust the seasoning.
Squeeze over the lemon juice and drizzle with extra virgin olive oil before serving, if you like.

title: Chorizo & soft-boiled egg salad
instructions: Boil the potatoes in a large pan of salted water for 12 minutes total.
Slip the eggs into the same pan after 6 minutes of potato cooking time.
Add the green beans for the final 2 minutes of cooking.
Drain everything together, then run the eggs under cold water to cool them.
While the potatoes cook, fry the chorizo slices for 1-2 minutes until they start to crisp, then lift them out with a slotted spoon, leaving the chorizo oil behind in the pan.
Add the garlic to the chorizo oil and cook gently for 1 minute.
Take the pan off the heat and stir in the sherry vinegar and chopped parsley.
Toss this dressing with the potatoes, beans, chorizo and a good seasoning of salt and pepper.
Peel and quarter the eggs, then scatter them over the salad to finish.

title: Air fryer patatas bravas
instructions: Soak the potatoes in just-boiled water for 30 minutes, then drain and let them air-dry for 5 minutes.
Heat the air fryer to 200°C.
Toss the potatoes in a bowl with 1 tablespoon of the olive oil, 1/2 teaspoon salt and 1/2 teaspoon black pepper until well coated.
Tip the potatoes into the air fryer basket and cook for 20-30 minutes until crisp and golden.
Meanwhile, heat the remaining olive oil in a small pan over medium-low heat and fry the onion for 8-10 minutes until softened but not coloured.
Stir in the garlic and cook for a minute, then add the paprika and cook for 30 seconds more.
Stir in the tomato purée and cook for 1 minute, then add the chopped tomatoes.
Simmer for 5-10 minutes over medium heat until slightly thickened.
Once the potatoes are done, tip them onto a platter and spoon over the tomato sauce.
Scatter with basil leaves and serve.

title: Chorizo, potato & cheese omelette
instructions: Boil the potato for 8-10 minutes until tender, then drain and let it steam-dry.
Heat the olive oil in an omelette pan, add the chorizo and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the potato to the pan and cook for a further 5 minutes until it starts to crisp.
Spoon the chorizo and potato out of the pan and set aside; wipe the pan clean.
Crack the eggs into the same pan and cook a 2-3 egg omelette.
When the omelette is almost set, scatter over the chorizo, potato, parsley and cheese.
Fold the omelette in the pan and cook for 1 more minute to melt the cheese.

title: Pisto con huevos
instructions: Warm the olive oil in a large flameproof casserole or cast-iron skillet over low heat.
Add the onions with a pinch of salt, cover, and cook gently for 15 minutes, stirring now and then.
Stir in the garlic and cook for a further 2 minutes.
Add the mixed peppers, cover, and cook over medium heat for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until just tender.
Mix in the oregano, thyme, bay leaves, black pepper, and a little more salt if needed.
Tip in the courgettes and aubergine, stir well, cover, and cook over medium heat for 10 minutes.
Stir in the tomatoes, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Crack the eggs directly over the pisto, taking care to keep the yolks whole.
Cook over medium heat for 5-6 minutes until the eggs are set but still a little soft.
Scatter with parsley and serve.

title: Chicken & chorizo rice pot
instructions: Heat the oil in a large flameproof casserole and brown the chicken pieces on all sides, working in batches if needed, then set the chicken aside.
Lower the heat, add the onion and red pepper to the same dish, and cook gently for about 10 minutes until softened.
Stir in the garlic and chorizo, and cook for a further 2 minutes until the chorizo releases its oils into the dish.
Stir in the tomato purée and cook for 1 minute more.
Return the browned chicken to the dish along with the thyme, white wine and stock.
Bring the liquid to a boil, cover with a tight-fitting lid, lower the heat, and cook for 30 minutes.
Tip in the rice, stir well, cover, and cook on low for a further 15 minutes, or until the rice is tender and has absorbed most of the liquid.
Take off the heat and let the pot rest for 10 minutes to soak up any remaining liquid.
Season to taste and scatter with parsley before serving.

title: Pollo en pepitoria
instructions: Steep the saffron threads in 75ml of just-boiled water and set aside to bloom.
Warm 2 tablespoons of the olive oil in a broad, shallow casserole and fry the garlic until pale gold.
Add the almonds and stale bread to the pan and fry everything together until golden.
Tip the garlic, almonds and bread into a food processor with salt, pepper and the chopped parsley, and blitz to a paste.
Heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of oil in the same pan and brown the chicken thighs all over, seasoning as you go, then set aside in a bowl.
Pour off all but about 2 tablespoons of the fat and cook the onion, carrot and celery until golden.
Pour in the sherry, scraping up any browned bits stuck to the pan.
Add the stock and the bloomed saffron with its water, bring to the boil, then reduce to a simmer.
Stir in the cinnamon stick, cloves and bay leaves, then return the chicken and its juices to the pan.
Season, cover, and gently cook the chicken for about 40 minutes.
Lift the chicken back out into a bowl, cover with foil to keep warm, leaving the sauce in the pan.
Separate the eggs, roughly chop the whites and mash the yolks in a small bowl with a couple of tablespoons of the hot sauce to temper them.
Boil the remaining sauce to reduce until it will just coat the chicken, then lower the heat and fish out the bay leaves and cinnamon stick.
Stir in the tempered egg yolks and cook gently for a few minutes until the sauce thickens.
Stir in the almond and bread paste to thicken the sauce further.
Return the chicken to the pan and warm through for about 3 minutes, spooning the sauce over it, then adjust the seasoning.
Scatter over the extra parsley, almond pieces and chopped egg whites, and serve straight from the dish, with rice if you like.

title: Spanish fig & almond balls
instructions: Blitz the almonds in a food processor until finely chopped, then tip into a large bowl.
Roughly chop the figs and whizz them to a smooth sticky paste.
Scrape the fig paste onto the almonds.
Mix in the dried apricots, cranberries, brandy, honey and cloves by hand until well combined.
Divide the mixture into 6 portions and roll each into a ball.
Tip the sesame seeds onto a tray and roll the balls in them until fully coated.
Cover the tray loosely with a clean tea towel and leave the balls to dry for a week before packaging.
Store in a cool place for up to 2 months.

title: Spanish Chicken
instructions: Set your oven to 190°C (170°C fan/gas 5).
Tip the chicken thighs, sliced onions, paprika, lemon zest and juice, parsley, chicken stock and olive oil into a large, wide ovenproof dish.
Use your hands to mix everything together thoroughly and season well.
Bake for 45 minutes, giving the onions a stir after 20 minutes, until the chicken is golden and the onions are tender.
Serve with rice.

title: Torrijas with sherry
instructions: Whisk the eggs together with the double cream, milk, golden caster sugar and sweet sherry in a wide shallow bowl.
Cut each thick slice of bread in half and dunk the pieces in the egg mixture, turning to coat both sides evenly.
Let the bread soak in the egg mixture for about 10 minutes, turning occasionally so it soaks through without turning to mush.
Warm the olive oil in a large frying pan and fry the soaked bread for about 3 minutes per side until deep golden and crisp at the edges.
Transfer cooked torrijas to a low oven to keep warm while you fry the rest in batches.
Plate up the torrijas, dust generously with icing sugar, and serve with crème fraîche or Greek yogurt on the side.

title: Chickpea, chorizo & spinach stew
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pan and fry the onion gently for 3-4 minutes until it starts to soften.
Stir in the carrot, celery, thyme and bay leaves, season, and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring now and then.
Add the garlic, chorizo, cinnamon and smoked paprika, then fry gently until the vegetables soften and the chorizo releases its oils and turns crisp.
Tip in the chickpeas, sherry vinegar and 150ml water, and bring to a simmer for 1-2 minutes until the chickpeas are heated through.
Stir in the spinach and let it wilt slightly into the chickpeas.
Take off the heat, season to taste, and serve warm with crusty bread.

title: Seafood rice
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a deep frying pan and soften the sliced leek for about 5 minutes without letting it brown.
Add the chorizo and fry until it releases its oils.
Stir in the turmeric and rice until everything's coated in the oils.
Pour in the fish stock, bring to the boil, then simmer for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Tip in the peas and cook for a further 5 minutes.
Stir in the frozen seafood mix and heat through for 1-2 minutes, or until the rice is fully cooked.
Taste and adjust the seasoning, then serve immediately with lemon wedges.

title: Chorizo & chickpea soup
instructions: Set a medium pan over heat and pour in the tinned tomatoes along with a can of water, letting them come up to temperature.
While that heats, chop the chorizo into chunky pieces, trimming off any skin, and shred the cabbage.
Tip the chorizo and cabbage into the pan with the chilli flakes and chickpeas, then crumble in the stock cube.
Stir everything together, cover, and let it bubble over high heat for about 6 minutes until the cabbage is just tender.
Ladle into bowls and serve with crusty or garlic bread.

title: Ajo blanco
instructions: Tear the bread into a bowl and pour over 350ml of water, then let it soak for 10 minutes.
Tip the soaked bread, almonds, garlic, oil, vinegar, another 350ml water and 1 tsp salt into a blender and blitz until smooth.
Chill the soup in the fridge for about an hour, then ladle it out with a drizzle of oil and a crack of black pepper on top.

title: Chocolate churros with chocolate & salted caramel sauce
instructions: Make the caramel sauce: melt the sugar and butter together in a saucepan until the sugar dissolves and it starts to simmer.
Stir in the cream and simmer for 2-3 minutes until smooth.
Take off the heat, add a pinch of sea salt and the chopped chocolate, then stir for 2-3 minutes until fully melted; keep warm or set aside to reheat later.
Mix the caster sugar with the cinnamon and a small pinch of sea salt for the coating and set it aside.
Whisk the flour, cocoa, baking powder and a pinch of salt together in a bowl.
Add the vanilla extract and melted butter, then slowly pour in the boiling water while whisking to a smooth, very thick batter.
Let the batter cool a few minutes, then spoon it into a piping bag fitted with a medium star nozzle.
Heat sunflower oil in a deep-fat fryer or heavy pan (no more than a third full) to 170C, testing with a cube of bread that browns in 30 seconds.
Pipe 12-15cm lengths of the batter straight into the hot oil, snipping each one off with scissors.
Fry four or five churros at a time for about 4 minutes, turning them until golden and crisp.
Lift the churros out onto a wire rack; repeat with the remaining batter.
Roll the warm churros in the cinnamon sugar and serve at once with the chocolate sauce and ice cream, or cool them for reheating later.
To reheat, bake the churros for 5-8 minutes then roll in the sugar coating, warming the sauce alongside for dipping.

ingredients.note: Quantity not given; estimated ~1000g based on 'no more than a third full' deep-fry instruction; most is drained off as frying medium, not eaten.

title: Salt cod tortilla
instructions: Heat half the olive oil in a frying pan and sauté the onion over medium heat for about 8 minutes until soft and pale gold, then remove it and set aside.
Add the potatoes to the same pan and cook until tender but not falling apart, turning them carefully and covering the pan now and then to help them cook through.
Return the onions to the pan with the garlic and cook for another 4 minutes.
Tip everything into a bowl with the eggs, parsley and seasoning, mix well, and leave to sit for half an hour.
Meanwhile, put the salt cod in a saucepan, cover with water, bring to a simmer, then take off the heat, cover and leave for 10 minutes.
Drain the cod, let it cool, remove the skin and bones, break it into large flakes and fold it into the potato and egg mixture.
Heat the remaining oil in a non-stick frying pan and pour in the tortilla mix.
Cook over medium-low heat, covering if needed to set the centre, until just set and coming away from the sides, checking underneath with a spatula so it doesn't stick.
Slide the tortilla onto a plate, place the pan on top, flip it back in uncooked side-down, and cook over low heat until golden (or finish under the grill).
Let the tortilla cool a little before serving.

title: Patatas bravas
instructions: Warm the olive oil in a pan and gently fry the diced onion for about 5 minutes until soft.
Stir in the garlic, tinned tomatoes, tomato puree, paprika, chilli powder, sugar and a pinch of salt, then bring to the boil.
Lower the heat and simmer for about 10 minutes until the sauce turns pulpy - this can be chilled for up to 24 hours if made ahead.
Heat the oven to 200C/180C fan/gas 6.
Pat the potatoes dry with kitchen paper, then toss them with the 2 tbsp olive oil and some seasoning in a roasting tin.
Roast for 40-50 minutes until crisp and golden.
Tip the roasted potatoes into serving dishes and spoon the tomato sauce over the top.
Scatter with fresh parsley before serving.

title: Quick gazpacho
instructions: Blitz the passata, red pepper, chilli, garlic, sherry vinegar and lime juice together in a blender or with a stick blender until smooth.
Season to taste.
Serve chilled with ice cubes.

title: Prawns with Romesco sauce
instructions: Halve the red pepper lengthways, remove the seeds and stalk.
Line a grill pan with foil and lay the pepper halves skin-side up alongside the whole garlic cloves, chilli and tomato.
Grill for 2 minutes, turn the tomato, then grill for a further 2 minutes.
Lift out the tomato, peel it, quarter it, remove the seeds and chop it roughly.
Keep grilling the pepper, chilli and garlic for 4-5 minutes until the pepper and chilli skins blacken and the garlic softens and starts to split its skin.
Once cool enough to handle, peel the chilli, halve it and scrape out the seeds; peel the pepper, then roughly chop both the pepper and chilli.
Spread the hazelnuts and almonds over the foil and grill until toasted.
Finely chop the toasted nuts and parsley together in a food processor, then tip into a small bowl.
Heat 3 tablespoons of the olive oil in a frying pan and fry the pepper, garlic and chilli for 3 minutes.
Tear the bread into pieces, add to the pan and turn it in the oil until lightly browned.
Pulse the fried mixture in the food processor with the chopped tomato, salt, vinegar and remaining oil until roughly chopped, then tip into a bowl.
Let the sauce cool and store in the fridge for up to 3 days if making ahead.
On serving day, stir the toasted nuts and parsley into the sauce.
Serve the sauce in a small bowl alongside the peeled prawns, with cocktail sticks for spearing.
Peel the prawns before serving with the romesco sauce.

title: Spanish-style slow-cooked lamb shoulder & beans
instructions: Mix all the spice ingredients together with a generous pinch of salt to make a rub.
Score the lamb shoulder deeply all over and massage the spice rub into the cuts.
Marinate for up to 24 hours if you have time, though it's fine to cook straight away.
Preheat the oven to 150°C fan 130°C gas 2.
Heat the olive oil in a large flameproof casserole over medium-high heat.
Add the onions, carrots and garlic bulb and sizzle for 5 minutes until softened.
Pour in the chicken stock and bring to the boil.
Nestle the lamb into the pan, cover, and roast in the oven for 2 hours.
Uncover and lift the lamb onto a plate.
Stir the butter beans, roasted peppers and olives through the braising liquid.
Sit the lamb back on top, return uncovered to the oven, and roast for a further 1 hour 30 minutes until cooked through.
Transfer the lamb to a board and shred with two forks.
Stir the parsley through the beans before serving.

title: Spanish tomato bread with jamón Serrano
instructions: Combine the chopped tomatoes, minced garlic, olive oil, salt and pepper in a bowl.
Cover and chill the tomato mixture in the fridge until ready to serve.
Toast the baguette slices until golden.
Spoon a little of the tomato mixture onto each toasted slice.
Tear the jamón Serrano into pieces and lay one piece on top of each topped slice.
Serve immediately.

title: Spaghetti with Spanish flavours
instructions: Bring a pan of water to a rolling boil.
While it heats, snip the chorizo into strips with scissors and chop the parsley; slice the peppers, checking for stray seeds.
Once the water's boiling hard, tip in the spaghetti with a good pinch of salt, stir, bring back to the boil and cook for just 3 minutes.
Meanwhile heat the oil in a large frying pan, add the chorizo and peppers with plenty of black pepper, and cook for a minute or so until the juices run red from the chorizo's paprika.
Scoop out half a mugful of the pasta water, then drain the spaghetti and tip it straight into the frying pan.
Toss in the parsley and parmesan, splash in the reserved pasta water to loosen, and mix well.
Serve with extra parmesan at the table.

ingredients.note: par-boiled then finished in the sauce pan; not a leftover 'cooked' addition

title: Fried calamari
instructions: Slice the squid into rings about half a centimetre thick.
Tip the flour into a freezer bag, season well, add the capers, then shake to combine.
Add the squid rings to the bag and shake until fully coated.
Stir the crushed garlic into the mayonnaise and spoon into a serving bowl.
Pour oil into a large pan so it comes about 7cm up the sides, keeping the pan no more than a third full, and heat over medium until a piece of bread sizzles on contact.
Lift a handful of squid from the flour, shaking off the excess, and lower gently into the hot oil.
Fry for about 3 minutes until crisp, then lift out with a slotted spoon onto kitchen paper.
Repeat with the remaining squid in batches.
Serve immediately with the garlic mayonnaise and lemon wedges.

title: Ham croquetas
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a pan until shimmering, then sauté the leek until soft but not coloured.
Stir in the ham, fry for a minute, then add the flour and cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until golden, about 5 minutes.
In a separate pan, warm the stock and milk together until steaming (not boiling) and season with a few scrapes of nutmeg.
Gradually whisk the warm milk-stock mixture into the roux, a few tablespoons at a time, stirring constantly.
Keep cooking the filling over medium heat for about 10 minutes until it thickens and pulls away from the sides of the pan.
Season with black pepper and adjust the salt to taste, remembering the ham can be quite salty.
Spread the filling onto a baking tray, cover with cling film once it stops steaming, then chill for at least 1 hour.
Set up three bowls - flour, beaten egg, and breadcrumbs - and lightly oil your hands to make rolling easier.
Roll the chilled mixture into walnut-sized balls.
Coat each ball first in flour, then egg, then breadcrumbs.
Chill the coated croquetas for 30 minutes before frying.
Deep-fry at 180°C, or in a pan of shimmering oil, in batches of 5-6 until golden all over, then drain on kitchen paper and serve immediately.

title: Garlicky prawns with sherry
instructions: Warm the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat.
Add the garlic slices and sizzle for a few seconds until fragrant.
Stir in the prawns and cook for a couple of minutes until they start turning pink.
Pour in the sherry and cook a few minutes more until the prawns are cooked through.
Scatter with parsley and serve straight away.

title: Spanish beans with chicken & chorizo
instructions: Soak the pinto beans for at least 4 hours, or better yet overnight, fully covered with water.
Rinse and drain the beans the next day.
Place the beans in a large heavy-based pan with the chopped onion, paprika, herb bundle (thyme, bay leaves, parsley) and chicken thighs.
Pour in enough water or stock to cover everything, bring to the boil, then reduce the heat, cover and simmer for 45 minutes.
Lift the chicken out onto a plate, then add the potatoes and chorizo to the pan.
Keep cooking for another 30 minutes until the beans and potatoes are tender.
Discard the chicken skin and bones, tear the meat into large chunks.
Return the chicken to the pan along with the spinach and simmer for a final 5 minutes.

title: Spanish seafood rice
instructions: Warm the olive oil in a large saucepan and let the onion soften gently for 6-7 minutes.
Stir in the peppers and garlic and cook for a further 2 minutes.
Tip in the paella rice and stir for 1 minute so every grain gets coated in oil.
Pour in the stock, add the saffron and bring everything to the boil.
Let it bubble gently, uncovered, for about 20 minutes, stirring now and then, until the rice is tender.
Stir in the seafood mix and lemon juice and cook for 2 minutes more until piping hot and cooked through.
Ladle into warm bowls and scatter with parsley to serve.

title: Spanish meatballs with clams, chorizo & squid
instructions: Melt the butter in a heavy-based casserole and soften the diced shallots for 5 minutes.
Add the smoked paprika and crushed garlic, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Splash in the dry sherry, then tip everything into a bowl with the breadcrumbs; season and let cool.
Beat the pork mince and egg yolk into the cooled mixture, then shape into 18 small meatballs.
Wipe out the pan, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat, and fry the meatballs for 5 minutes just to colour, then lift onto a plate, keeping the oil in the pan.
Sizzle the chorizo with the chopped garlic in the same pan.
Add the squid and fry briefly to colour it.
Pour in the white wine, bring to the boil, scraping up the browned bits from the bottom.
Stir in the pulped tomatoes and bring back to the boil.
Return the meatballs to the pan along with the clams, cover, and cook for 5 minutes until the clam shells open, discarding any that stay shut.
Scatter over the chopped parsley, drizzle with extra virgin olive oil, and serve with crusty bread.

title: Spanish rice & prawn one-pot
instructions: Get the kettle boiling.
Heat the oil in a non-stick frying pan or shallow pan with a lid, then fry the onion, peppers, chorizo and garlic over high heat for 3 minutes.
Stir in the rice and chopped tomatoes along with 500ml boiling water, cover, and cook over high heat for 12 minutes.
Uncover and give it a stir — the rice should be almost tender.
Stir in the prawns, adding a splash more water if the rice looks dry, then cook for another minute until the prawns are just pink and the rice is tender.

title: Chorizo & tomato salad
instructions: Toss the tomatoes, onion, and thyme in a bowl.
Season, then drizzle with sherry vinegar and olive oil.
Let the flavours mingle while you get the chorizo going.
Fry the chorizo slices in a hot, dry pan until browned on both sides.
Serve the tomato salad topped with the fried chorizo, drizzled with a little of the oil from the pan.

title: Chicken with saffron, raisins & pine nuts
instructions: Season the chicken well, then get a large frying pan smoking hot with the olive oil.
Brown the chicken pieces about 5 minutes per side, then lift them out onto a plate and set aside.
Drop the heat to medium and fry the onions in the same fat for 3 minutes, then stir in the garlic and saffron and cook 3-4 minutes more.
Pour in the sherry and simmer 3-5 minutes until syrupy.
Return the chicken leg pieces to the pan, add the stock, thyme and raisins, cover and cook gently on low for 20 minutes.
Add the breast meat plus any resting juices from the plate and simmer 10 more minutes until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has reduced.
Meanwhile heat the oven to 200C/180C fan/gas 6 and roast the pine nuts on a baking sheet for 10 minutes until golden.
Season the finished chicken to taste, scatter over the toasted pine nuts and parsley, and serve with rice.

title: Šúĺlance s Makom
instructions: Boil the potatoes in their skins until fork-tender, then let them cool completely, ideally overnight, before peeling and mashing them finely.
In a bowl, combine the mashed potatoes with the flour, semolina, and a pinch of salt, then knead until you get a smooth, non-sticky dough.
Divide the dough into portions and roll each into a thin rope about 1.5 cm thick, then cut into 3 cm pieces and roll each between your palms into a small dumpling.
Bring a pot of salted water to a boil and drop in the dumplings in batches, cooking until they float to the surface, about 2-3 minutes, then drain and transfer to a bowl.
In a separate bowl, mix the ground poppy seeds with the powdered sugar, and melt the butter.
Drizzle the cooked dumplings with the melted butter, then toss them in the poppy seed-sugar mixture until evenly coated.
Serve warm, with an optional dusting of extra powdered sugar or a drizzle of honey.

title: Fašírky
instructions: Soak the bread slices in milk until soft, then squeeze out the excess liquid and mash into fine crumbs.
In a large bowl, combine the ground pork, chopped onion, minced garlic, soaked bread, egg, salt, pepper, marjoram, mustard, and parsley.
Mix everything together until evenly combined.
Shape portions of the mixture into palm-sized patties and flatten slightly for even cooking.
Dust each patty lightly with flour, dip into the beaten egg, then coat with breadcrumbs.
Heat vegetable oil in a pan over medium heat and fry the patties for 4-5 minutes per side until golden brown and cooked through.
Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve hot with mashed potatoes, cabbage salad, or fresh bread, alongside mustard, pickles, or garlic sauce.

title: Zemiakové Placky
instructions: Peel and finely grate the potatoes, then squeeze them in a clean kitchen towel to wring out as much moisture as possible for extra crispiness.
In a large bowl, combine the grated potatoes with the chopped onion, minced garlic, eggs, flour, marjoram, salt, and black pepper, stirring until you have a thick, even batter.
Heat a generous pool of oil in a large frying pan over medium heat until hot but not smoking.
Spoon portions of the batter into the pan, flatten into thin pancakes, and fry until golden and crisp on one side, then flip and cook the other side the same way.
Lift the pancakes onto paper towels to drain off excess oil, then serve hot with sour cream, a garlic dip, or as a side.

title: Bryndzové Halušky
instructions: Grate the potatoes finely by hand or in a food processor.
Tip the grated potatoes into a bowl and mix in the flour, egg, and salt until you get a thick, sticky, pliable dough.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.
Press the dough through a halušky maker directly into the boiling water, or scrape small pieces off a tilted cutting board with a knife.
Cook the dumplings until they float to the surface, about 2-3 minutes.
Scoop the dumplings out with a slotted spoon and set them aside in a large bowl.
Chop the bacon into small pieces and fry in a skillet over medium heat until crispy.
If using feta instead of bryndza, mix it with a dollop of sour cream to mimic the tangy Slovak sheep cheese.
Toss the cooked dumplings with the bryndza cheese until well-coated and creamy.
Top with the crispy bacon and its drippings.
Garnish with chopped chives or parsley before serving.

title: Pad Thai
instructions: Soak the rice noodles in a large heatproof bowl with boiling water for about 4 minutes, then drain and rinse under cold water.
Whisk together the lime juice, cayenne pepper, sugar and fish sauce in a small bowl, then set all the other ingredients ready by the stove.
Heat the oil in a wok and stir-fry the prawns until just warmed through, then toss in the spring onions and drained noodles.
Pour in the lime juice mixture, stir through the bean sprouts and half the peanuts and coriander, and cook for a minute until piping hot.
Pile everything onto a serving dish, scatter over the remaining peanuts and coriander, and serve with lime wedges and sweet chilli sauce.

title: Panang chicken curry (kaeng panang gai)
instructions: Pound the dried and fresh chillies, shrimp paste, garlic, galangal, lemongrass, lime zest, white pepper, coriander, cumin, nutmeg, peanuts and a teaspoon of salt into a rough paste using a pestle and mortar, or blitz them in a food processor with a splash of coconut milk.
Store any extra curry paste in a lidded jar in the fridge; it keeps for up to two weeks.
Heat the thick part of the coconut milk in a saucepan over medium-high heat until it starts to bubble.
Stir in a spoonful of the curry paste and cook for about a minute until fragrant.
Add the chicken and cook for 3-4 minutes until it begins to brown.
Stir in the green beans.
Season with the fish sauce and sugar, then pour in the rest of the coconut milk.
Add half the makrut lime leaves and simmer for 3-5 minutes until the chicken is cooked through.
Taste and add more sugar or fish sauce if needed, until it's salty, nutty and sweet.
Stir through the basil leaves and take off the heat.
Serve over steamed jasmine rice, garnished with sliced chilli and the remaining lime leaves.

title: Drunken noodles (pad kee mao)
instructions: Cook the flat rice noodles according to the pack instructions, then drain and set them aside.
Whisk the soy sauce, oyster sauce, fish sauce and sugar together in a small bowl to make the sauce, then set it aside.
Heat the oil in a large wok or frying pan over high heat and stir-fry the garlic and chillies for about 20 seconds until just starting to turn golden.
Add the sirloin steak and stir-fry for 1 minute until seared and starting to brown.
Add the broccoli and stir-fry for another minute.
Tip in the cooked noodles and pour over the sauce, stir-frying for 1-2 minutes until everything is well combined and piping hot.
Stir through the basil leaves, turn off the heat, and serve immediately to avoid overcooking.

title: Tom yum soup with prawns
instructions: Pour 1.3 litres of water into a large saucepan and bring it to a boil over high heat.
Add the onion, tomato, chilli, galangal, lemongrass, prawn heads and chicken stock cube, then stir well.
Reduce the heat to medium and simmer for 20 minutes until the liquid has reduced and infused.
Strain the hot broth carefully into a large heatproof bowl or jug, discarding the prawn heads.
Return the strained vegetables and herbs to the saucepan and pour the broth back over them.
Stir in the mushrooms and lime leaves, then cook for 3 minutes until the mushrooms are tender.
Add the sugar, fish sauce, lime juice, coconut milk and prawns to the pot.
Bring back to a boil and cook for 1-2 minutes until the prawns are cooked through.
Remove the pan from the heat and take out the lemongrass stalks.
Stir in the Thai chilli jam if using.
Scatter over the coriander to finish and serve.

Stir in the curry paste and cook for 1 minute to release its aroma.
Add the stir-fry vegetables and prawns, cooking for about 3 minutes until the prawns turn mostly pink.
Pour in the coconut milk, vegetable stock and noodles.
Bring everything to the boil, then lower the heat and simmer for 5 minutes until the noodles are cooked and the vegetables are tender but still crisp.
Divide the soup between two bowls and scatter over the coriander, basil, chilli and spring onion.

Add the prawns, fish sauce, chillies and lime juice, and bring back to the boil.
Taste and balance with a touch more lime juice or fish sauce as needed.

title: Thai pork & peanut curry
instructions: Warm the oil in a large saucepan or flameproof casserole over medium heat.
Add the spring onions and coriander stalks, and cook for 1 minute to soften.
Add the pork slices and cook for about 5 minutes until they start to brown.
Stir in the curry paste and peanut butter, and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the sugar, soy sauce, coconut milk and half a can of water; mix well.
Cover with a lid and let it simmer gently for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove the lid, add the baby corn, and turn up the heat.
Bubble for about 3 minutes until the corn is cooked and the sauce has thickened slightly.
Stir in the lime juice and adjust the seasoning to taste.
If freezing, cool completely and freeze for up to 2 months; defrost thoroughly and reheat on the hob until piping hot before serving.
Serve scattered with coriander leaves, alongside steamed jasmine rice.

Tip in the rice and peas, and keep tossing until piping hot.
Stir in the soy sauce and fish sauce, then fold through the chopped coriander.
Keep the rice mix warm while you fry the eggs.
Heat the remaining oil in a frying pan and fry the eggs with a little seasoning.
Divide the fried rice between 4 bowls and top each with a fried egg.
Scatter with coriander and serve with chilli sauce on the side, if you like.

instructions: Get your wok or a large frying pan smoking hot.
Pour in the oil and swirl it round to coat the pan.
Tip in the beef strips and sliced chilli, stirring constantly until the meat is lightly browned, about 3 minutes.
Pour over the oyster sauce and cook until it's heated through and coats the meat.
Stir in the basil leaves and serve with plain rice.

title: Prawn stir-fry
instructions: Tip the prawns into a bowl.
Blitz the chilli, garlic, snipped coriander stalks and caster sugar together in a small food processor or spice grinder.
Stir in half the lime juice and the fish sauce, then pour this marinade over the prawns.
Heat 1 tbsp of the oil in a wok and fry the ginger and spring onions for 1 minute.
Add the red pepper and fry for another minute until it starts to soften.
Toss in the water chestnuts and bean sprouts, cooking until the sprouts start to wilt.
Season with the soy sauce and a good grind of black pepper, then tip everything into a serving dish.
Heat the remaining oil in the wok, lift the prawns from their marinade and add them, tossing for 1-2 minutes until pink.
Pour in the reserved marinade, swirl the wok quickly, then tip the prawns over the vegetables.
Snip the coriander leaves over the top and sprinkle with the remaining lime juice.
Serve over rice noodles with extra lime wedges for squeezing.

title: Stir-fried chicken with chillies & basil
instructions: Get your wok or a large frying pan screaming hot, then swirl in a tablespoon of the oil.
Once it's shimmering, tip in the chicken and stir-fry hard for 8-10 minutes until it's browned all over, then scoop it out with a slotted spoon and set it aside.
Return the wok to the heat, add the rest of the oil, then toss in the garlic and shallots and stir-fry for about 3 minutes until golden.
Tip the chicken back in along with the chillies, fish sauce, soy sauce and sugar.
Stir-fry hard for another 8-10 minutes until the chicken is cooked through.
Stir through the basil leaves and serve immediately.

title: Thai-style steamed fish
instructions: Lay the trout fillets side by side on a large sheet of foil.
Scatter the ginger, garlic, chilli and lime zest over the fish.
Drizzle the lime juice on top.
Arrange the pak choi around and over the fish.
Pour the soy sauce over the pak choi.
Loosely fold and seal the foil into a parcel, leaving room at the top for steam to circulate.
Steam the parcel for 15 minutes.
If you don't have a steamer, set the parcel on a heatproof plate over a pan of gently simmering water, cover with a lid, and steam that way instead.

title: Thai rice noodle salad
instructions: Soak the vermicelli rice noodles and bean sprouts in a heatproof bowl of boiling water for about 4 minutes until the noodles turn tender.
Drain, rinse under cold running water, drain again, then tip back into the bowl.
Whisk together the lime zest, lime juice, fish sauce and muscovado sugar, then stir this dressing through the noodles along with the sliced red onion and lettuce.

For the pork version: heat a little oil in a non-stick frying pan and stir-fry the minced pork with grated ginger and a pinch of cayenne pepper for about 10 minutes until browned and cooked through, then mix into the noodles and serve warm in four bowls.

For the steak version (alternative): heat oil in a frying pan, press sesame-seed-coated, oil-rubbed sirloin steaks and fry about 5 minutes for medium rare, turning halfway, then rest 5 minutes and slice thinly; toss shredded red chilli and mint through the noodles and top with the sliced steak to serve.

Thread the chicken and vegetables onto skewers.

title: Thai prawn curry
instructions: Warm the oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and ginger, and cook for a few minutes until softened.

Pour in the tinned tomatoes and coconut cream, stir well, and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for about 5 minutes, adding a splash of boiling water if the sauce thickens too much.
Add the prawns and cook for 5-10 minutes, depending on their size, until they turn pink and are cooked through.
Serve hot alongside plain rice, sprinkled with chopped coriander if desired.

Blitz until the chicken is finely ground and everything is well combined.
Shape the mixture into six small cakes with your hands.
Heat the oil in a frying pan and fry the cakes over medium heat for 6-8 minutes, turning once.
Serve the cakes hot with sweet chilli sauce, lime wedges, coriander, shredded spring onion and red chilli.

title: Tom kha gai
instructions: Simmer the chicken stock and coconut milk together in a large saucepan over medium heat, then drop in the galangal, lemongrass and lime leaves and let it bubble gently for 6-8 minutes.
Slide in the chicken and poach at a gentle simmer for 8-10 minutes until tender and cooked through.
Stir in the mushrooms and chillies and simmer for a further 3-5 minutes until everything is cooked.
Sprinkle in the sugar, then add 3 tablespoons each of fish sauce and lime juice, tasting and topping up with more if you like.
Fish out the galangal, lemongrass and lime leaves with a slotted spoon before serving.
Ladle into bowls, scatter over coriander leaves and serve with steamed rice on the side.
Store chilled for up to three days, letting it cool completely first.

title: Spicy Thai prawn noodles
instructions: Cook the noodles per the pack instructions, then drain and set them aside.
Heat the sesame oil in a large frying pan and pour in the beaten egg, swirling to form a thin omelette.
Cook the omelette for 1-2 minutes, flip, and cook the other side for 1 minute, then tip it out and slice into thin strips.
Add the chopped chilli and ginger to the same pan and fry for 1-2 minutes.
Tip the cooked noodles, prawns and sliced egg into the pan.
Splash in the soy sauce and stir-fry everything together for 1 minute more, until the prawns are cooked through.
Throw in the chopped coriander and mint, then pour over the lime juice and scatter the zest.
Sprinkle the chopped peanuts over the top and serve.

title: Thai pumpkin soup
instructions: Heat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan / gas 6).
Toss the pumpkin with half the oil, salt and pepper in a roasting tin.
Roast for about 30 minutes until golden and tender.
Meanwhile, warm the remaining oil in a pan and add the onion, ginger and lemongrass.
Cook gently for 8-10 minutes until softened.
Stir in the curry paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add the roasted pumpkin, all but 3 tablespoons of the coconut milk, and the stock.
Bring to a simmer and cook for 5 minutes.
Fish out the lemongrass and let the soup cool for a few minutes.
Blend until smooth with a hand blender, or in batches in a large blender.
Return the soup to the pan to heat through, seasoning with salt, pepper, lime juice and sugar to taste.
Serve drizzled with the reserved coconut milk and scattered with chilli if you like.

title: Lemongrass beef stew with noodles
instructions: Blitz the ginger, garlic, lemongrass, coriander leaves and one sliced chilli in a food processor until you have a fragrant purée.
Warm the vegetable oil in a large pan over low heat, then stir in the purée and cook for 5 minutes to release the aromas.
Add the beef, soy sauce, five-spice powder, brown sugar and beef stock, then bring to the boil.
Cover, lower the heat and simmer gently for 1 hour 15 minutes.
Uncover and cook for a further 15 minutes until the beef is tender.
Just before serving, cook the rice noodles according to the pack instructions and drain well.
Divide the noodles between two bowls and spoon the beef stew over the top.
Scatter with the remaining sliced chilli and coriander leaves, and serve with lime wedges for squeezing over.

title: Thai drumsticks
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan/gas 6) and line a baking tray with foil.
In a bowl, mix the sweet chilli sauce with the orange zest, orange juice, crushed garlic, Thai red curry paste and a pinch of salt.
Add the chicken drumsticks and coat them thoroughly in the marinade.
Arrange the drumsticks on the foil-lined tray, spaced apart, and spoon over any leftover marinade.
Roast for 35-40 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and tender.
Wrap in foil or pack into a container to serve.

instructions: Cook the noodles per the pack instructions, then refresh under cold water and drain well.
Pour the stock into a large saucepan, stir in the curry paste, lime leaves, fish sauce and 250ml cold water, and bring to a simmer for 5 minutes.

title: Thai green chicken soup
instructions: Warm the oil in your biggest pan and toss in the onion, frying for about 3 minutes to soften it.
Add the chicken thighs and garlic, cooking until the chicken is no longer pink.
Stir in the curry paste, coconut milk, stock, lime leaves and fish sauce, then let it simmer for 12 minutes.
Add the chopped spring onion tops, green beans and bamboo shoots, and cook for 4-6 minutes until the beans are just tender.
Meanwhile, blitz the lime juice and basil together in a narrow jug with a hand blender until you get a smooth green paste.
Pour the green paste into the soup along with the sliced spring onions, and heat through.
Serve with lime wedges on the side, as a light lunch, supper, or make-ahead starter.

title: Shakshouka
instructions: Dry-fry the black pepper and garlic in a hot pan until fragrant.
Pour in a generous amount of extra virgin olive oil and let it infuse for a minute.
Once the oil is hot, tip in the tomatoes and salt, cover and simmer for 5 minutes.
Uncover, mash the tomatoes and reduce to your preferred consistency.
Make wells in the sauce, lower the heat, and crack the eggs directly into the pan so they sit on the base, not the sauce.
Cover and cook for 5 minutes without lifting the lid.
Take off the heat and let residual steam finish the eggs for 1-2 minutes.
Serve hot with flatbread.

title: Knafeh
instructions: Roughly chop the knafeh dough in a bowl, then work in the melted butter and yellow food colouring by hand until evenly coated.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the milk, cream cheese, sugar and cornflour until smooth.
Heat the milk mixture over a flame, stirring until it thickens, then remove from heat and let it cool slightly.
Stir the mozzarella into the cooled mixture until well combined.
Press half of the buttered knafeh dough into a baking dish to form an even base.
Spoon the cheese filling over the base and smooth it level.
Cover with the remaining knafeh dough.
Bake in a preheated oven at 200°C for 20-25 minutes, until the top is golden and crisp.
Meanwhile, boil water and dissolve sugar in it for 4-5 minutes, or simmer until it thickens slightly, stirring throughout.
Stir in the lemon juice and rose water.
As soon as the knafeh comes out of the oven, pour the syrup evenly over the top.
Serve the knafeh warm.

title: Shawarma chuck roast wrap
instructions: Whisk the beef stock, lemon juice, and all the spices together in your dutch oven or slow cooker until combined.
Add the chuck roast and turn it to coat, then spoon some of the sauce over the top.
Cover and cook on low for 8-10 hours, until the beef is fork-tender.
Lift the beef out and shred it with two forks, discarding any excess fat.
Skim the fat from the surface of the remaining cooking liquid.
Stir the shredded beef back into the sauce and keep it warm until ready to serve.
Spread garlic sauce on a pita, pile on the beef, and add your favorite garnishes.
Fold the wrap up burrito style.
Grill the wrap on a hot grill pan until toasted.

title: Chicken Shawarma with homemade garlic herb yoghurt sauce
instructions: Slice the chicken breast into bite-sized strips and toss it into a large ziplock bag.

Add the minced garlic, ground coriander, ground cumin, ground cardamom, cayenne pepper, smoked paprika, salt, pepper, lemon juice and olive oil to the bag, seal it, and massage everything together so the chicken is well coated.
Let the chicken marinate in the fridge for 10-12 hours, though a shorter rest works if you're pressed for time.
When you're ready to cook, heat a frying pan over medium-high heat with a touch of olive oil and toast one side of each flatbread until lightly charred, then set aside.
Add a little more oil to the pan and get the fries crisping in an air fryer at 180°C for about 15 minutes, shaking the basket occasionally.
Once the pan is hot, sear the chicken in batches so you don't overcrowd the pan, cooking each batch for 5-8 minutes until nicely browned and cooked through.
While the chicken cooks, stir the Greek yoghurt with the crushed garlic, chopped mint and parsley, a squeeze of lemon juice and the cumin, then season with salt to taste.
In a small bowl mix garlic powder, paprika, cumin, onion powder, oregano, dried parsley and a pinch of cayenne pepper for seasoning the fries.
Toss the hot fries in the spice mix and season with salt as soon as they're out of the fryer.

To serve, lay out the flatbreads, spread the garlic herb yoghurt sauce over each, then pile on lettuce, onion, tomato, fries, chicken and feta, finishing with a final drizzle of sauce. Serve immediately.

title: Shakshuka Feta Cheese
instructions: Warm a tablespoon of olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat.
Toss in the chopped onion, garlic, celery and red pepper, and stir for about 5 minutes until they soften.
Stir in the ground cumin, salt and pepper, then pour in the tomato sauce and let everything simmer for 7 minutes.
Crack the eggs into the pan, cover, and let them cook for 8 minutes until set.
Fold in the fresh spinach and crumble the feta over the top just before serving.
Serve hot with fresh bread on the side.

title: kabse
instructions: Sear the chicken in olive oil in a pot until golden, then stir in a stock cube and let it simmer for about 30 minutes.
Meanwhile, in a separate pot, sauté all the vegetables until softened and lightly caramelized.
Once the chicken is done, tip the rice, raisins and spices into the vegetable pot, pour in two and a half cups of water, and let the rice cook through.
When the rice is fully cooked, combine everything and finish it off in the oven.

title: Mamoul (Eid biscuits)
instructions: Cream the butter and sugar together until light and fluffy.
Mix in the oil, flour, and semolina until fully combined.
Pour in the warm milk and knead gently, just enough for the dough to come together.
Cover the dough and set it aside for up to an hour while you prepare the filling.
Toast the sesame in a pan, then stir in the butter and date paste, adding a splash of water until soft but not mushy.
Take the filling off the heat, let it cool on a plate, then roll into 15g balls.
Chill the dough balls for about 30 minutes.
Shape the rested dough into 30g balls.
Stuff each dough ball with a date filling ball and press it into a mamoul mould to shape.
Bake in a preheated 180°C oven for 20 minutes, until golden underneath and lightly golden on top.

title: Vegetarian Shakshuka
instructions: Sizzle the onion and pepper together over medium heat until the onion turns soft.
Stir in the garlic granules and let them cook for a minute.
Tip in the chopped tomatoes plus the remaining spices, stirring to combine.
Let the sauce simmer gently for about 20 minutes.
Make little wells in the sauce and crack an egg into each one.
Cook until the egg whites are set, then garnish and serve alongside toast.

title: Mutabbaq
instructions: Heat a frying pan with a little oil and fry the mashed garlic and ginger until fragrant.
Add the minced meat and all the dry seasoning, and cook until the meat is fully done, then set it aside to cool.
In a large bowl, combine the cooled cooked meat with the beaten egg, chopped tomato and chopped spring onion to make the filling.
Lay out two sheets of filo pastry (or one spring roll wrapper), brush with melted butter, and spoon the filling into the centre.
Fold the pastry over the filling to seal, brushing the seam with more melted butter.
Heat oil in a frying pan and fry the parcels until both sides and all four edges turn golden brown, or alternatively brush the parcels all over with melted butter and bake at 180°C for 10-15 minutes.
Serve and enjoy.

title: Pistachio Kunafa Chocolate Cake and Cupcakes
instructions: Gather all the ingredients for the chocolate cake.
Crush the Oreo biscuits to a fine powder, mix in a pinch of salt and warm milk as needed to form a cake batter, then fold in the baking powder and vinegar.
Grease a loose-bottomed tin and pour in a 1 cm layer of the batter.
Bake at 160°C for 7-8 minutes, until a knife comes out clean, then cool on a wire rack.
Gather the pistachio paste ingredients and gently melt the white chocolate over a double boiler so the mixer blade isn't stressed by hard chunks.
Coarsely grind the pistachios in a mixer, then mix in the condensed milk.
Blend in the warmed melted white chocolate and enough milk to crush everything into a smooth paste.
Melt ghee in a kadhai and roast the sevaiiya over low heat, stirring constantly until a pleasant roasted aroma comes through, then cool it.
Mix the cooled roasted sevaiiya with the homemade pistachio paste to make the kunafa layer.
Spread a 2 cm layer of the kunafa over the cooled chocolate cake base.
Melt the dark chocolate with the strained malai to make a ganache, cool it slightly, and pour it over the kunafa layer.
Garnish with chopped pistachio and silver balls, then chill in the fridge for 2-3 hours before unmoulding.
Spoon the same cake, kunafa and ganache layers into cupcake moulds to make kunafa cupcakes, then slice and serve.

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a pan over medium heat, then toss in the diced onion and cook until softened.
Add the diced tomato and let it cook for 4-5 minutes.
Stir in the allspice, salt, and cracked black pepper.
Crack the eggs into the pan, mix thoroughly for about 2 minutes, then cover and let cook for 5-6 minutes until the top sets.
Spread the liquid cheese over the top, cover for another minute, then serve.

title: Shawarma bread
instructions: Sieve the flour into a bowl and stir in the baking powder, salt and sugar.
Add the oil and mix everything together.
Pour in the water and knead the dough for about 10 minutes until smooth.
Cover the dough and let it rise until doubled in size.
Once risen, turn the dough onto a work surface and shape it into a round, using a plate or pot lid as a guide.
Heat a dry pan and place the dough round onto it.
Let it cook until it starts to puff up, then flip to cook the other side.
Move the cooked bread to a warm place to rest, where you'll see steam pockets form inside.

title: Rice paper dumplings
instructions: Pulse the cabbage in a food processor until finely chopped.
Season the cabbage with salt, toss well, and let it sit for 15 minutes to draw out moisture.
Wrap the cabbage in a clean tea towel and squeeze out as much water as possible.
Combine the squeezed cabbage with the carrot, mushrooms, ground pork, garlic, ginger, soy sauce, sesame oil and spring onions.
Season with black pepper and mix everything together thoroughly by hand.
Soak a rice paper sheet in warm water for a couple of seconds, then lay it on an oiled board.
Spoon a heaped tablespoon of filling into the centre of the sheet.
Fold the bottom up over the filling, then fold the top down and the sides in to seal into a neat square parcel.
If a parcel feels fragile, soak a second sheet and wrap it again for extra strength.
Repeat with the remaining sheets and filling, placing finished dumplings on an oiled plate.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large non-stick frying pan over medium-high heat, keeping it no more than a third full.
Fry the dumplings for 3-5 minutes until golden, turning them carefully with a slotted spoon.
Cover and cook for a further 3-4 minutes until golden all over.
Uncover and cook for another 1-2 minutes to finish.
Meanwhile, whisk together all the dipping sauce ingredients in a small bowl.
Serve the dumplings hot with the dipping sauce on the side.

title: Vietnamese caramel trout
instructions: Melt the sugar with a small splash of water in a large shallow pan, swirling gently until it turns a dark amber caramel.
Stir in the fish sauce along with most of the chilli and ginger, then splash in a tablespoon of water and boil again until syrupy.
Lay the fish fillets skin-side down and the bok choi cut-side down into the caramel sauce.
Cover the pan and simmer for 4-5 minutes until the fish is cooked through and the bok choi has wilted.
Turn off the heat, squeeze over the lemon juice, and scatter with the remaining chilli, ginger and coriander sprigs.
Serve with steamed rice.

Heat the oil in a frying pan over high heat, season the steak, and sear it to your liking - about 2-3 minutes per side for medium rare.
Let the steak rest for 5 minutes, then slice it thinly.
Divide the noodle salad and steak slices between bowls, scattering coriander over the top to serve.

instructions: Whisk the nuoc cham ingredients together with 5 tbsp hot water in a small bowl and set aside.
Whisk the corn flour, plain flour, sesame seeds (if using) and a big pinch of salt together in a bowl.

title: Vietnamese lamb shanks with sweet potatoes
instructions: Heat your oven to 160°C (140°C fan / gas 3).
Warm 1 tablespoon of ground nut oil in a heavy casserole, season the lamb shanks, then brown them two at a time on all sides, adding the rest of the oil for the second batch.
Lift the lamb out and tip in the onions, frying briskly for about 30 seconds.
Add the ginger, garlic and chopped chilli, then turn the heat down and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in 1 tablespoon of brown sugar, then add the star anise, lemongrass, lamb stock, tomato purée and seasoning.
Bring everything to the boil.
Cover the casserole and cook in the oven for 1½ hours, then add the sweet potatoes and cook for a further hour, until the lamb is completely tender and almost falling off the bone.
Stir in the fish sauce, lime juice and the remaining teaspoon of sugar to lift the flavour.
Scatter over the mint, basil and sliced chilli before serving.

instructions: Whisk all the dressing ingredients together in a large serving bowl.
Cook the rice noodles according to the pack instructions, then drain and toss them into the dressing.
Peel the carrot into long ribbons with a vegetable peeler.
Peel the cucumber into ribbons the same way, stopping once you hit the seedy core, and discard that part.
Add the carrot and cucumber ribbons to the noodle bowl along with the shredded chicken, radishes, red onion and mint.
Toss everything well so it's evenly coated in the dressing.
Scatter the peanuts over the top and serve.

title: Salt & pepper squid
instructions: Mix all the dipping sauce ingredients together in a small bowl and stir until the sugar dissolves, then set aside.
In a large bowl, combine the corn flour, plain flour, black pepper, Szechuan peppercorns and 2 tsp sea salt.
Line a tray with kitchen paper and keep extra salt on hand for sprinkling.
Heat about 7cm of oil in a deep fryer, wok or deep pan to 180°C — test with a cube of bread, which should brown in 20 seconds if you don't have a thermometer.
Coat the squid thoroughly in the flour mixture.
Fry the squid in batches for about 2 minutes each, until crisp.
Lift the squid out with a slotted spoon, drain on the kitchen paper and sprinkle with a little more salt.
Scatter the squid with sliced spring onion and chilli, and serve with the dipping sauce on the side.

title: Vietnamese-style caramel pork
instructions: Heat half the oil in a wok over high heat and stir-fry the pork in batches until browned all over, then remove with a slotted spoon and set aside.
Turn the heat down low, add the remaining oil, and stir in the shallots, ginger and chilli; cook gently for a couple of minutes until just starting to soften.
Add the sugar, fish sauce and 200ml water, stirring to combine, then bring to the boil so the sugar dissolves.
Return the pork to the pan and bubble vigorously for 8-10 minutes until the sauce thickens and coats the meat with a glossy finish.
Taste and stir in a little more fish sauce if needed, along with the chilli sauce.
Sprinkle with spring onions and serve with steamed rice and pak choi.

title: Vietnamese pork salad
instructions: The day before, make the dressing: simmer the sugar and lime juice with a tablespoon of water until the sugar dissolves.

title: Beef pho
instructions: Simmer the beef stock with 500ml water in a large saucepan.
Char the onion and ginger in a hot frying pan (or under the grill) on all sides for 3-5 minutes, then add to the stock.
In the same pan, toast the cinnamon, star anise, coriander seeds and cloves for 2-3 minutes until fragrant, then add to the stock.
Bring the stock to the boil, reduce to a simmer and cook for 30 minutes, then strain.
Meanwhile, trim the fat from the steak, wrap in cling film and freeze for 15 minutes to firm it up.
Slice the steak thinly, then re-wrap and chill until needed.
Taste the strained stock and season with palm sugar, fish sauce and soy sauce.
Cook the rice noodles according to the packet instructions and divide between two bowls.
Top the noodles with the sliced raw beef.
Bring the stock back to the boil and ladle over the bowls — the hot broth will cook the beef.
Scatter over the spring onions, chilli slices and herbs.
Serve with lime wedges for squeezing over.

To eat, wrap a roll in a lettuce leaf with a herb sprig and dip it into the sauce.

title: Barbecue pork buns
instructions: Heat the oven to 200C/fan 180C/gas mark 6.
Stir the sugar into the bread mix in a large bowl, then add water as directed on the pack.
Bring the dough together with a wooden spoon, then knead on a lightly floured surface for 5 minutes until smooth.
Put the dough into a large bowl, cover with oiled cling film and leave in a warm place until doubled in size.
Meanwhile heat the oil in a pan and fry the bacon until crisp, about 5 minutes.
Add the ginger and garlic and fry for 1 minute until soft.
Stir in the soy sauce, honey and tomato purée and mix well; this filling can be made up to 3 days ahead.
Turn out the risen dough, knead briefly, then divide into 12 even-sized balls.
Flatten each ball with your hands and spoon a teaspoon-size blob of filling into the middle.
Draw the dough up around the filling and pinch closed like a purse, then turn over and sit on a large baking sheet.
Cover with oiled cling film and leave to rise for about 30 minutes until pillowy.
Brush with beaten egg and bake for 20 minutes until golden.
Serve warm with dipping sauce; buns can be frozen after the second rise for up to 1 month, or baked a day ahead and re-warmed.

ingredients.note: oil used to fry bacon and aromatics then incorporated into the filling sauce; stays in the dish, so not counted as a frying medium
quantity inferred: 12 rashers assumed ~30g each; not specified in original text
'knob' of ginger inferred as ~15g
used as egg wash, not eaten as-is but counted whole per doctrine

title: Vietnamese prawn spiralized rolls
instructions: Whisk the lime juice, rice vinegar, palm sugar, fish sauce and chopped chilli with 50ml water in a bowl, then set aside so the sugar dissolves and the flavours infuse.
Fill a wide bowl with warm water and lay a clean damp tea towel on your work surface.
Dip a rice paper wrapper into the warm water for a few seconds until softened, then lay it flat on the tea towel.
Scatter a few mint and coriander leaves in the centre, top with two prawns and a small handful of the spiralized carrot and courgette, trimming the spirals if they're too long.
Fold the sides of the wrapper over the filling, then fold in the edges to fully enclose it, and roll up tightly.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Slice the rolls on the diagonal and serve with the dipping sauce.

title: Prawn & noodle salad with crispy shallots
instructions: Heat about 5cm of oil in a wok until a piece of shallot sizzles instantly when dropped in.
Toss the shallot slices in flour, shake off the excess, then fry until golden and crisp, about 1 minute.
Drain the fried shallots on kitchen paper and sprinkle with salt; set aside.
Whisk together the lime juice, brown sugar, fish sauce and crushed garlic to make the dressing; set aside.
Pour boiling water over the rice noodles in a large bowl and leave for 2 minutes until just cooked.
Rinse the noodles under cold water and drain thoroughly, shaking out excess water.
Return the noodles to the bowl and add the prawns, sliced red onion, chilli, cucumber, coriander and mint.
Pour the dressing over the salad and toss well to coat.
Scatter over the crispy shallots and roasted peanuts before serving.

title: Noodle bowl salad
instructions: Drop the rice noodles into a large bowl and pour over enough boiling water to cover them.
Tip in the sliced sugar snap peas and let everything sit for 4 minutes.
Drain in a colander, then run under cold water to cool, and drain again.
Tip the noodles and sugar snaps into a bowl and toss with the sesame oil.
Whisk together the soy sauce, honey, lemon juice, ground nut oil, garlic and ginger to make the dressing.
Pile the spring onions, coriander and flaked salmon on top of the noodles.
Pour over the dressing and toss everything together.
Serve with a pretzel or bread roll on the side.

title: Tangy carrot, cabbage & onion salad
instructions: Toss the sliced carrots, red cabbage, and red onions together in a large bowl.
Whisk the lime juice, ground nut oil, chopped red chilli, and brown sugar together until the sugar dissolves.
Pour the dressing over the vegetables and toss well to coat.
Add the mint and coriander leaves, then toss again.
Scatter the roasted peanuts over the top and serve.

title: Sea bass with sizzled ginger, chilli & spring onions
instructions: Season the sea bass fillets with salt and pepper, then slash the skin three times on each.
Heat a heavy-based frying pan and add a tablespoon of sunflower oil.
Once the oil is hot, fry the fillets skin-side down for about 5 minutes until the skin turns crisp and golden - the flesh should be almost cooked through by then.
Flip the fillets, cook for another 30 seconds to a minute, then move them to a serving plate and keep them warm; fry in two batches so the pan isn't crowded.
In the same pan, heat the remaining sunflower oil and fry the ginger matchsticks, sliced garlic and shredded red chillies for about 2 minutes until golden.
Take the pan off the heat, stir in the shredded spring onions, splash the fish with soy sauce, then spoon the sizzling ginger, chilli and spring onion mixture over the top.

title: Salmon noodle wraps
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200°C (fan 180°C / gas 6).
Soak the rice noodles in a bowl of just-boiled water for 2 minutes until pliable but not too soft, then drain well.
Return the noodles to the bowl with the spring onions, petit pois, salt, pepper and a third of the butter, and mix until the butter melts through.
Divide the noodle mixture between two large squares of baking parchment.
Sit a salmon fillet on top of each pile.
Slice the remaining butter and lay it over the salmon.
Bring the parchment edges together over the fish and fold to seal, tucking the ends underneath so there are no gaps.
Set the parcels on a baking sheet and bake for 15-20 minutes.
Transfer the parcels to plates and serve straight away.

title: Turkey Bánh mì
instructions: Tip the carrots and cabbage into a large bowl.
Whisk the ginger, rice vinegar, sugar and a few pinches of salt together in a small bowl.
Pour the dressing over the vegetables and toss well to coat.
Let the pickled slaw sit for at least 15 minutes.
Halve the baguettes lengthways.
Spread the pâté over the bottom half of each baguette.
Top with the pickled slaw, cucumber and turkey.
Stir the chopped chilli into the mayonnaise and dollop it over the filling.
Scatter over the mint leaves and sliced chilli.
Sandwich the baguettes together and serve.

title: Vegan banh mi
instructions: Toss the shredded vegetables with the vinegar, sugar and a teaspoon of salt in a bowl, then leave them to pickle quickly while you get the rest ready.
Heat the oven to 180C/160C fan/gas 4.
Cut the baguette into four pieces, then slice each piece horizontally in half.
Toast the baguette pieces in the oven for 5 minutes until lightly warmed through.
Spread each baguette piece with a layer of hummus.
Top four of the pieces with the tempeh slices and pile the pickled vegetables on top.
Sprinkle over the coriander and mint, then squeeze over some hot sauce.
Top with the remaining baguette pieces to close the sandwiches and serve.

title: Turkish lahmacun
instructions: Sift the flour into a large bowl, make a well in the centre and sprinkle in the yeast.
Pour 125ml water over the yeast, then flick flour over the liquid to seal it in a layer.
Cover and let the dough rest in a warm spot for 15 minutes until cracks appear on the surface.
Mix in a further 250ml water and 1 tsp salt, then knead for about 10 minutes until smooth and elastic, adding a touch more flour if needed.
Cover and let it rise again in a warm place for 30 minutes until doubled in size.
Heat the oven as high as it will go, around 240C/220C fan/gas 9.
Dust one or two baking trays lightly with cornmeal.
Pour boiling water over the tomatoes, let them sit briefly, then drain and peel off the skins.
Halve the tomatoes, remove the stalks and seeds, and chop the flesh.
Halve the chillies lengthways, remove the stalks, seeds and white membrane, rinse, then dice finely.
Combine the tomatoes, chillies, spring onions, finely chopped parsley, beef mince, spices, 1 tsp salt and ½ tsp black pepper in a bowl and mix well.
Knead the dough briefly, then divide into four pieces and shape each into a ball.
Roll each ball into a thin circle and place on the prepared trays.
Spread a thin layer of the meat mixture over each flatbread.
Bake for 10-15 minutes until the edges begin to darken.
Remove from the oven, scatter with roughly chopped parsley and sliced red onion, then squeeze over a few drops of lemon juice before serving straightaway.

ingredients.note: amount inferred from 'sprinkling' for dusting trays
grams inferred: 2 medium tomatoes ~100g each
grams inferred: 2 chillies ~15g each
grams inferred: 4 spring onions ~10g each
grams inferred: a typical bunch ~30g

title: Turkish rice (vermicelli rice)
instructions: Rinse the rice thoroughly under cold running water, draining through a sieve, until the water runs clear.
Cover the rinsed rice with fresh cold water and let it soak for 10 minutes while you prepare the vermicelli.
Heat the oil in a medium pan over medium heat, add the vermicelli, and stir continuously until the strands turn a rich golden brown, about 2-3 minutes.
Take the pan off the heat, stir in the butter until melted, and let the toasted vermicelli cool for 1-2 minutes.
Drain the soaked rice well, add it to the pan with the vermicelli, and stir to coat.
Pour in the hot chicken stock, sprinkle in the salt, and stir everything together.
Return the pan to the hob over high heat, bring to a boil, then lower the heat to the lowest setting, cover, and simmer for 7-9 minutes.
Remove the pan from the heat, cover with kitchen paper or a light tea towel plus the lid, and let the rice rest for 10 minutes.
Fluff the rice with a fork just before serving.

ingredients.note: oil remains in the dish, toasting vermicelli in same pan used for cooking rice; not a discarded frying medium
600ml converted to grams at 1:1 density

title: Imam bayildi with BBQ lamb & tzatziki
instructions: Preheat the oven to 190°C (170°C fan/gas 5).
Halve the aubergines lengthways, score the flesh deeply, brush generously with olive oil and roast on a baking sheet for about 20 minutes until the flesh is soft.
Meanwhile, fry the chopped onion in a little oil until soft, then add the garlic and cinnamon and cook for a further minute.
Once the aubergines are cool enough to handle, scoop out the centres, roughly chop the flesh and stir it into the onion mixture.
Halve the tomatoes, strain the seeds and juice into a bowl, chop the flesh and add it to the pan; cook everything together for about 10 minutes until soft, adding a splash more oil if needed.
Stir in most of the parsley, saving a little for garnish.

Lay the aubergine halves in a baking dish, divide the tomato mixture between them, pour over the reserved tomato juice, drizzle with more olive oil and bake for 30 minutes until the aubergines have collapsed.
While that bakes, mix the tzatziki ingredients together in a small serving bowl.
Season the lamb chops with salt, black pepper and a pinch of paprika, then griddle, grill or barbecue for about 3 minutes per side until nicely browned.
Transfer the lamb to a serving dish and squeeze the lemon halves over the top.
Scatter the baked aubergines with the reserved parsley and serve alongside the lamb and tzatziki.

ingredients.note: Weight inferred: 3 standard aubergines at ~300g each; no yield stated for this recipe, servings inferred from 3 aubergines giving 6 halves and 12 lamb chops giving 2 per serving (6 servings).
2 tbsp converted at ~15g/tbsp; oil is absorbed into the braised filling and drizzled over the dish, so it remains in the served food.

title: Ezme
instructions: Blitz the tomatoes and all the peppers together in a food processor until finely chopped, then tip into a sieve set over a bowl and let the juices drain away.
Add the onions, garlic and parsley to the processor and pulse until finely chopped; set this aside separately.
In a serving bowl, whisk together the red pepper paste, tomato purée, pomegranate molasses, pul biber, sumac, dried mint and most of the olive oil until it forms a cohesive sauce.
Fold in the blitzed onion mixture and the strained pepper mixture along with a teaspoon of flaky sea salt, stirring well to combine.
Finish with a drizzle of the remaining olive oil before serving.

title: Grilled aubergines with spicy chickpeas & walnut sauce
instructions: Warm the oil in a pan, tip in the onion and fry gently until soft and lightly golden, about 10 minutes.
Stir in the chilli, ginger and cumin, cook briefly until fragrant.
Add the chickpeas, tomatoes and a splash of water, bring to a boil, then simmer for 10 minutes.
Season with salt, pepper and the lemon juice.
Lay the aubergine slices on a grill pan, brush with oil, season, and grill until golden; flip, brush again, season and grill until tender and golden all over.
Combine the yogurt with the garlic, most of the walnuts and most of the coriander, seasoning with salt and pepper to make the sauce.
Arrange the grilled aubergine on a warm platter, spoon over the spiced chickpeas, drizzle with the walnut yogurt sauce, and scatter with the remaining walnuts and coriander.

title: Cacik
instructions: Line a sieve set over a large bowl with a thick sheet of plain kitchen paper or a clean muslin cloth, spoon in the yoghurt, cover with another sheet of paper, and let it strain in the fridge for at least 12 hours.
In a bowl, stir together the lemon juice, most of the olive oil, and the dried mint, letting the mint soften and soak up the liquid.
Mix the strained yoghurt into that bowl, then discard the strained-off liquid and set the empty bowl aside.
Halve the cucumber lengthways, scoop out the seeds with a teaspoon, halve widthways for easier handling, then coarsely grate it into the bowl the yoghurt was straining over.
Squeeze the grated cucumber with clean hands or a muslin cloth to remove as much liquid as possible.
Add the squeezed cucumber, minced garlic, and flaky salt to the yoghurt mixture and stir well to combine.
Finish with a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil and a sprinkling of dried mint on top.

title: kofta burgers
instructions: Tip the lamb mince into a big bowl with the onion, garlic, garam masala, coriander and chilli sauce, plus a good pinch of salt.
Get your hands in and squelch everything together until fully combined.
Shape the mixture into 16 small burgers; these can be frozen for up to a month or chilled for a day.
Heat your grill to its highest setting.
Lay the burgers in a single layer on a baking tray, working in batches if needed.
Grill on the top shelf for 5-6 minutes on each side until browned and cooked through.
Pile the burgers onto a platter and serve with the pita, tomato, cabbage, red onion and yogurt so everyone can build their own sandwich.

title: Hot cumin lamb wrap with crunchy slaw & spicy mayo
instructions: Heat a griddle pan until hot.
Rub the lamb leg steaks with the olive oil, ground cumin and a little seasoning.
Griddle the lamb for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until cooked to your liking, then set aside to rest on a plate.
Stir the sugar into the white wine vinegar until dissolved.
Toss the carrots, spring onions, cabbage and seasoning into the dressing to make a crunchy slaw.
Blitz the sweet peppadew peppers with the mayonnaise to make a spicy mayo.
Spoon a good heap of slaw onto each flatbread or pitta.
Slice the rested lamb, trimming off any excess fat, and lay it over the slaw, drizzling over the resting juices.
Spoon the spicy mayo on top and scatter with a few extra sliced peppers.
Roll up the wraps (or split and stuff the pitta) and serve any remaining slaw on the side.
