title: Samlar Machu Kreung – Cambodian Sour Stew
instructions: Warm the vegetable oil in a large pot over medium-high heat until it shimmers.
Brown the chicken thighs on all sides, stirring now and then so nothing sticks, about 5-7 minutes.
Pour in the water and bring everything to a boil, then drop the heat to a simmer.
Stir in the tamarind paste, fish sauce, and sugar until fully dissolved.
Add the lemongrass (bruise it first to release its oils) and the kaffir lime leaves, cover, and let the broth simmer for 10 minutes to build flavor.
Stir in the pineapple chunks, cherry tomatoes, and green beans.
Simmer uncovered for 10-12 minutes until the beans are tender-crisp and the tomatoes just start to soften — don't overcook or you'll lose their color and bite.
Add the sliced chili and simmer 2 more minutes to bring in the heat.
Taste and adjust with a little more tamarind or fish sauce if needed, going gradually so you don't blow out the balance.
Take the pot off the heat and fish out the lemongrass stalk and lime leaves before serving.
Ladle the stew into bowls hot, with fresh cilantro on top if you like.

oil sears the chicken then remains in the pot as the stew base, not drained off
4 cups converted to grams

1 cup estimated at 150g

2 leaves estimated at ~1g each; removed and discarded before serving per instructions

title: Macaroni Pudding
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the macaroni until tender, about 25-30 minutes.
While the macaroni cooks, beat the eggs and sugar together, then stir in the vanilla and nutmeg.
In a separate saucepan, heat the evaporated milk, the extra cup of water, and the butter until almost boiling, then take it off the heat.
Whisk the hot milk mixture into the egg and sugar mixture.
Drain the cooked macaroni and fold it into the milk mixture along with the raisins.
Pour the mixture into a buttered baking dish and dust the top with a little more nutmeg.
Bake at 350°F (175°C) for about an hour, until golden brown and just firm enough to slice but still moist.

title: Conch Fritters
instructions: Make the conch mixture a day ahead if you can, letting it rest covered in the fridge so the flavours meld.
Whip up the tartar sauce ahead too if you like — just mix all its ingredients together, cover and chill.
Grind the conch, green pepper, red pepper, onions and chillies together with a food grinder.
Stir in the lime juice, minced garlic, black pepper, salt, basil and red pepper flakes until thoroughly combined.
Beat in the egg and mix again.
Add the flour a tablespoon at a time, mixing until it just comes together.
Wrap and chill the mixture for at least an hour, or overnight for the best flavour.
When ready to fry, pour a high-heat oil of your choice into a deep pan to a depth of about 1½–2 inches and heat over medium-low.
Using two spoons, shape the mixture into 1-inch balls and drop them into the hot oil one at a time.
Fry each fritter 5–7 minutes, until golden brown and cooked through inside.
Repeat until all the conch mixture is used.
Serve hot with tartar sauce and lime wedges.

ingredients.note: No weight given for the can; assumed a standard ~400g can of conch meat. No yield stated in the recipe text; servings inferred as 4 from total mixture mass (~1kg) and typical 1-inch fritter portions (~5 fritters/serving).

title: Rice and Beans
instructions: Simmer the kidney beans until they're tender.
Stir in the rice, coconut milk, diced onion, black pepper and salt.
Pour in just enough water to cover everything in the pot.
Nestle the whole Scotch Bonnet peppers on top, being careful not to burst them.
Continue simmering until the rice is tender.
Lift out the peppers before serving.

ingredients.note: "1/2 bag" - no bag size given; assumed a standard 500g dried bean bag, so 1/2 bag ≈ 250g. "1/2 tbs Salt" ≈8.5g based on standard salt density.

title: Cayman Style Potato Salad
instructions: Peel the potatoes, cut into cubes, and boil in salted water until tender.
Chop the green pepper, red pepper, and onion, and set them aside.
Whisk together the mayonnaise, mustard, sugar, and black pepper, and set aside.
Drain the cooked potatoes and place them in a large mixing bowl.
Add the remaining ingredients and mix well.
Serve warm, or refrigerate for 2 to 3 hours before serving for a chilled salad.

ingredients.note: no yield stated; servings and potato weight inferred (~150g each, 10 potatoes) — sanity-checked against total dish mass for a side salad

title: Cassava Cake
instructions: Bring the coconut milk to a boil in a saucepan.
Stir in the sugar until it fully dissolves, then keep boiling for about 45 minutes.
Set aside half a cup of this hot coconut milk and sugar mixture for basting later.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the grated cassava, nutmeg, allspice and cinnamon, stirring until well blended.
Pour the hot coconut milk mixture into the bowl and stir until you have a thick, batter-like consistency.
Grease a baking pan with a tablespoon of the coconut milk mixture.
Pour the batter into the prepared pan.
Bake at 350°F for 2 to 3 hours, basting occasionally with the reserved coconut milk mixture, until a knife inserted in the centre comes out clean.
Spread any leftover coconut milk mixture over the top of the cake.
Let the cake cool completely on top of the stove before serving.

ingredients.note: assumed standard 400 ml can
quantity given as '1/4' with no unit; assumed 1/4 tsp ground nutmeg

title: Fried Crab
instructions: Scrub the crabs clean and drop them into a large pot of boiling water; boil for 15 minutes, then lift out and let cool.
Crack the legs and open the backs, picking out all the meat and setting it aside.
Deseed and dice the scotch bonnet pepper, and dice the onions.
Sauté the onions and scotch bonnet in oil over medium heat until the onions turn transparent.
Stir in the crab meat, black pepper and salt, and cook over medium heat for another 5 to 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Serve alongside 'bread kind' — a mix of boiled starchy vegetables like pumpkin, squash, plantain, potato, cassava and breadfruit.
Optionally, pack the seasoned crab meat back into cleaned crab shells, cover with foil, and bake at 350°F for 30 minutes.

ingredients.note: 1 lb converted to grams; crab meat already boiled/cooked before being added to the sauté
2 medium onions estimated at 150g each
estimated weight for 1 whole scotch bonnet pepper

title: Cayman-Style Lobster
instructions: Rinse the lobsters under cold running water.
Bring a big pot of salted water to a rolling boil.
Plunge the lobsters in headfirst, cover, and boil for 20 minutes until they turn bright red.
Let the lobsters cool, then crack the tails and claws to pick out all the meat, removing the vein from the tail.
Chop the lobster meat into bite-size pieces.
Heat the olive oil in a large pan over low heat and sauté the red pepper, green pepper, onion, scotch bonnet, thyme, and garlic.
Stir in the salt and chicken stock.
Add the lobster meat and sauté for 10 to 15 minutes.
Stir in the cornstarch and water mixture to thicken the sauce.
Serve with your choice of vegetables, salad, or rice.

ingredients.note: no yield stated; assumed ~500g per whole lobster (6 lobsters ≈3000g raw); servings inferred as 1 lobster per person = 6 servings

title: Salt Beef and Beans
instructions: Cover the diced corned beef with water and bring it to a boil.
Drain and replace the water as needed, repeating the process to draw out some of the brine.
In a separate pot, combine the kidney beans with water, coconut milk, bay leaves, allspice, whole garlic cloves, the whole scotch bonnet pepper and scallions.
Bring slowly to a boil, then simmer gently for about 2 hours until the beans are tender.
Stir in the salt beef, onions, thyme and finely chopped garlic, then simmer for another 20 minutes.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Carefully remove the bay leaves and the scotch bonnet pepper without bursting it.
Serve with white rice and your choice of salad or vegetables.

ingredients.note: 1 1/2 cup dried kidney beans estimated at ~200g/cup

title: Conch Stew
instructions: Clean the conch meat thoroughly and pound it to tenderize.
Boil the conch for 2–3 hours until fork-tender.
While the conch simmers, prepare the Sea Pie dough.
Once the conch has cooled, cut it into bite-sized pieonces and season it.
Add the seasoned conch, onion, peppers, scallions, salt, black pepper, seasoning, coconut, and chilli to a pan and simmer for 10 to 20 minutes.
Mix the tablespoon of flour with a cup of water and stir it into the pan to thicken the stew.
Add the Sea Pie squares to the stew and continue cooking for an additional 10 minutes.
For the Sea Pie, combine the all-purpose flour, a pinch of salt, and water into a dough, then roll it out as thin as possible.
Cut the dough into 2½-inch squares and let them rest for 30 minutes.
Stretch each square thin before adding it to the conch stew mixture.
Serve with white rice, plantain, breadfruit, or your choice of vegetables.

title: Chilean-Style Sopaipillas
instructions: Peel, seed, and chop the zapallo squash into chunks.
Boil the squash chunks in water until fork-tender, about 15 to 20 minutes, then drain and let cool slightly.
Whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt in a bowl.
Stir the cooked squash with the melted butter until combined.
Fold the flour mixture into the squash mixture until a dough forms.
Turn the dough onto a floured surface and knead until soft and smooth, adding a touch more flour if it's sticky.
Cover the dough with a towel and let it rest for 15 minutes.
Roll the dough out to about 1/8-inch thickness and cut into 3-inch circles.
Poke each circle a few times with a fork so they don't puff up too much.
Heat the oil in a large deep skillet to about 385°F (195°C).
Fry the dough circles in batches until lightly browned, 3 to 4 minutes per batch.
Drain the fried sopaipillas on paper towels before serving.

ingredients.note: boiled until soft before mashing into dough per instructions
5 cups converted at ~120g/cup
ingredient list says baking powder but instructions say baking soda; kept as listed, small quantity assumed ~4g/tsp

deep-frying oil, mostly drained off after cooking; not absorbed into final dish mass

title: Chilean Empanada
instructions: Melt the butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the onion, garlic, oregano, cumin, salt and pepper, and cook, stirring, until the onion turns golden, about 5 to 7 minutes.
Add the ground pork and cook until fully browned and crumbly, 7 to 10 minutes.
Drain off the excess fat from the skillet.
Stir the raisins, black olives and hard-cooked eggs into the pork mixture.
Whisk the water with cornstarch in a small bowl, pour into the skillet, and stir until the mixture thickens; remove from heat and set the filling aside.
For the dough, whisk the melted shortening into the milk until smooth.
In a separate bowl, mix the flour and salt, then pour in the milk-shortening mixture, whisking until a dough comes together.
Let the dough rest for 10 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Roll the dough out on a floured surface to about 1/8 inch thick and cut into 12 rounds using a cookie cutter or glass.
Spoon the pork filling into the center of each round.
Fold each circle in half and crimp the edges with a fork to seal.
Brush the tops with beaten egg for a golden finish.
Arrange the empanadas on the prepared baking sheet.
Bake until golden brown, about 25 minutes.

1 tsp minced garlic ≈5g
1 tsp dried oregano ≈1g
1 tsp ground cumin ≈2g
1/2 tsp salt ≈3g
1/2 tsp black pepper ≈1g

title: Manjar (Dulce de Leche)
instructions: Pour the milk, sugar, and vanilla extract into a saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat.
Drop the heat to medium-low and keep it cooking, stirring constantly, until the milk thickens and turns a rich caramel color — this can take an hour or more, so be patient.
Stir in the baking soda and keep cooking until the caramelized milk reaches a thick, pudding-like consistency.
Pour the manjar into a bowl and let it cool completely in the refrigerator before serving.

ingredients.note: 1 quart milk converted to grams (~946 g).
1 cup sugar converted to grams (~200 g).
1/2 tsp vanilla extract converted to grams (~2.5 g).

Ingredient list says baking powder but instructions say 'Stir in baking soda' — likely means bicarbonate of soda; kept as listed ingredient (baking powder) per honesty gate, flagging the discrepancy. 1/4 tsp ≈1.2 g.

title: Chilean Dobladitas
instructions: Crank the oven up to 450°F (230°C) and grease a baking sheet.
Whisk the flour, 1 cup of melted butter, milk, baking powder, and salt together in a bowl until the dough just starts to come together.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic.
Roll the dough out to about ⅛-inch thick and cut into 8-inch circles.
Brush the circles with the remaining melted butter.
Fold each circle in half, then in half again to form a triangle, pressing firmly to seal the layers.
Arrange the triangles on the prepared baking sheet.
Bake until golden brown, about 15 minutes, and serve warm.

ingredients.note: 4 cups converted at ~125g/cup
1 cup butter converted at ~227g/cup; baked into dough, not a frying medium
1/2 cup converted at ~240g/cup
2 tsp converted at ~4.6g/tsp

title: Pastel de Papas (Chilean Potato Pie)
instructions: Boil the potatoes in salted water until fork-tender, about 20 minutes, then drain and let them steam dry for a minute or two.
While the potatoes cook, heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat and sauté the onion until translucent, about 5 minutes, adding the garlic for the last minute.
Stir in the diced tomato and tomato puree and cook until the tomatoes soften and break down, 3 to 5 minutes.
Add the ground beef to the skillet and cook, breaking it up, until browned, about 10 minutes.
Stir in the Panquehue cheese, parsley, and cayenne pepper, then season with salt and pepper; take off the heat and set aside.
Preheat the oven to 175°C (350°F) and grease a 2-quart baking dish.
Mash the drained potatoes with the butter and salt until smooth, then let cool until just warm before stirring in the beaten eggs.
Spread half the mashed potato over the bottom of the baking dish, layer the beef mixture on top, then cover completely with the remaining potato.
Bake until hot and lightly browned on top, about 40 minutes.

'to taste' estimated as a small pinch
2 large eggs estimated at 50 g each
used to sauté onion in the same pan that becomes part of the filling, so it remains in the served dish

title: Empanadas Fritas de Queso
instructions: Sift the flour, baking powder, and salt together in a big bowl.
Warm the lard and milk together in a small saucepan until the milk is hot and the lard has melted.
Pour the hot milk mixture into the flour and stir until a shaggy dough comes together.
Turn the dough out onto a floured counter and knead until smooth and elastic.
Cover the dough and let it rest for 15 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 190°C (375°F) if you plan to bake.
Roll the rested dough out on a floured surface to about 1/4-inch thickness.
Cut the dough into 4-inch rounds.
Brush the edge of each round with beaten egg, then spoon a small mound of Swiss cheese onto the center.
Fold each round in half into a half-moon and press the edges with your fingers or a fork to seal tightly.
Arrange the sealed empanadas on a parchment-lined baking sheet.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with the remaining beaten egg if baking.
Bake until golden-brown, about 20 minutes.
Alternatively, skip the egg wash on the outside and deep-fry the empanadas in 190°C (375°F) oil until golden.
Drain fried empanadas on paper towels before serving.

ingredients.note: Servings not stated in the recipe; inferred ~10 mini empanadas (~60-70g each) from dough volume of 3 cups flour plus wet ingredients rolled into 4-inch rounds — a conservative estimate to avoid an absurdly large per-serving size.
Lard is worked into the dough itself, not used as a separate frying medium, so it's counted as eaten.
Used as egg wash for sealing/brushing the empanadas, counted as raw egg amount.

title: Leche Asada
instructions: Heat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
Pour the milk into a saucepan with the lemon zest and cinnamon sticks.
Bring to a simmer over medium-high heat, then lower the heat and let it cook gently for 5 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let the milk steep for 15 minutes to soak up the flavors.
Beat the eggs and sugar together gently until the sugar dissolves.
Slowly whisk in a cup of the hot milk, a little at a time, until fully incorporated.
Stir the egg mixture back into the rest of the hot milk and mix until smooth.
Strain the custard through a fine mesh sieve into a 2-quart ceramic casserole dish to remove the zest and cinnamon.
Bake until the custard is set and browned on top, about 30 minutes.
Chill before serving.

ingredients.note: 1 quart milk converted to grams using standard density
zest of 1 lemon estimated at ~6g, no explicit weight given
2 cinnamon sticks estimated at ~2g each
4 eggs estimated at 50g each
1/2 cup sugar estimated at 100g

title: Pastel de Choclo
instructions: Boil the eggs for about 15 minutes, then let them sit off the heat in the hot water.
Meanwhile, heat oil in a skillet over medium heat and sauté the onions, garlic, a pinch of salt, half the paprika, and black pepper until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Add the beef with the remaining salt, paprika, and black pepper; cook, stirring, until mostly browned, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the chicken, raisins, and olives.
Cool the eggs under cold water, peel, roughly chop, and fold them into the beef mixture to make the pino; keep it warm.
Blend the sweetcorn and basil together until smooth.
Pour the corn purée into a saucepan over medium heat, add the butter, and cook, stirring, until thickened, 7 to 10 minutes.
Stir in the milk, sugar, and salt, and cook until it thickens to a paste, about 15 minutes more.
Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
Spread the pino into a baking dish, cover with the corn paste, and sprinkle a little sugar on top.
Bake until golden brown on top, about 20 minutes.

2 medium onions ~150g each; volume-to-weight estimate.

title: Porotos Granados (Chilean Bean Stew)
instructions: Warm the olive oil in a stock pot over medium heat.
Add the onion, cover, and let it soften and turn translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the butternut squash, great northern beans, and lima beans.
Pour in the chicken stock, cover, and simmer until the squash is tender and starting to break apart, 30 to 45 minutes.
Stir in the corn and basil, then cook uncovered until the stew thickens to the consistency of pancake batter, about 10 more minutes.
Ladle into bowls and sprinkle chopped banana pepper over each portion to serve.

title: Arroz con Leche Recipe
instructions: Simmer the cinnamon sticks in the water for about 10 minutes to infuse it, then discard the sticks and keep the cinnamon-scented water.
Tip the rice into a large saucepan with that cinnamon water and cook over medium heat for 5 minutes.
Stir in the salt, butter, vanilla extract, 2 cups of the milk, and the sugar, then cook uncovered for about 15 minutes, stirring well.
Turn the heat down to medium-low, add the rest of the milk and the condensed milk, and cook for 1 hour 15 minutes, stirring with a wooden spoon, until the pudding thickens to your liking.
Take it off the heat, stir well, and let it cool at room temperature - it should be very creamy.
Chill in the fridge for at least 1 hour, or overnight, before serving.

title: Colombian Buñuelos Recipe
instructions: Tip everything except the frying oil into a bowl and knead by hand until it forms a soft, smooth dough.
Roll the dough into small balls using your hands.
Heat the vegetable oil in a deep pot to about 300°F (roughly 150°C) — keep it warm, not scorching.
Carefully lower the dough balls into the oil and fry for 3 to 4 minutes, turning as needed, until golden brown.
Lift the buñuelos out with a slotted spoon, drain on paper towels, and serve.

ingredients.note: Amount for frying not specified in original; estimated 500g deep-frying oil, excluded from food mass since it drains off.
Pinch estimated at 1g.

title: Coconut Natilla Recipe
instructions: Pour 1 cup of the milk into a small bowl, stir in the cornstarch until fully dissolved, and set aside.
Blend the coconut milk and grated coconut together until smooth, then set aside.
Bring the remaining milk and the cinnamon sticks to a gentle boil in a medium pot over medium-low heat.
When the milk is warm but not yet boiling, stir in the blended coconut mixture.
Once the milk starts to boil, add the condensed milk, sugar and salt, stirring well with a wooden spoon.
Add the reserved milk-and-cornstarch slurry and keep stirring constantly.
Stir in the vanilla extract.
Lower the heat and continue stirring until the natilla thickens, about 10 to 20 minutes.
Add the butter, stir it through, and remove the pot from the heat.
Discard the cinnamon sticks.
Ladle the natilla into a serving dish or individual custard cups.
Sprinkle ground cinnamon on top, let it cool at room temperature for at least 2 hours, then refrigerate until ready to serve.

title: Ají de Aguacate Recipe (Colombian Spicy Avocado Sauce)
instructions: Toss all the ingredients into a food processor.
Blitz until everything is well combined and smooth.
If the mixture is too thick, add a splash more lime juice until you reach the consistency you want.

title: Colombian Style Stuffed Potatoes
instructions: Peel the potatoes, cut into chunks, and put them in a pot with water and a quarter teaspoon of salt.
Bring to a boil, then simmer over medium heat until fork-tender, about 20 minutes.
Drain the potatoes and mash them with a fork or masher, then set aside to cool.
For the filling, heat a tablespoon of olive oil in a large pan over medium heat and add the onion, tomato, scallions, garlic, cumin, Sazon Goya with Azafran, pepper and salt.
Cook for about 5 minutes until the onions turn translucent.
Add the ground beef and cook, stirring occasionally, for 10–15 minutes, then set aside to cool.
For the batter, whisk together the egg, flour, a pinch of salt, Sazon Goya with Azafran and milk in a bowl until smooth.
Divide the mashed potato into 10 equal portions, about a third of a cup each, and roll into balls.
Flatten each ball into a patty, spoon about 1½ tablespoons of the beef filling into the center, and reshape the potato around it to fully enclose the filling.
Heat vegetable oil in a large heavy pot to 360°F for deep frying.
Dip each stuffed potato ball into the batter, then carefully lower into the hot oil.
Fry for about 4 minutes, turning halfway, until golden brown.
Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
Serve hot with Ají sauce.

title: Achiote Oil (Aceite Achiotado) Recipe
instructions: Warm the vegetable oil with the achiote seeds in a small skillet over medium heat for 2 to 3 minutes, watching closely so the seeds don't scorch black.
Pull the skillet off the heat and let the oil sit for 5 minutes to deepen in color.
Strain out the seeds and pour the oil into a jar with a tight lid.
Store at room temperature for up to 15 days.

ingredients.note: 2 cups converted to grams (~218g/cup); this oil is the finished product, not a discarded frying medium

title: Chuletas de Cerdo a la Criolla (Pork Chops in Creole Sauce)
instructions: Seal the pork chops in a zip-top bag with the mustard, cumin, crushed garlic, salt and pepper.
Refrigerate for at least 1 hour, or overnight, turning the bag so the marinade coats the chops evenly.
Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Sear the chops for 3 minutes per side, then lift them out onto a warm plate.
Tip the onions and tomatoes into the same skillet, adding a splash more oil if the pan looks dry.
Cook over high heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring often, until the vegetables soften and catch a little colour.
Nestle the pork chops back into the skillet, cover, and drop the heat to medium-low.
Simmer for about 15 minutes until the pork is tender and just barely pink in the centre.
Plate the chops, spoon the sauce over the top, scatter with cilantro and serve at once.

title: Almojábanas (Colombian Cheese Bread)
instructions: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C).
Pulse the masarepa (cornmeal) and cheeses together in a food processor until combined.
Add the butter, baking powder, salt, eggs, and milk, then mix well into a soft dough.
Divide the dough into 8 equal portions and roll each into a ball.
Arrange the balls on a greased baking sheet.
Bake for 25 to 30 minutes, until golden brown.
Serve warm.

title: Creamy Mustard Chicken
instructions: Pat the chicken legs dry, then season them with salt, pepper and paprika.
Heat the olive oil in a saucepan over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Sear the chicken pieces on all sides, turning a couple of times, until golden brown, about 10 minutes.
Transfer the browned chicken to a plate and set aside.
Add the onions and garlic to the same pan and cook for about 2 minutes until softened.
Pour in the chicken broth, mustard, honey and cream, then season with salt and black pepper.
Stir well and return the chicken pieces to the pan.
Cover and cook over medium-low heat for about 45 minutes until the chicken is cooked through, reaching 170°F for the legs.
Serve warm over white rice, garnished with chopped parsley.

ingredients.note: no per-piece weight given; assumed ~120g per chicken leg for 8 legs
oil sears chicken then aromatics/sauce are built in the same pan and served with the dish, so it remains in the food

garnish quantity not specified; estimated a small garnish amount

title: Chicken in Orange Sauce Recipe (Pollo a la Naranja)
instructions: Warm the olive oil and butter together in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Tip in the chopped onion, red pepper and garlic, and cook for about 3 minutes, stirring often, until softened.
Add the chicken legs, season with salt and pepper, and sear for about a minute on each side.
Pour in the chicken broth and orange juice, then cook for 2 minutes, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
Stir in the brown sugar, cover the pot, reduce the heat to medium and let it simmer for about 45 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through.
Spoon the sauce over the chicken and serve over white rice, garnished with fresh cilantro.

title: Creamy Corn Soup Recipe
instructions: Toss the corn, water, stock, garlic, salt, pepper, cilantro, potatoes and scallions into a large pot and bring it to a boil.
Turn the heat down to medium-low and let it simmer for about 4 minutes, just until the corn turns tender.
Pull the pot off the heat and blitz the soup smooth in a blender.
Pour the puréed soup back into the pot, stir in the cream, and warm it over medium heat for 15 minutes, stirring now and then.
Ladle into bowls and finish with a scatter of cilantro and potato sticks.

title: Breaded Steak Recipe (Lomo de Res Apanado)
instructions: Sandwich each steak between two sheets of plastic wrap and pound with a mallet until about ¼ to ½ inch thick.
Place the pounded steaks in a glass dish, pour over the alinos sauce, and let marinate in the fridge for 3 hours or overnight.
Mix the flour, breadcrumbs, cumin, salt, pepper, and parsley together on a plate.
Coat each steak in the flour mixture, dip in beaten eggs, then coat again in the flour mixture.
Either fry the breaded steaks right away or lay them on parchment paper until ready to cook.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat until it begins to smoke lightly.
Add the breaded steaks and fry for about 3 to 4 minutes per side until golden brown.
Transfer to a paper-towel-lined plate to drain off excess oil before serving.

title: Papas Chorreadas Recipe (Potatoes with Cream and Cheese Sauce)
instructions: Boil the potatoes with the chicken bouillon cube in enough water to cover, about 30 minutes until fork-tender, then drain and set aside.
While the potatoes cook, warm the oil and butter together in a frying pan over medium heat.
Add the onion (scallions) and tomato, stirring and cooking about 7 minutes until softened.
Stir in the garlic powder, cumin, salt and pepper, and cook 2 minutes more.
Pour in the cream, add the cilantro and cheese, mix well, lower the heat and cook 3 minutes until the cheese melts into a smooth sauce.
Arrange the drained potatoes in a serving dish and pour the warm cheese sauce over the top.
Serve immediately while hot.

title: Tamal De Maicena
instructions: Preheat your oven to 350°F.
In a non-stick medium pot, combine the milk, butter, sugar and vanilla, bring to a boil, then drop the heat to low.
In a large bowl, whisk the eggs with the cornstarch until smooth.
Temper the eggs by slowly whisking in about a cup of the hot milk mixture, then keep adding milk gradually until the mixture is smooth and lump-free.
Pour this mixture back into the pot slowly, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon.
Keep cooking and stirring for a few more minutes until it thickens — you'll know it's ready when a spoon dragged across the bottom leaves a clean line.
Remove from the heat and pour into an ungreased 11x8-inch pyrex dish.
Cover with foil and bake for about 50 minutes, removing the foil after 30 minutes so the top browns lightly.
Let it cool completely before serving.

title: Black Beans Hotpot
instructions: Toss the black beans, onion, green pepper, red pepper, celery, crushed garlic, cumin, oregano, salt, black pepper and water into the Instant Pot.
Lock the lid on and cook at High Pressure for 35 minutes.
Once the beans are done, let the pressure release naturally for 20 minutes, then release any remaining pressure and open the lid.
Serve hot.
Let any leftovers cool completely before storing in the fridge or freezer.

title: Pan De Yucca
instructions: Preheat your oven to 175°C (350°F).
Grease and flour a 12x8-inch baking dish (a smaller dish works too if you want a thicker bread).
Peel and shred the yucca, then set it aside.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt; set aside.
In a large bowl, cream the butter with the sugar until fluffy.
Beat in the eggs one at a time, then stir in the vanilla.
Mix in half of the dry ingredients, then add the cream cheese and mix well.
Add the remaining dry ingredients and mix until combined.
Fold in the shredded yucca with a wooden spoon until fully incorporated.
Pour or spoon the batter into the prepared pan and bake for 45 to 55 minutes.

title: Mango Tropical Salsa
instructions: Toss the red onions, red pepper, tomato, mango, avocado, cilantro, lime juice, salt, and black pepper together in a large bowl until well combined.
Cover the bowl and let the salsa chill in the fridge for at least 15 minutes before serving.
Serve alongside chips.
Make this only 15-20 minutes ahead if you can — chop everything in advance and toss it together right before your guests arrive so it doesn't turn soupy.

title: Picadillo de Vainicas (Green Beans Picadillo)
instructions: Warm a splash of olive oil in a medium pot over medium heat.
Add the onion, pepper, and garlic, and cook until the onion turns translucent.
While that cooks, finely chop the green beans.
Stir the shredded meat, chopped green beans, thyme, salt, and black pepper into the pot, mixing well.
Pour in the water, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes.
Uncover and cook for a further 5 minutes, or until the green beans are soft.

title: Pollo en Salsa
instructions: Chop the pepper, onion and tomato and set them aside.
Turn on the Instant Pot to the sauté setting for 8 minutes.
Add a little olive oil, then the onion, garlic, tomato, bell pepper, cumin, oregano, salt and pepper; sauté for 4 minutes.
Add the chicken and cook for 2 minutes on each side, stirring often.
Pour in the chicken stock, cover, and set the pressure cooker to high for 20 minutes.
When done, release the pressure and add a little more salt if needed.
Serve with some of the juice spooned over rice and beans.

title: Gallo pinto
instructions: Heat olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Add the onion, peppers, cilantro, garlic, cumin, salt and pepper, and cook for 3-5 minutes until the onion turns translucent.
Stir in the black beans, lower the heat to medium, add the Salsa Lizano, and cook for 5 more minutes.
Add the rice and stir well to soak up all the flavors, cooking a few minutes more.
Keep cooking, 5-7 minutes total for a balance between wet and toasted, longer if you like it drier, shorter if you like it wetter.

title: Chorreadas

Pour about 1/4 cup of batter onto the pan for each pancake.
Cook until lightly browned on each side, flipping like you would a pancake.
Serve warm, traditionally topped with a spoon of sour cream, or plain if you prefer.

title: Dulce de Leche Cheesecake with a Maria Cookie Crust.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 325ºF (163ºC).
Grind the Maria cookies in a food processor for about 30 seconds until finely crumbed (or crush by hand in a zip-top bag with a heavy skillet).
Stir the softened butter into the cookie crumbs until evenly combined.
Press the cookie mixture firmly into the bottom and sides of a 9-inch springform pan.
Bake the crust for about 10 minutes until firm and fragrant, then let it cool.
Beat the cream cheese with an electric mixer on medium speed for about 3 minutes until fluffy.
Add the condensed milk and beat until smooth, about 1 minute.
Beat in the eggs, vanilla extract, and 3 tablespoons of dulce de leche until fully combined, about 3 minutes.
Pour the filling into the cooled crust.
Bake for 45 to 55 minutes, until golden and set but still slightly wobbly in the center.
Turn off the oven, crack the door open, and let the cheesecake sit inside until it reaches room temperature, about 1 hour.
Transfer the cheesecake to the refrigerator and chill completely, at least 4 hours.
Release the cooled cheesecake from the springform pan.
Spoon the remaining dulce de leche into a zip-top bag and snip a small hole in one corner.
Pipe stripes of dulce de leche over the top of the cheesecake to finish.

title: CAFE LA LLAVE ESPRESSO ICE CREAM
instructions: Warm the cream, milk, sugar and salt together in a medium saucepan over medium heat, whisking until the sugar dissolves, about 4 to 5 minutes.
Whisk the egg yolks in a separate bowl.
Slowly pour about a cup of the hot cream mixture into the egg yolks while whisking constantly, to temper them gently without scrambling.
Pour the tempered yolk mixture back into the saucepan, whisking until it just comes to a simmer.
Take the pan off the heat and stir in the vanilla extract and espresso.
Chill the base, then churn it in an ice cream maker of your choice.
Use two to four shots of espresso to taste — about three shots gives the best balance.
Serve and enjoy your espresso ice cream.

title: Ropa Veija
instructions: Pat the flank steak dry and cut it into four rectangular pieces, then season all over with salt.
Sear the steak in batches in hot oil in a large pot until browned on both sides, about 5-6 minutes per batch, then set aside on a plate.
In the same pot, cook the peppers, onion and salt in the remaining oil until softened, about 4-5 minutes.
Stir in the garlic, tomato puree, paprika, cumin and oregano and cook until fragrant, 1-2 minutes.
Pour in the wine, scraping up the browned bits, and simmer until reduced by half, about 2 minutes.
Add the canned tomatoes with their juice and the bay leaves, mashing the tomatoes down, then nestle the seared steak into the stew, topping up with water to cover if needed.
Bring to a simmer, cover, reduce heat to low and cook until the steak shreds easily, about 2.5-3 hours.
Remove the steak, shred it with two forks, then stir it back into the stew.
Simmer uncovered until the sauce thickens and coats the meat, 5-10 minutes, then stir in the olives and adjust salt.
Serve the ropa vieja spooned over rice and black beans, finished with a scatter of cilantro.

title: Black Bean soup
instructions: Warm the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until soft and translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the jalapeño and garlic and cook until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Add the tomato purée, stir to coat the vegetables, and cook for another minute.
Season with salt, pepper, cumin, and chilli powder, stirring to coat everything evenly.
Add the black beans and chicken stock, stir well, drop in the bay leaf, and bring to a boil.
Immediately reduce to a simmer and let it cook until slightly reduced and thickened, 15 to 20 minutes, adding more stock if you want a thinner soup.
Remove the bay leaf and let the soup cool slightly.
Blend with an immersion blender or food processor to your desired consistency.
Serve with a dollop of sour cream, sliced avocado, and fresh cilantro.

title: Cuban Sandwich
instructions: Poke holes all over the pork chops with a fork and season with the salt.
Drizzle the pork with oil and toss to coat.
Whisk together the orange zest and juice, lime zest and juice, oregano, pepper, garlic powder, and cumin/onion powder for the marinade.
Pour half the marinade over the pork and let it rest for 10 to 15 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 425°F while the pork marinates.
Stir the mustard and mayonnaise into the remaining marinade until smooth.
Roast the pork until an instant-read thermometer reads 140°F, about 15 to 20 minutes.
Cut the loaf in half crosswise, then split each half lengthwise for 4 pieces of bread.
Butter the outside of each bread piece and spread the mustard mixture on the cut sides.
Layer two of the bread pieces with cheese, ham, the sliced roast pork, pickles, and more cheese.
Close the sandwiches with the remaining bread and wrap each tightly in foil.
Heat a cast-iron skillet over medium heat and place the wrapped sandwiches in it, weighing them down with another heavy skillet.
Grill the sandwiches, flipping halfway, until toasted and the cheese melts, about 3 to 4 minutes per side.
Cut each sandwich in half and serve.

ingredients.note: inferred: 'pinch' assumed ~1g
line covers both juice and zest of one orange; quantity inferred (~60g) as zest isn't separately measured

title: Baked Yuca Fries
instructions: Preheat your oven to 450°F (230°C) and bring a large pot of water to a boil.
Trim the ends off the yuca, halve each root crosswise, stand the pieces on end and peel away the waxy skin with a sharp knife.
Cut each peeled piece lengthwise in half, then in half again to get 16 sticks total, and trim out the fibrous center core.
Boil the yuca sticks until fork-tender, 8 to 10 minutes, then lift out with a slotted spoon onto paper towels and pat dry.
Toss the dried yuca in a large bowl with the olive oil, then season with chili powder, salt and pepper, tossing again to coat evenly.
Spread the yuca in a single layer on a large baking sheet and bake until golden and crispy, about 25 minutes, tossing halfway through.
While the fries bake, blend the avocado, sour cream, lime juice and cilantro in a small food processor until smooth, then season with salt and pepper.
Serve the yuca fries hot alongside the avocado dipping sauce.

title: Chicken Salad With Mushrooms and Bacon
instructions: Pop the chicken breast in a saucepan and cover with water.
Bring to a boil, stir in 1 tsp salt and simmer for about 15 minutes until cooked through.
Let the chicken cool, then shred it into bite-sized pieces with two forks.
Finely chop the shallot.
In a bowl, stir together the mayonnaise, crème fraîche, Dijon mustard and apple cider vinegar until smooth.
Season the dressing with salt, pepper and a touch more Dijon mustard if you like.
Fold the shredded chicken and chopped shallot into the dressing.
Fry the bacon until crisp, then lift onto paper towels to drain.
Rinse and slice the mushrooms, then fry them in the same pan as the bacon until golden, about 5 minutes.
Season the mushrooms with a little salt and pepper.
Spoon the chicken salad into a serving dish and top with the crisp bacon, fried mushrooms and chopped parsley.
Serve alongside a slice of light or dark bread.

title: Strawberry tart
instructions: Preheat the oven to 175°C (347°F).
Grate the marzipan and mix thoroughly with the sugar.
Whip the marzipan-sugar mixture together with the butter until fluffy.
Beat in the eggs, then fold in the flour to form the dough.
Line a 24 cm springform pan with baking paper and grease with butter.
Roll out the dough and press it into the prepared pan.
Bake in the middle of the oven for about 25 minutes.
While the shell bakes, prepare the vanilla custard.
Finely chop the dark chocolate and sprinkle it over the hot baked shell, spreading it with a spatula as it melts.
Let the shell cool to room temperature.
For the custard, whisk the vanilla sugar, egg yolks, sugar and cornstarch together in a saucepan.
Set the pan over medium heat and whisk in the milk.
Cook, stirring constantly, until the custard thickens.
Transfer the custard to a container and let it cool completely.
Whip the double cream to soft peaks and fold it into the cooled custard.
Rinse the strawberries, remove the stems, and quarter them from top to bottom.
Spread a thick layer of the vanilla custard over the cooled tart shell.
Top with the fresh strawberries and a scatter of fresh mint if using.
Serve and enjoy.

title: Old-fashioned Danish Apple Trifle
instructions: Peel and core the apples, then cut them into cubes.
Put the apple cubes in a saucepan with water, vanilla and lemon juice.
Bring to a boil, then cover and reduce the heat to medium-low.
Stir in sugar to taste once the apples release their juices — go easy, since the breadcrumb mix and whipped cream are already sweet.
Simmer for about 15 minutes until you have a coarse applesauce, or longer if you want it smoother.
Chill the applesauce in the fridge.
Melt the butter in a pan, keeping it from browning.
Add the breadcrumbs and sugar and stir to combine.
Toast briefly over low heat, stirring constantly, until lightly golden — it browns fast so don't walk away.
Tip the toasted breadcrumbs onto baking paper to cool.
Right before serving, lightly whip the double cream.
Layer breadcrumbs, chilled applesauce, then more breadcrumbs in glasses or a bowl, repeating as you like.
Top with a generous layer of whipped cream and serve immediately so the breadcrumbs stay crisp.

title: Hveder - Danish Wheat Buns
instructions: Warm the milk in a saucepan until it feels just warm to the touch, about 25°C.
Pour the warm milk into a mixing bowl and stir in the yeast until dissolved.
Add the eggs, sugar, salt, cardamom and flour, then knead everything together thoroughly (a stand mixer makes this easier).
Cut the butter into cubes, work it into the dough, and knead for 5 to 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Cover the dough and let it rise somewhere warm for 45 minutes.
Turn the risen dough out onto a floured counter and divide it into 10-12 equal pieces.
Roll each piece into a round bun and arrange them close together on a lined roasting pan or oven-proof dish, about 20 x 30 cm.
Cover the buns and let them rise for about an hour, until doubled in size.
Heat the oven to 180°C on the fan setting.
Beat the egg and brush it over the tops of the buns.
Bake on the middle rack for 14 to 16 minutes until golden, then serve warm or toasted with butter.

title: Frikadeller - Danish Meatballs
instructions: Finely chop the onion and garlic.
Stir the salt into the minced pork, then work in the flour, oats and pepper, followed by the chopped onion and garlic, then the egg, and finally the milk; mix until the meat mixture turns firm.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes (or make it a day ahead and chill overnight).
Heat a pan over medium heat and melt the butter, then spoon out portions of the mixture (about 2 tablespoons each) and shape into frikadeller in the pan.
Fry the frikadeller until they form a nice browned crust on both sides and are cooked through.
Serve hot alongside a classic potato salad.

title: Open-faced Sandwich With Potato
instructions: Scrub the potatoes and set them in a small saucepan with water and a tablespoon of salt.
Bring to a boil, then simmer for about 10 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let the potatoes sit in the hot water for 5 minutes.
Drain and let cool slightly; they're done when a fork slides out easily.
Fry the bacon slices until crisp, then drain on paper towels.
Peel the red onions and slice into thin rings.
Toss the onion rings in flour, then shake off the excess.
Heat the oil in a saucepan over medium heat until a piece of onion sizzles and bubbles when dropped in.
Fry the floured onion rings in batches, not overcrowding the pan, until golden brown.
Drain the fried onions on paper towels and sprinkle with salt; repeat until all onions are fried.
Butter a slice of rye bread and lay potato slices (or halves, if small) on top.
Finish each open sandwich with a spoon of mayonnaise, the crispy onions, fried bacon, and sliced chives.

title: Classic Potato Salad
instructions: Boil the new potatoes in lightly salted water for 10 to 12 minutes, until a fork slides in and the potato slips back into the pot easily.
Drain the potatoes and let them stand and cool.
Snap off and discard the stringy ends of the asparagus, then chop the spears into small pieces.
If you want less raw a bite, pour boiling water over the chopped asparagus and let it soak for 2 minutes, then drain.
Finely chop the chives, red onion and radish.
Stir together the creme fraiche, mayonnaise, dijon mustard, sugar and apple cider vinegar, then season with salt and pepper to make the dressing.
Set aside some of the chopped chives for garnish.
Combine the cooled potatoes, asparagus, red onion and radish with the dressing.
Season again with salt and pepper, scatter the reserved chives on top, and serve as a side dish.
If fresh peas are in season, stir some into the salad for extra sweetness.

title: Danish Rye Bread
instructions: Boil the rye grain in the water, then cover and simmer for 2-3 minutes.
Tip the boiled grain into a mixing bowl and stir in the beer, buttermilk and salt.
Dissolve the fresh yeast into that mixture, then stir in the seeds.
Work in the all-purpose flour and rye flour, kneading for about 10 minutes by hand or with a mixer.
Scrape the dough into an oiled 2.5 l loaf pan and smooth the top with a spatula.
Poke the surface all over with a fork, cover with plastic wrap, and let it rise 1½ hours at room temperature (or up to 24 hours in the fridge).
Preheat the oven to 200°C (175°C fan) and bake on the bottom rack for about 75 minutes, until the centre is dry or reads 98°C on a thermometer.
Let the loaf cool completely before slicing.
If the dough rose in the fridge, bring it back to room temperature for 1 hour before baking.

title: Extra crispy chicken wings
instructions: Rinse the chicken wings and pat them completely dry.
Place them in a large bowl.
Season with garlic powder, salt, black pepper and turmeric (add more spices if you like).
Let the wings marinate for at least an hour.
Spread the cassava flour on a plate.
Coat each wing evenly in the flour, then set on a rack or platter to dry for 30 minutes to an hour.
Heat the oil in a saucepan until hot.
Fry the wings on medium heat until light brown.
Drain on paper towels and let the wings rest for about 10 minutes.
Return the wings to the hot oil and fry on high heat until golden and extra crispy.
Serve and enjoy.

title: Stuffed plantain cups
instructions: Peel and wash the plantains, then pat them completely dry.
Slice the plantains into 1½-inch chunks.
Heat oil in a deep pan until shimmering hot.
Drop the plantain chunks into the hot oil, making sure they're fully submerged.
Fry for about 7 minutes until softened.
Lift out onto paper towel and let rest about 5 minutes — don't let them dry out too much.
Press each warm plantain slice into a cup shape using a citrus squeezer.
Set the formed cups on a paper towel or tray.
Return the cups to the deep fryer or a pan with enough oil to submerge them.
Fry until golden brown, then remove.
Fill the crispy cups with your favorite filling and serve.

title: Baked brie with sorrel jam
instructions: Combine the sugar, dried sorrel, orange zest and water in a medium pot and bring to a boil, cooking until the sorrel petals soften.
Reduce the heat and simmer, stirring, until the mixture thickens into a jam.
Let the jam cool and rest before using it on the brie.
Slice the brie in half like a sandwich, or leave the wheel whole and trim the top rind.
Spread a generous layer of sorrel jam over the cheese or between the sliced halves.
Roll the puff pastry out on a floured surface.
Trim the excess pastry, saving scraps for decoration.
Wrap the pastry completely around the cheese, pressing the edges together to seal tightly.
Flip the wrapped cheese so the seam sits on the bottom, then place it on a baking sheet lined with non-stick spray.
If making ahead, refrigerate the wrapped brie for up to two days, then let it come to room temperature before baking.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Whisk the egg with the water and brush it over the top and sides of the pastry.
Add any decorative pastry scraps and brush again with the egg wash.
Bake until the pastry is puffed and golden, then serve warm with extra sorrel jam.

title: Mini butter buns
instructions: Whisk together the bread flour, sugar, yeast, and salt in a large bowl.
Pour in the warm milk and knead until a rough dough forms.
Once the dough turns smooth and elastic, work in the softened butter and keep kneading until fully incorporated.
Cover the bowl and let the dough rise until it has doubled in size, about 90 minutes.
Turn the risen dough out onto a floured surface, divide it into small portions, and roll each into a smooth ball.
Grease your baking pans lightly and arrange the dough balls on them.
Cover the pans and let the buns rise again until they look puffy and soft.
Brush the tops of the risen buns with egg white using a pastry brush.
Near the end of the rise, preheat the oven to 375°F.
Uncover the buns and bake for 28 to 32 minutes, until the crusts turn golden brown.
Pull them from the oven and enjoy warm.

title: Codfish deviled eggs
instructions: Boil the eggs for 15 minutes, then let them rest in the hot water another 5 minutes.
Run the eggs under cold water and let them cool fully.
Peel the cooled eggs.
Halve each egg and scoop the yolks into a bowl.
Mash the yolks smooth.
Mix in the mayonnaise, mustard, pepper, salt and paprika, leaving out the chives and crushed peppercorn.
Load the yolk mixture into a piping bag or a sealed plastic bag with a corner snipped off.
Pipe the filling into the egg white halves.
Lay a piece of codfish on the wider side of each filled egg.
Finish with a scatter of crushed peppercorn and chopped chives.

title: Breadfruit Tacos
instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil and add the whole breadfruit.
Let it simmer for about 30 minutes until tender.
Lift the breadfruit out and let it cool for a few minutes so it's safe to handle.
Slice the breadfruit into thin rounds with a sharp knife, cutting as many as you need.
Trim away the skin and core from each round (leave the core in if you prefer).
Gently fold each round into a taco shell shape and pin it with two toothpicks.
Lay the folded shells on a tray and repeat until all the breadfruit is shaped.
Heat oil for deep frying, then use tongs to lower the shells into the hot oil, guiding them as needed.
Fry until golden brown, carefully removing toothpicks either before or after cooking as you feel comfortable.
Lift the fried shells out and drain them on tissue paper.
Repeat with the remaining shells.
Fill each crispy shell with your favourite filling — salt cod and lettuce work beautifully.

title: Dominica mastiff bread
instructions: Whisk the flour, brown sugar, salt and instant yeast together in a large bowl.
Rub in the shortening or lard with your hands until well combined.
Pour in the warm water and mix until you have a stiff dough.
Work in a little extra flour if the dough feels sticky.
Turn the dough onto a floured board and knead for 8-10 minutes until smooth.
Shape into a large round, cover and let it rest for 45 minutes.
Knead the dough again briefly on a lightly floured surface.
Shape into your desired bread designs and place on a baking sheet or in a bread pan.
Let the shaped breads rest for 25 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Bake until golden brown.

title: Cassava bread
instructions: Stir the farine, cassava flour and salt together in a bowl.
Pour in the water and mix until you get a doughy consistency.
Pinch off pieces and roll them into small balls.
Flatten each ball between your palms into a small disk.
Coat the disks generously with the remaining farine on a plate for a coarse crust.
Heat a heavy cast-iron griddle and press the disks down, roasting on both sides until lightly browned.
Take off the griddle and serve, plain or with your favorite accompaniment.

title: Chicken Lollipop
instructions: Rinse the chicken wings well with lemon to freshen them up.
Drain thoroughly and season the wings.
Take kitchen shears and cut through the meat at the tip of each drumette to shape the lollipop.
Rub all the seasoning ingredients into the drumettes.
Heat oil in a deep fryer or a deep saucepan to about 5 inches so the wings are fully submerged.
Fry until medium brown if you plan to finish them in the oven with sauce, or fry until golden brown if serving straight from the fryer.
Brush your favourite sauce (honey garlic barbecue works well) over the fried wings using a silicone brush, then transfer to the oven to finish.

title: plantain rolls
instructions: Peel the plantains and slice them crosswise into rounds about ¼ inch thick.
Heat vegetable oil in a frying pan over medium heat.
Fry the plantain slices until golden on the underside, then flip and brown the other side.
Lift the fried plantain slices out onto a plate lined with paper towel to drain off excess oil.
Lay a piece of codfish bulljaw at one end of each plantain slice and roll it up.
Secure each roll with a toothpick or skewer.
Garnish and serve.

title: Dominican Bakes
instructions: Whisk together the flour, salt, and baking powder in a large bowl.
Stir in the lightly melted butter until evenly combined.
Gradually pour in the lukewarm milk, mixing as you go.
Knead the dough gently by hand until it comes together.
Aim for a soft, slightly sticky dough rather than a firm one.
Divide the dough into small portions and roll each into a ball.
Cover the balls with a towel or plastic wrap and let them rest for 15–20 minutes.
Flatten each ball with your hands, pressing a thumb into the center to shape it.
Heat oil in a saucepan over medium heat and deep-fry the bakes (a saucepan uses less oil than a wide frying pan).
Flip each bake when the underside turns golden brown, then cook the other side until browned.
Serve warm with your favorite side.

title: Apple pie pops
instructions: Gather your lollipop sticks before you start.
Pulse the flour, sugar, and salt together in a food processor.
Add the butter and shortening, pulsing until the mixture looks like small pebbles.
Drizzle in three-quarters of the ice water, pulsing until the dough clumps into a ball; add a splash more water if needed, but don't overmix.
Turn the dough onto the counter, flatten it into a disc, wrap in plastic, and chill for at least an hour or overnight.
Melt butter in a pan over medium heat until it turns golden brown.
Stir in the brown sugar, cinnamon, nutmeg, salt, and lemon juice.
Add the diced apples and cook for 5-7 minutes over medium heat.
Remove from the heat and let the apple filling cool, popping it in the fridge to speed things along if you like.
Whisk the egg with the water to make an egg wash.
Roll the chilled dough out to about 1/8 inch thick.
Cut out 2½-3 inch rounds, rerolling the scraps as needed, and lay them on parchment.
Cut decorative vents into half of the rounds.
Press a lollipop stick halfway into each of the remaining rounds.
Spoon a tablespoon of the cooled apple filling onto the center of each stick-topped round.
Top with a vented round and pinch the edges to seal.
Brush each pop with egg wash and sprinkle with sugar.
Bake for 15-20 minutes, until golden brown.

title: Creamy mango smoothie
instructions: Peel the mangoes and slice them.
Cut two of the mangoes into chunks for blending.
Blend the mango chunks with the vanilla extract, condensed milk and ice cubes until smooth and creamy.
Pour the smoothie into a glass.
For the garnish, cut two of the largest opposite slices from the remaining mango.
Lay the slices flat on a cutting board.
Using a sharp knife, cut small ¼-inch slices, angling slightly diagonal for longer pieces.
Roll one slice into a tight bud, then wrap the remaining slices around it one by one to build up the shape of a rose.
Keep adding slices until the rose reaches your desired size, then set it on top of the smoothie.

title: Layered mango custard
instructions: Sprinkle the gelatin over the water and let it sit for 7–10 minutes to bloom.
Warm the cream, milk and sugar together gently, stirring until the sugar fully dissolves — don't let it boil.
Pull the pan off the heat and let the mixture rest for about 5 minutes.
Stir in the bloomed gelatin mixture, then add the vanilla extract.
Pour the custard into small glasses, tilting them at an angle for a layered look.
Chill the glasses in the refrigerator for at least 4 hours, or overnight — never freeze them.
Blend the mango chunks into a smooth purée.
Strain the purée for a silkier texture.
Sweeten the purée with sugar to taste if you like.
Warm the sweetened purée on the stove, stirring until any added sugar dissolves, then remove from heat.
Stir in the second batch of bloomed gelatin mixture.
Spoon the mango purée over the chilled custard layer.
Return the glasses to the fridge and chill for at least 2 hours more.
Garnish with mint leaves and small mango pieces before serving.

ingredients.note: Original ingredient listed as 'Custard' but instructions describe blooming it with water then dissolving it (a gelatin process); mapped to unflavoured gelatin. Amount inferred from typical 1½ tbsp gelatin usage.

title: Dominica’s coconut rough cake
instructions: Whisk together all the dry ingredients in a large bowl.
Stir in the coconut milk until you have a thick, well-combined batter.
Grease your baking pan or sheet with vegetable oil.
Scoop the mixture into rounds using an ice cream scoop, placing them on the prepared sheet, or press the mixture gently into a cake mould or baking pan.
Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C).
Bake for 25 to 35 minutes, until golden brown, then let cool before serving.

ingredients.note: 4 cups wheat flour converted at ~120 g/cup
1 cup oats converted at ~90 g/cup
1 1/2 cups brown sugar converted at ~220 g/cup
2 cups shredded coconut estimated at ~85 g/cup

1 1/2 tsp nutmeg ~2 g/tsp

1/4 cup raisins converted at ~160 g/cup

title: Fried cocoy banana
instructions: Peel the cocoy bananas.
Whisk the eggs with the cinnamon in a bowl.
Dip each banana in the egg mixture, then roll it in the panko breadcrumbs to coat.
Heat the oil in a pan over medium heat.
Lower the coated bananas into the hot oil.
Turn them with tongs as they fry.
Lift them out once golden brown and drain.

title: Papaya salad
instructions: Peel and shred the green papaya, using a julienne shredder or a coarse grater if you don't have one.
Peel and shred the carrot the same way.
Finely chop the spring onions.
Slice the cherry tomatoes in half.
Toss the papaya, carrot, spring onions, tomatoes, lemon juice, garlic and salt together in a bowl.
Finish with the roasted peanuts scattered on top.

title: Quark and Rhubarb Pie
instructions: Bring all the ingredients to room temperature before you start.
Warm the milk to about 37°C and stir in the yeast and sugar until dissolved.
Mix in about half of the flour along with the salt and beat briefly.
Dust the dough with a little flour, cover the bowl with a towel, and let it rise in a warm spot for about 1 hour.
Work in the remaining flour and the cardamom.
Stir in the melted butter, then beat an egg — save about a teaspoon for glazing — and mix the rest into the dough.
Knead the dough thoroughly until it pulls cleanly away from the bowl.
Dust again with flour, cover, and let rise a second time for about 1 hour, until doubled in size.
Roll the dough into a square, spoon the quark into the centre, fold the edges over it, and gently roll out into a rectangle.
Scatter the rhubarb, sugar and cinnamon over the rectangle, leaving about 2 cm bare along one edge.
Roll up the dough and slice into 6-7 equal rolls, tucking the bare edge under each roll as the base.
Arrange the rolls flower-style in a buttered springform pan — one in the centre, the rest around it — leaving room for further rising.
Brush the tops with the reserved beaten egg.
Bake at 180°C for 20-30 minutes.
Dust with icing sugar before serving.

ingredients.note: 1 tablespoon sugar estimated as ~12 g
1 egg estimated at ~50 g, split between dough and glaze per instructions
1/2 tsp salt estimated as ~3 g
1/2 tsp cardamom estimated as ~1 g
300 ml flour converted to grams using ~0.5 g/ml density
'Sweet Quark' has no exact match in the ingredient table; approximated as fromage frais, the closest fresh soft cheese
1 cup chopped rhubarb estimated as ~122 g
pinch of sugar estimated as ~0.5 g
pinch of cinnamon estimated as ~0.5 g

title: Apple Pie
instructions: Peel and slice the apples.
Beat the eggs and sugar together until light and foamy.
Whisk together the flour, salt, and bicarbonate of soda.
Fold the dry ingredients into the egg mixture, adding the sour milk gradually while stirring, then fold in the apples.
Pour the batter into a buttered pan.
Sprinkle sugar (and cinnamon, if using) over the top.
Bake at 180°C for 30 minutes.

title: Carrot and Bean Fritters
instructions: Drain the canned beans well in a sieve, shaking off all the liquid.
Grate the carrots and combine them with the drained beans in a bowl, mashing everything together until it holds.
Season the mash with garlic, thyme, salt and pepper, mixing well.
Shape the mixture into round patties, dip each lightly in the breadcrumbs, and press flat into fritters.
Fry the fritters briefly in hot oil until browned on both sides.
Transfer the fritters to a greased or baking-paper-lined tray and finish in a 200C oven for 10 minutes.

title: Sauerkraut and Fish Salad
instructions: Boil the eggs until firm, then toast the rye bread slices.
Whisk together a simple dressing (swap in sour cream if you like a different take).
Toss the sauerkraut with sliced boiled egg, smoked haddock, and pieces of toasted bread.
Serve right away.

title: Gingerbread Waffles
instructions: Whisk the egg and sugar together until they form a light, airy foam.
Sift the flour, salt and gingerbread spice together in a separate bowl.
Gradually fold the dry ingredients into the egg mixture, stirring as you go.
Pour in the milk and beer, stirring to combine into a smooth batter.
Stir in the melted butter.
Let the batter rest, then cook in a waffle iron until golden.

title: Honey Cookies
instructions: Dissolve the honey into the water until fully combined.
Stir in the rye flour and let the dough rest for a few minutes.
Knead the dough briefly, then use a teaspoon to shape it into small round buns, placing them on a greased or parchment-lined baking sheet.
Bake at 200°C for 10-15 minutes until set.
